Remove deprecated VPC link.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway delete-vpc-link \
    --vpc-link-id vpc123old
Output: (No output on success)
Django Context: # Remove old VPC link # JobPost.objects new network # Backend migration complete
Example 2: Delete staging link

Remove staging VPC link.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway delete-vpc-link \
    --vpc-link-id vpcstaging

echo "Staging VPC link removed"
Output: Staging VPC link removed
Django Context: # Remove staging link # User.objects prod link only # Environment cleanup
Example 3: Delete test link

Remove VPC link used for testing.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway delete-vpc-link \
    --vpc-link-id vpctest456
Output: (No output on success)
Django Context: # Remove test VPC link # Skills.objects testing done # Clean test resources
Example 4: Delete failed link

Remove VPC link that failed to provision.

#!/bin/bash
VPC_LINK_ID="vpcfailed"

# Check status
STATUS=$(aws apigateway get-vpc-link --vpc-link-id $VPC_LINK_ID \
    --query 'status' --output text)

if [ "$STATUS" = "FAILED" ]; then
    aws apigateway delete-vpc-link --vpc-link-id $VPC_LINK_ID
    echo "Failed VPC link removed"
fi
Output: Failed VPC link removed
Django Context: # Remove failed link # Author.objects retry setup # Clean failed resources
Example 5: Delete unused links

Remove VPC links not in use.

#!/bin/bash
# List VPC links not in AVAILABLE status
aws apigateway get-vpc-links \
    --query "items[?status!='AVAILABLE'].id" --output text | \
    tr '\t' '\n' | while read LINK_ID; do
    aws apigateway delete-vpc-link --vpc-link-id $LINK_ID
    echo "Deleted: $LINK_ID"
done
Output: Deleted: vpc001 Deleted: vpc002
Django Context: # Cleanup broken VPC links # Location.objects clean network # Working links only

flush-stage-authorizers-cache

Flushes the authorizer cache for a stage.

Syntax
aws apigateway flush-stage-authorizers-cache \
    --rest-api-id <value> \
    --stage-name <value>
Parameters
  • --rest-api-id (string): REST API identifier
  • --stage-name (string): Stage name
Return Values
  • No return value on success
Example 1: Flush prod authorizer cache

Clear cached auth tokens in production.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway flush-stage-authorizers-cache \
    --rest-api-id abc123def4 \
    --stage-name prod
Output: (No output on success)
Django Context: # Flush auth cache # JobPost.objects fresh auth # Token changes take effect
Example 2: Flush after policy change

Clear cache after updating authorizer.

#!/bin/bash
# After updating Cognito user pool
aws apigateway flush-stage-authorizers-cache \
    --rest-api-id abc123def4 \
    --stage-name prod

echo "Auth cache flushed - new policies active"
Output: Auth cache flushed - new policies active
Django Context: # Flush after policy update # User.objects new permissions # Immediate auth changes
Example 3: Flush staging cache

Clear cache in staging environment.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway flush-stage-authorizers-cache \
    --rest-api-id abc123def4 \
    --stage-name staging
Output: (No output on success)
Django Context: # Flush staging auth # Skills.objects test fresh tokens # Debugging auth issues
Example 4: Flush after token revocation

Clear cache to revoke user immediately.

#!/bin/bash
# User compromised - revoke immediately
echo "Revoking user tokens..."

aws apigateway flush-stage-authorizers-cache \
    --rest-api-id abc123def4 \
    --stage-name prod

echo "Authorizer cache flushed - user revoked"
Output: Revoking user tokens... Authorizer cache flushed - user revoked
Django Context: # Immediate user revocation # Author.objects access blocked # Security incident response
Example 5: Flush all stages

Clear authorizer cache for all stages.

#!/bin/bash
API_ID="abc123def4"
STAGES=("dev" "staging" "prod")

for STAGE in "${STAGES[@]}"; do
    aws apigateway flush-stage-authorizers-cache \
        --rest-api-id $API_ID \
        --stage-name $STAGE
    echo "Flushed: $STAGE"
done
Output: Flushed: dev Flushed: staging Flushed: prod
Django Context: # Flush all stage caches # Location.objects global auth refresh # Consistent auth state

flush-stage-cache

Flushes the API stage cache for a REST API.

Syntax
aws apigateway flush-stage-cache \
    --rest-api-id <value> \
    --stage-name <value>
Parameters
  • --rest-api-id (string): REST API identifier
  • --stage-name (string): Stage name
Return Values
  • No return value on success
Example 1: Flush production cache

Clear cached responses in production.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway flush-stage-cache \
    --rest-api-id abc123def4 \
    --stage-name prod
Output: (No output on success)
Django Context: # Flush response cache # JobPost.objects fresh data # Updated content visible
Example 2: Flush after data update

Clear cache after database changes.

#!/bin/bash
echo "Database updated, flushing cache..."
aws apigateway flush-stage-cache \
    --rest-api-id abc123def4 \
    --stage-name prod
echo "Cache flushed - fresh data served"
Output: Database updated, flushing cache... Cache flushed - fresh data served
Django Context: # Clear after User.objects update # Fresh QuerySet results # No stale data
Example 3: Flush staging cache

Clear cache during testing.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway flush-stage-cache \
    --rest-api-id abc123def4 \
    --stage-name staging
Output: (No output on success)
Django Context: # Flush staging cache # Skills.objects test data fresh # Debug caching issues
Example 4: Scheduled cache flush

Flush cache on schedule.

#!/bin/bash
# Run hourly to ensure fresh data
API_ID="abc123def4"
STAGE="prod"

aws apigateway flush-stage-cache \
    --rest-api-id $API_ID \
    --stage-name $STAGE

echo "$(date): Cache flushed for $STAGE"
Output: Mon Jan 15 10:00:00 UTC 2024: Cache flushed for prod
Django Context: # Scheduled cache invalidation # Author.objects always current # Hourly refresh cycle
Example 5: Flush all stages

Clear cache across all stages.

#!/bin/bash
API_ID="abc123def4"

for STAGE in dev staging prod; do
    aws apigateway flush-stage-cache \
        --rest-api-id $API_ID \
        --stage-name $STAGE
    echo "Flushed cache: $STAGE"
done
Output: Flushed cache: dev Flushed cache: staging Flushed cache: prod
Django Context: # Global cache flush # Location.objects all environments # Complete cache reset

generate-client-certificate

Generates a new client certificate for mutual TLS authentication.

Syntax
aws apigateway generate-client-certificate \
    [--description <value>] \
    [--tags <value>]
Parameters
  • --description (string): Certificate description
  • --tags (map): Resource tags
Return Values
  • clientCertificateId: Certificate identifier
  • pemEncodedCertificate: PEM-encoded certificate
  • expirationDate: Expiration date
Example 1: Generate certificate

Create new client certificate.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway generate-client-certificate \
    --description "Django jobpost backend mTLS certificate"
Output: { "clientCertificateId": "cert123new", "description": "Django jobpost backend mTLS certificate", "pemEncodedCertificate": "-----BEGIN CERTIFICATE-----\n...\n-----END CERTIFICATE-----", "createdDate": "2024-01-15T10:00:00Z", "expirationDate": "2025-01-15T10:00:00Z" }
Django Context: # New mTLS certificate # JobPost.objects secure backend # Install on backend servers
Example 2: Generate with tags

Create certificate with resource tags.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway generate-client-certificate \
    --description "Production API certificate" \
    --tags Environment=production,Team=backend
Output: { "clientCertificateId": "certprod456", "tags": {"Environment": "production", "Team": "backend"} }
Django Context: # Tagged production cert # User.objects secure access # Team ownership tracked
Example 3: Generate and save

Generate and save certificate to file.

#!/bin/bash
CERT=$(aws apigateway generate-client-certificate \
    --description "Django backend cert" \
    --query 'pemEncodedCertificate' --output text)

echo "$CERT" > /etc/ssl/api-gateway-client.pem
echo "Certificate saved to /etc/ssl/api-gateway-client.pem"
Output: Certificate saved to /etc/ssl/api-gateway-client.pem
Django Context: # Save cert for Skills.objects backend # Configure Django server # Enable mTLS authentication
Example 4: Generate rotation certificate

Create new certificate for rotation.

#!/bin/bash
NEW_CERT=$(aws apigateway generate-client-certificate \
    --description "Rotation certificate $(date +%Y-%m-%d)" \
    --query 'clientCertificateId' --output text)

echo "New certificate generated: $NEW_CERT"
echo "Update backend before removing old certificate"
Output: New certificate generated: certrot789 Update backend before removing old certificate
Django Context: # Certificate rotation # Author.objects security update # Rolling update procedure
Example 5: Generate for staging

Create staging environment certificate.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway generate-client-certificate \
    --description "Staging mTLS certificate" \
    --tags Environment=staging
Output: { "clientCertificateId": "certstg123", "description": "Staging mTLS certificate", "expirationDate": "2025-01-15T10:00:00Z" }
Django Context: # Staging certificate # Location.objects test mTLS # Separate from production

get-account

Gets information about the current API Gateway account settings.

Syntax
aws apigateway get-account
Parameters
  • No parameters required
Return Values
  • cloudwatchRoleArn: CloudWatch Logs role ARN
  • throttleSettings: Account-level throttle settings
  • features: Enabled features
Example 1: Get account info

Retrieve current account settings.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway get-account
Output: { "cloudwatchRoleArn": "arn:aws:iam::123456789012:role/APIGatewayCloudWatchRole", "throttleSettings": { "burstLimit": 5000, "rateLimit": 10000.0 }, "features": ["UsagePlans"] }
Django Context: # Account-level settings # JobPost.objects API limits # CloudWatch logging enabled
Example 2: Get throttle limits

Check account throttle settings.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway get-account \
    --query 'throttleSettings' --output json
Output: { "burstLimit": 5000, "rateLimit": 10000.0 }
Django Context: # Account throttle limits # User.objects 10K req/sec limit # 5K burst capacity
Example 3: Get CloudWatch role

Check CloudWatch logging configuration.

#!/bin/bash
ROLE=$(aws apigateway get-account \
    --query 'cloudwatchRoleArn' --output text)

if [ "$ROLE" != "None" ]; then
    echo "CloudWatch role: $ROLE"
else
    echo "CloudWatch logging not configured"
fi
Output: CloudWatch role: arn:aws:iam::123456789012:role/APIGatewayCloudWatchRole
Django Context: # CloudWatch logging role # Skills.objects API logs # Debugging enabled
Example 4: Check features

List enabled API Gateway features.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway get-account \
    --query 'features' --output text
Output: UsagePlans
Django Context: # Enabled features list # Author.objects usage plans active # Check feature availability
Example 5: Account summary

Display account configuration summary.

#!/bin/bash
echo "API Gateway Account Configuration"
echo "================================="
aws apigateway get-account --output yaml
Output: API Gateway Account Configuration ================================= cloudwatchRoleArn: arn:aws:iam::123456789012:role/APIGatewayCloudWatchRole throttleSettings: burstLimit: 5000 rateLimit: 10000.0 features: - UsagePlans
Django Context: # Full account summary # Location.objects API config # Governance documentation

get-api-key

Gets information about a specific API key.

Syntax
aws apigateway get-api-key \
    --api-key <value> \
    [--include-value]
Parameters
  • --api-key (string): API key identifier
  • --include-value (boolean): Include the key value in response
Return Values
  • id: API key identifier
  • name: Key name
  • value: Key value (if include-value specified)
  • enabled: Whether key is enabled
Example 1: Get API key details

Retrieve key information.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway get-api-key \
    --api-key key123prod
Output: { "id": "key123prod", "name": "anihpj-production", "description": "Production API key for jobpost", "enabled": true, "createdDate": "2024-01-01T00:00:00Z", "lastUpdatedDate": "2024-01-15T10:00:00Z" }
Django Context: # Get key metadata # JobPost.objects API key info # Check key status
Example 2: Get key with value

Include the actual key value.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway get-api-key \
    --api-key key123prod \
    --include-value
Output: { "id": "key123prod", "name": "anihpj-production", "value": "abc123xyz789secretkey", "enabled": true }
Django Context: # Get key value # User.objects credential retrieval # Secure key distribution
Example 3: Check key status

Verify if key is enabled.

#!/bin/bash
KEY_ID="key456"
ENABLED=$(aws apigateway get-api-key \
    --api-key $KEY_ID \
    --query 'enabled' --output text)

if [ "$ENABLED" = "True" ]; then
    echo "Key is active"
else
    echo "Key is disabled"
fi
Output: Key is active
Django Context: # Verify key status # Skills.objects access check # Debug auth issues
Example 4: Get key description

Retrieve key description and tags.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway get-api-key \
    --api-key keyclient789 \
    --query '{name: name, description: description, tags: tags}'
Output: { "name": "partner-acme", "description": "API key for ACME Corp integration", "tags": {"client": "acme", "tier": "premium"} }
Django Context: # Key metadata # Author.objects client info # Partner identification
Example 5: Export key for client

Get key value for client distribution.

#!/bin/bash
KEY_ID="keynewclient"

aws apigateway get-api-key \
    --api-key $KEY_ID \
    --include-value \
    --query 'value' --output text > client-api-key.txt

echo "API key saved to client-api-key.txt"
Output: API key saved to client-api-key.txt
Django Context: # Export key for client # Location.objects API onboarding # Secure key delivery

get-api-keys

Gets information about all API keys in the account.

Syntax
aws apigateway get-api-keys \
    [--name-query <value>] \
    [--include-values] \
    [--customer-id <value>]
Parameters
  • --name-query (string): Filter by name prefix
  • --include-values (boolean): Include key values
  • --customer-id (string): Filter by customer ID
Return Values
  • items: List of API keys
  • position: Pagination token
Example 1: List all API keys

Get all keys in account.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway get-api-keys
Output: { "items": [ {"id": "key001", "name": "prod-key", "enabled": true}, {"id": "key002", "name": "staging-key", "enabled": true}, {"id": "key003", "name": "partner-acme", "enabled": true} ] }
Django Context: # List all API keys # JobPost.objects key inventory # Key management overview
Example 2: Filter by name

Search keys by name prefix.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway get-api-keys \
    --name-query "partner"
Output: { "items": [ {"id": "key101", "name": "partner-acme", "enabled": true}, {"id": "key102", "name": "partner-globex", "enabled": true} ] }
Django Context: # Filter partner keys # User.objects external access # Partner key management
Example 3: List enabled keys only

Filter for active keys.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway get-api-keys \
    --query 'items[?enabled==`true`].{id: id, name: name}' \
    --output table
Output: --------------------------------- | GetApiKeys | +----------+--------------------+ | id | name | +----------+--------------------+ | key001 | prod-key | | key002 | staging-key | +----------+--------------------+
Django Context: # Active keys only # Skills.objects valid access # Exclude disabled keys
Example 4: Count API keys

Get total number of keys.

#!/bin/bash
COUNT=$(aws apigateway get-api-keys \
    --query 'items | length(@)')

echo "Total API keys: $COUNT"
Output: Total API keys: 15
Django Context: # Count all API keys # Author.objects key audit # Capacity planning
Example 5: Export key report

Generate API key report.

#!/bin/bash
echo "API Key Report - $(date)"
echo "========================="
aws apigateway get-api-keys \
    --query 'items[].{ID: id, Name: name, Enabled: enabled, Created: createdDate}' \
    --output table
Output: API Key Report - Mon Jan 15 2024 ========================= ----------------------------------------------------------- | GetApiKeys | +----------+----------------+---------+--------------------+ | ID | Name | Enabled | Created | +----------+----------------+---------+--------------------+ | key001 | prod-key | True | 2024-01-01T00:00Z | | key002 | staging-key | True | 2024-01-05T00:00Z | +----------+----------------+---------+--------------------+
Django Context: # API key audit report # Location.objects access inventory # Security compliance

get-authorizer

Gets information about a specific authorizer.

Syntax
aws apigateway get-authorizer \
    --rest-api-id <value> \
    --authorizer-id <value>
Parameters
  • --rest-api-id (string): REST API identifier
  • --authorizer-id (string): Authorizer identifier
Return Values
  • id: Authorizer identifier
  • name: Authorizer name
  • type: Authorizer type
  • authorizerUri: Lambda authorizer URI
  • providerARNs: Cognito provider ARNs
Example 1: Get Cognito authorizer

Retrieve Cognito authorizer details.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway get-authorizer \
    --rest-api-id abc123def4 \
    --authorizer-id auth123cognito
Output: { "id": "auth123cognito", "name": "cognito-authorizer", "type": "COGNITO_USER_POOLS", "providerARNs": ["arn:aws:cognito-idp:us-east-1:123456789012:userpool/us-east-1_ABC123"], "identitySource": "method.request.header.Authorization" }
Django Context: # Cognito authorizer config # JobPost.objects JWT auth # User pool integration
Example 2: Get Lambda authorizer

Retrieve Lambda authorizer details.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway get-authorizer \
    --rest-api-id abc123def4 \
    --authorizer-id auth456lambda
Output: { "id": "auth456lambda", "name": "custom-authorizer", "type": "TOKEN", "authorizerUri": "arn:aws:apigateway:us-east-1:lambda:path/2015-03-31/functions/arn:aws:lambda:us-east-1:123456789012:function:authFunction/invocations", "authorizerResultTtlInSeconds": 300 }
Django Context: # Lambda authorizer config # User.objects custom auth # Token validation function
Example 3: Check cache settings

Get authorizer caching configuration.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway get-authorizer \
    --rest-api-id abc123def4 \
    --authorizer-id auth789 \
    --query '{name: name, cacheTTL: authorizerResultTtlInSeconds}'
Output: { "name": "token-authorizer", "cacheTTL": 300 }
Django Context: # Authorizer cache TTL # Skills.objects auth caching # 5-minute token cache
Example 4: Get identity source

Check where auth token is extracted from.

#!/bin/bash
SOURCE=$(aws apigateway get-authorizer \
    --rest-api-id abc123def4 \
    --authorizer-id auth123 \
    --query 'identitySource' --output text)

echo "Authorization extracted from: $SOURCE"
Output: Authorization extracted from: method.request.header.Authorization
Django Context: # Identity source location # Author.objects header auth # Token extraction point
Example 5: Verify authorizer type

Check authorizer configuration type.

#!/bin/bash
API_ID="abc123def4"
AUTH_ID="auth456"

TYPE=$(aws apigateway get-authorizer \
    --rest-api-id $API_ID \
    --authorizer-id $AUTH_ID \
    --query 'type' --output text)

echo "Authorizer type: $TYPE"
case $TYPE in
    COGNITO_USER_POOLS) echo "Using Cognito authentication" ;;
    TOKEN) echo "Using Lambda token authorizer" ;;
    REQUEST) echo "Using Lambda request authorizer" ;;
esac
Output: Authorizer type: COGNITO_USER_POOLS Using Cognito authentication
Django Context: # Verify auth type # Location.objects auth method # Debug authorization

get-authorizers

Gets all authorizers for a REST API.

Syntax
aws apigateway get-authorizers \
    --rest-api-id <value>
Parameters
  • --rest-api-id (string): REST API identifier
Return Values
  • items: List of authorizers
Example 1: List all authorizers

Get all authorizers for an API.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway get-authorizers \
    --rest-api-id abc123def4
Output: { "items": [ {"id": "auth001", "name": "cognito-auth", "type": "COGNITO_USER_POOLS"}, {"id": "auth002", "name": "lambda-auth", "type": "TOKEN"} ] }
Django Context: # List all authorizers # JobPost.objects auth options # Security configuration
Example 2: Count authorizers

Get number of authorizers.

#!/bin/bash
COUNT=$(aws apigateway get-authorizers \
    --rest-api-id abc123def4 \
    --query 'items | length(@)')

echo "Total authorizers: $COUNT"
Output: Total authorizers: 2
Django Context: # Count authorizers # User.objects auth methods # Security audit
Example 3: List Cognito authorizers

Filter for Cognito type only.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway get-authorizers \
    --rest-api-id abc123def4 \
    --query 'items[?type==`COGNITO_USER_POOLS`]'
Output: [ {"id": "auth001", "name": "cognito-auth", "type": "COGNITO_USER_POOLS"} ]
Django Context: # Filter Cognito authorizers # Skills.objects JWT auth # User pool integration
Example 4: Authorizer summary

Display authorizer summary table.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway get-authorizers \
    --rest-api-id abc123def4 \
    --query 'items[].{ID: id, Name: name, Type: type}' \
    --output table
Output: ------------------------------------------ | GetAuthorizers | +----------+---------------+-------------+ | ID | Name | Type | +----------+---------------+-------------+ | auth001 | cognito-auth | COGNITO.. | | auth002 | lambda-auth | TOKEN | +----------+---------------+-------------+
Django Context: # Authorizer overview # Author.objects auth config # Security documentation
Example 5: Export authorizer IDs

Get list of authorizer IDs.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway get-authorizers \
    --rest-api-id abc123def4 \
    --query 'items[].id' --output text
Output: auth001 auth002
Django Context: # Export authorizer IDs # Location.objects scripting # Automation reference

get-base-path-mapping

Gets a base path mapping for a custom domain.

Syntax
aws apigateway get-base-path-mapping \
    --domain-name <value> \
    --base-path <value>
Parameters
  • --domain-name (string): Custom domain name
  • --base-path (string): Base path (use "(none)" for root)
Return Values
  • basePath: Base path
  • restApiId: REST API identifier
  • stage: Stage name
Example 1: Get v2 path mapping

Retrieve v2 API path mapping.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway get-base-path-mapping \
    --domain-name api.anihpj.com \
    --base-path v2
Output: { "basePath": "v2", "restApiId": "abc123def4", "stage": "prod" }
Django Context: # Get v2 mapping # JobPost.objects current API # Version routing
Example 2: Get root mapping

Check root path configuration.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway get-base-path-mapping \
    --domain-name api.anihpj.com \
    --base-path "(none)"
Output: { "basePath": "(none)", "restApiId": "abc123def4", "stage": "prod" }
Django Context: # Root path mapping # User.objects default route # No version prefix
Example 3: Verify API connection

Check which API a path maps to.

#!/bin/bash
API_ID=$(aws apigateway get-base-path-mapping \
    --domain-name api.anihpj.com \
    --base-path v2 \
    --query 'restApiId' --output text)

echo "Path /v2 maps to API: $API_ID"
Output: Path /v2 maps to API: abc123def4
Django Context: # Verify API mapping # Skills.objects routing # Debug domain issues
Example 4: Check stage mapping

Verify which stage is mapped.

#!/bin/bash
STAGE=$(aws apigateway get-base-path-mapping \
    --domain-name api.anihpj.com \
    --base-path v2 \
    --query 'stage' --output text)

echo "Mapped to stage: $STAGE"
Output: Mapped to stage: prod
Django Context: # Verify stage # Author.objects prod/staging # Environment routing
Example 5: Mapping summary

Display complete mapping info.

#!/bin/bash
DOMAIN="api.anihpj.com"
PATH="v2"

echo "Base Path Mapping for $DOMAIN/$PATH"
aws apigateway get-base-path-mapping \
    --domain-name $DOMAIN \
    --base-path $PATH \
    --output yaml
Output: Base Path Mapping for api.anihpj.com/v2 basePath: v2 restApiId: abc123def4 stage: prod
Django Context: # Full mapping details # Location.objects configuration # Domain documentation

get-base-path-mappings

Gets all base path mappings for a custom domain.

Syntax
aws apigateway get-base-path-mappings \
    --domain-name <value>
Parameters
  • --domain-name (string): Custom domain name
Return Values
  • items: List of base path mappings
Example 1: List all mappings

Get all path mappings for domain.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway get-base-path-mappings \
    --domain-name api.anihpj.com
Output: { "items": [ {"basePath": "v1", "restApiId": "oldapi123", "stage": "prod"}, {"basePath": "v2", "restApiId": "abc123def4", "stage": "prod"} ] }
Django Context: # List all path mappings # JobPost.objects versioned APIs # Domain routing overview
Example 2: Count mappings

Get number of path mappings.

#!/bin/bash
COUNT=$(aws apigateway get-base-path-mappings \
    --domain-name api.anihpj.com \
    --query 'items | length(@)')

echo "Total mappings: $COUNT"
Output: Total mappings: 2
Django Context: # Count API versions # User.objects supported paths # Version management
Example 3: List base paths only

Extract just the path names.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway get-base-path-mappings \
    --domain-name api.anihpj.com \
    --query 'items[].basePath' --output text
Output: v1 v2
Django Context: # Available API versions # Skills.objects path options # Client documentation
Example 4: Mappings table

Display mappings in table format.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway get-base-path-mappings \
    --domain-name api.anihpj.com \
    --query 'items[].{Path: basePath, API: restApiId, Stage: stage}' \
    --output table
Output: ------------------------------------------- | GetBasePathMappings | +------+---------------+------------------+ | Path | API | Stage | +------+---------------+------------------+ | v1 | oldapi123 | prod | | v2 | abc123def4 | prod | +------+---------------+------------------+
Django Context: # Mapping overview table # Author.objects routing # Version documentation
Example 5: Find deprecated versions

Identify old API versions.

#!/bin/bash
echo "Checking for legacy API versions..."
aws apigateway get-base-path-mappings \
    --domain-name api.anihpj.com \
    --query 'items[?basePath==`v1` || basePath==`legacy`]'
Output: Checking for legacy API versions... [ {"basePath": "v1", "restApiId": "oldapi123", "stage": "prod"} ]
Django Context: # Find deprecated APIs # Location.objects migration check # Plan version sunset

get-client-certificate

Gets a client certificate by identifier.

Syntax
aws apigateway get-client-certificate \
    --client-certificate-id <value>
Parameters
  • --client-certificate-id (string): Certificate identifier
Return Values
  • clientCertificateId: Certificate ID
  • pemEncodedCertificate: PEM certificate
  • expirationDate: Expiration date
Example 1: Get certificate details

Retrieve certificate information.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway get-client-certificate \
    --client-certificate-id cert123prod
Output: { "clientCertificateId": "cert123prod", "description": "Production mTLS certificate", "pemEncodedCertificate": "-----BEGIN CERTIFICATE-----\n...", "createdDate": "2024-01-01T00:00:00Z", "expirationDate": "2025-01-01T00:00:00Z" }
Django Context: # Get certificate details # JobPost.objects mTLS config # Backend authentication
Example 2: Check expiration

Get certificate expiration date.

#!/bin/bash
EXPIRES=$(aws apigateway get-client-certificate \
    --client-certificate-id cert123prod \
    --query 'expirationDate' --output text)

echo "Certificate expires: $EXPIRES"
Output: Certificate expires: 2025-01-01T00:00:00Z
Django Context: # Check cert expiration # User.objects security planning # Schedule rotation
Example 3: Export certificate

Save certificate to file.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway get-client-certificate \
    --client-certificate-id cert123prod \
    --query 'pemEncodedCertificate' --output text > cert.pem

echo "Certificate saved to cert.pem"
Output: Certificate saved to cert.pem
Django Context: # Export certificate # Skills.objects backend setup # Configure Django server
Example 4: Verify certificate

Check certificate validity.

#!/bin/bash
CERT_ID="cert123prod"

aws apigateway get-client-certificate \
    --client-certificate-id $CERT_ID \
    --query '{ID: clientCertificateId, Expires: expirationDate, Created: createdDate}'
Output: { "ID": "cert123prod", "Expires": "2025-01-01T00:00:00Z", "Created": "2024-01-01T00:00:00Z" }
Django Context: # Verify certificate # Author.objects security audit # Certificate lifecycle
Example 5: Certificate summary

Display full certificate info.

#!/bin/bash
echo "Certificate Details"
echo "==================="
aws apigateway get-client-certificate \
    --client-certificate-id cert123prod \
    --output yaml
Output: Certificate Details =================== clientCertificateId: cert123prod description: Production mTLS certificate createdDate: '2024-01-01T00:00:00Z' expirationDate: '2025-01-01T00:00:00Z'
Django Context: # Full certificate info # Location.objects security # Documentation

get-client-certificates

Gets all client certificates in the account.

Syntax
aws apigateway get-client-certificates
Parameters
  • No required parameters
Return Values
  • items: List of client certificates
Example 1: List all certificates

Get all client certificates.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway get-client-certificates
Output: { "items": [ {"clientCertificateId": "cert001", "description": "Production cert"}, {"clientCertificateId": "cert002", "description": "Staging cert"} ] }
Django Context: # List all certificates # JobPost.objects mTLS inventory # Security audit
Example 2: Find expiring certificates

List certificates expiring soon.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway get-client-certificates \
    --query 'items[?expirationDate<`2024-06-01`].{ID: clientCertificateId, Expires: expirationDate}'
Output: [ {"ID": "cert001old", "Expires": "2024-03-01T00:00:00Z"} ]
Django Context: # Find expiring certs # User.objects security planning # Renewal reminders
Example 3: Count certificates

Get total certificate count.

#!/bin/bash
COUNT=$(aws apigateway get-client-certificates \
    --query 'items | length(@)')

echo "Total certificates: $COUNT"
Output: Total certificates: 3
Django Context: # Count certificates # Skills.objects security inventory # Capacity planning
Example 4: Certificate table

Display certificates in table.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway get-client-certificates \
    --query 'items[].{ID: clientCertificateId, Description: description, Expires: expirationDate}' \
    --output table
Output: --------------------------------------------------- | GetClientCertificates | +-----------+------------------+------------------+ | ID | Description | Expires | +-----------+------------------+------------------+ | cert001 | Production cert | 2025-01-01T00Z | | cert002 | Staging cert | 2025-06-01T00Z | +-----------+------------------+------------------+
Django Context: # Certificate overview # Author.objects security report # Compliance documentation
Example 5: Export certificate IDs

Get list of certificate IDs.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway get-client-certificates \
    --query 'items[].clientCertificateId' --output text
Output: cert001 cert002 cert003
Django Context: # Export cert IDs # Location.objects scripting # Automation support

get-deployment

Gets information about a specific deployment.

Syntax
aws apigateway get-deployment \
    --rest-api-id <value> \
    --deployment-id <value>
Parameters
  • --rest-api-id (string): REST API identifier
  • --deployment-id (string): Deployment identifier
Return Values
  • id: Deployment ID
  • description: Deployment description
  • createdDate: Creation date
Example 1: Get deployment details

Retrieve deployment information.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway get-deployment \
    --rest-api-id abc123def4 \
    --deployment-id dep789
Output: { "id": "dep789", "description": "JobPost API v2.1.0 release", "createdDate": "2024-01-15T10:00:00Z" }
Django Context: # Get deployment info # JobPost.objects version # Release history
Example 2: Check deployment date

Get when deployment was created.

#!/bin/bash
CREATED=$(aws apigateway get-deployment \
    --rest-api-id abc123def4 \
    --deployment-id dep789 \
    --query 'createdDate' --output text)

echo "Deployment created: $CREATED"
Output: Deployment created: 2024-01-15T10:00:00Z
Django Context: # Deployment timestamp # User.objects release date # Audit trail
Example 3: Get deployment description

Check deployment release notes.

#!/bin/bash
DESC=$(aws apigateway get-deployment \
    --rest-api-id abc123def4 \
    --deployment-id dep789 \
    --query 'description' --output text)

echo "Release: $DESC"
Output: Release: JobPost API v2.1.0 release
Django Context: # Deployment notes # Skills.objects changes # Version documentation
Example 4: Verify deployment exists

Check if deployment is valid.

#!/bin/bash
DEP_ID="dep789"

if aws apigateway get-deployment \
    --rest-api-id abc123def4 \
    --deployment-id $DEP_ID 2>/dev/null; then
    echo "Deployment $DEP_ID exists"
else
    echo "Deployment $DEP_ID not found"
fi
Output: Deployment dep789 exists
Django Context: # Verify deployment # Author.objects rollback check # Deployment validation
Example 5: Full deployment info

Display complete deployment details.

#!/bin/bash
echo "Deployment Information"
aws apigateway get-deployment \
    --rest-api-id abc123def4 \
    --deployment-id dep789 \
    --output yaml
Output: Deployment Information id: dep789 description: JobPost API v2.1.0 release createdDate: '2024-01-15T10:00:00Z'
Django Context: # Full deployment details # Location.objects API history # Release documentation

get-deployments

Gets all deployments for a REST API.

Syntax
aws apigateway get-deployments \
    --rest-api-id <value>
Parameters
  • --rest-api-id (string): REST API identifier
Return Values
  • items: List of deployments
Example 1: List all deployments

Get deployment history.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway get-deployments \
    --rest-api-id abc123def4
Output: { "items": [ {"id": "dep003", "description": "v2.1.0", "createdDate": "2024-01-15T10:00:00Z"}, {"id": "dep002", "description": "v2.0.1", "createdDate": "2024-01-10T10:00:00Z"}, {"id": "dep001", "description": "v2.0.0", "createdDate": "2024-01-01T10:00:00Z"} ] }
Django Context: # Deployment history # JobPost.objects releases # Version tracking
Example 2: Get latest deployment

Find most recent deployment.

#!/bin/bash
LATEST=$(aws apigateway get-deployments \
    --rest-api-id abc123def4 \
    --query 'items | sort_by(@, &createdDate) | [-1]')

echo "Latest deployment: $LATEST"
Output: Latest deployment: {"id": "dep003", "description": "v2.1.0"}
Django Context: # Find latest deployment # User.objects current version # Release identification
Example 3: Count deployments

Get total deployment count.

#!/bin/bash
COUNT=$(aws apigateway get-deployments \
    --rest-api-id abc123def4 \
    --query 'items | length(@)')

echo "Total deployments: $COUNT"
Output: Total deployments: 15
Django Context: # Count deployments # Skills.objects release count # History management
Example 4: Deployment history table

Display deployments in table.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway get-deployments \
    --rest-api-id abc123def4 \
    --query 'items[].{ID: id, Description: description, Created: createdDate}' \
    --output table
Output: --------------------------------------------------- | GetDeployments | +---------+-------------+-------------------------+ | ID | Description | Created | +---------+-------------+-------------------------+ | dep003 | v2.1.0 | 2024-01-15T10:00:00Z | | dep002 | v2.0.1 | 2024-01-10T10:00:00Z | +---------+-------------+-------------------------+
Django Context: # Deployment table # Author.objects changelog # Release documentation
Example 5: Export deployment IDs

Get list of deployment IDs.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway get-deployments \
    --rest-api-id abc123def4 \
    --query 'items[].id' --output text
Output: dep003 dep002 dep001
Django Context: # Export deployment IDs # Location.objects rollback options # Automation scripting

get-documentation-part

Gets a documentation part for a REST API.

Syntax
aws apigateway get-documentation-part \
    --rest-api-id <value> \
    --documentation-part-id <value>
Parameters
  • --rest-api-id (string): REST API identifier
  • --documentation-part-id (string): Documentation part ID
Return Values
  • id: Documentation part ID
  • location: API location
  • properties: Documentation content
Example 1: Get documentation part

Retrieve specific documentation.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway get-documentation-part \
    --rest-api-id abc123def4 \
    --documentation-part-id doc789
Output: { "id": "doc789", "location": {"type": "API"}, "properties": "{\"description\": \"JobPost API for job listings management\"}" }
Django Context: # Get API documentation # JobPost.objects API description # Developer portal content
Example 2: Get method documentation

Retrieve method-level docs.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway get-documentation-part \
    --rest-api-id abc123def4 \
    --documentation-part-id docmethod123
Output: { "id": "docmethod123", "location": {"type": "METHOD", "path": "/jobs", "method": "GET"}, "properties": "{\"summary\": \"List all job posts\"}" }
Django Context: # Method documentation # User.objects GET endpoint # OpenAPI spec content
Example 3: Get response documentation

Retrieve response docs.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway get-documentation-part \
    --rest-api-id abc123def4 \
    --documentation-part-id docresp456
Output: { "id": "docresp456", "location": {"type": "RESPONSE", "path": "/jobs", "method": "GET", "statusCode": "200"}, "properties": "{\"description\": \"Successful response with job list\"}" }
Django Context: # Response documentation # Skills.objects 200 response # API contract docs
Example 4: Extract properties

Get just the documentation content.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway get-documentation-part \
    --rest-api-id abc123def4 \
    --documentation-part-id doc789 \
    --query 'properties' --output text | jq .
Output: { "description": "JobPost API for job listings management" }
Django Context: # Extract doc content # Author.objects API description # Portal integration
Example 5: Check documentation location

Verify what the docs apply to.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway get-documentation-part \
    --rest-api-id abc123def4 \
    --documentation-part-id doc789 \
    --query 'location'
Output: { "type": "API" }
Django Context: # Documentation scope # Location.objects doc target # API vs method vs response

get-documentation-parts

Gets all documentation parts for a REST API.

Syntax
aws apigateway get-documentation-parts \
    --rest-api-id <value> \
    [--type <value>] \
    [--path <value>]
Parameters
  • --rest-api-id (string): REST API identifier
  • --type (string): Filter by location type
  • --path (string): Filter by path
Return Values
  • items: List of documentation parts
Example 1: List all documentation

Get all documentation parts.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway get-documentation-parts \
    --rest-api-id abc123def4
Output: { "items": [ {"id": "doc001", "location": {"type": "API"}}, {"id": "doc002", "location": {"type": "RESOURCE", "path": "/jobs"}}, {"id": "doc003", "location": {"type": "METHOD", "path": "/jobs", "method": "GET"}} ] }
Django Context: # List all docs # JobPost.objects API documentation # Complete doc inventory
Example 2: Filter by type

Get only method documentation.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway get-documentation-parts \
    --rest-api-id abc123def4 \
    --type METHOD
Output: { "items": [ {"id": "doc003", "location": {"type": "METHOD", "path": "/jobs", "method": "GET"}}, {"id": "doc004", "location": {"type": "METHOD", "path": "/jobs", "method": "POST"}} ] }
Django Context: # Method docs only # User.objects endpoint docs # OpenAPI operations
Example 3: Filter by path

Get docs for specific resource.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway get-documentation-parts \
    --rest-api-id abc123def4 \
    --path "/jobs"
Output: { "items": [ {"id": "doc002", "location": {"type": "RESOURCE", "path": "/jobs"}}, {"id": "doc003", "location": {"type": "METHOD", "path": "/jobs", "method": "GET"}} ] }
Django Context: # Path-specific docs # Skills.objects /jobs documentation # Resource-level docs
Example 4: Count documentation parts

Get total documentation count.

#!/bin/bash
COUNT=$(aws apigateway get-documentation-parts \
    --rest-api-id abc123def4 \
    --query 'items | length(@)')

echo "Documentation parts: $COUNT"
Output: Documentation parts: 25
Django Context: # Count doc parts # Author.objects API coverage # Documentation completeness
Example 5: Documentation summary

Display documentation overview.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway get-documentation-parts \
    --rest-api-id abc123def4 \
    --query 'items[].{ID: id, Type: location.type, Path: location.path}' \
    --output table
Output: ------------------------------------------- | GetDocumentationParts | +---------+----------+--------------------+ | ID | Type | Path | +---------+----------+--------------------+ | doc001 | API | None | | doc002 | RESOURCE | /jobs | | doc003 | METHOD | /jobs | +---------+----------+--------------------+
Django Context: # Documentation inventory # Location.objects API docs # OpenAPI generation

get-documentation-version

Gets a specific documentation version.

Syntax
aws apigateway get-documentation-version \
    --rest-api-id <value> \
    --documentation-version <value>
Parameters
  • --rest-api-id (string): REST API identifier
  • --documentation-version (string): Version identifier
Return Values
  • version: Version identifier
  • description: Version description
  • createdDate: Creation timestamp
Example 1: Get documentation version

Retrieve specific doc version.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway get-documentation-version \
    --rest-api-id abc123def4 \
    --documentation-version "2.0"
Output: { "version": "2.0", "description": "API documentation v2.0 - JobPost release", "createdDate": "2024-01-15T10:00:00Z" }
Django Context: # Get doc version # JobPost.objects API docs v2.0 # Version-specific documentation
Example 2: Check version date

Get when version was published.

#!/bin/bash
CREATED=$(aws apigateway get-documentation-version \
    --rest-api-id abc123def4 \
    --documentation-version "2.0" \
    --query 'createdDate' --output text)

echo "Documentation published: $CREATED"
Output: Documentation published: 2024-01-15T10:00:00Z
Django Context: # Doc publish date # User.objects documentation timeline # Version history
Example 3: Get version description

Check version release notes.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway get-documentation-version \
    --rest-api-id abc123def4 \
    --documentation-version "2.0" \
    --query 'description' --output text
Output: API documentation v2.0 - JobPost release
Django Context: # Version description # Skills.objects release notes # Documentation changelog
Example 4: Verify version exists

Check if doc version is available.

#!/bin/bash
DOC_VERSION="2.0"

if aws apigateway get-documentation-version \
    --rest-api-id abc123def4 \
    --documentation-version "$DOC_VERSION" 2>/dev/null; then
    echo "Version $DOC_VERSION exists"
else
    echo "Version $DOC_VERSION not found"
fi
Output: Version 2.0 exists
Django Context: # Verify doc version # Author.objects portal check # Documentation availability
Example 5: Full version info

Display complete version details.

#!/bin/bash
echo "Documentation Version Details"
aws apigateway get-documentation-version \
    --rest-api-id abc123def4 \
    --documentation-version "2.0" \
    --output yaml
Output: Documentation Version Details version: '2.0' description: API documentation v2.0 - JobPost release createdDate: '2024-01-15T10:00:00Z'
Django Context: # Complete version info # Location.objects documentation # Portal configuration

get-documentation-versions

Gets all documentation versions for a REST API.

Syntax
aws apigateway get-documentation-versions \
    --rest-api-id <value>
Parameters
  • --rest-api-id (string): REST API identifier
Return Values
  • items: List of documentation versions
Example 1: List all doc versions

Get documentation version history.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway get-documentation-versions \
    --rest-api-id abc123def4
Output: { "items": [ {"version": "2.0", "description": "v2.0 release"}, {"version": "1.1", "description": "v1.1 patch"}, {"version": "1.0", "description": "Initial release"} ] }
Django Context: # All doc versions # JobPost.objects documentation history # Version management
Example 2: Get latest version

Find most recent documentation.

#!/bin/bash
LATEST=$(aws apigateway get-documentation-versions \
    --rest-api-id abc123def4 \
    --query 'items | sort_by(@, &createdDate) | [-1].version' \
    --output text)

echo "Latest documentation: $LATEST"
Output: Latest documentation: 2.0
Django Context: # Find latest docs # User.objects current version # Portal default
Example 3: Count versions

Get total version count.

#!/bin/bash
COUNT=$(aws apigateway get-documentation-versions \
    --rest-api-id abc123def4 \
    --query 'items | length(@)')

echo "Documentation versions: $COUNT"
Output: Documentation versions: 5
Django Context: # Version count # Skills.objects doc history # Lifecycle tracking
Example 4: Version history table

Display versions in table.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway get-documentation-versions \
    --rest-api-id abc123def4 \
    --query 'items[].{Version: version, Description: description, Created: createdDate}' \
    --output table
Output: --------------------------------------------------- | GetDocumentationVersions | +---------+------------------+--------------------+ | Version | Description | Created | +---------+------------------+--------------------+ | 2.0 | v2.0 release | 2024-01-15T10:00Z | | 1.1 | v1.1 patch | 2024-01-05T10:00Z | +---------+------------------+--------------------+
Django Context: # Version history table # Author.objects changelog # Documentation timeline
Example 5: List version identifiers

Export version list.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway get-documentation-versions \
    --rest-api-id abc123def4 \
    --query 'items[].version' --output text
Output: 2.0 1.1 1.0
Django Context: # Export versions # Location.objects scripting # Automation support

get-domain-name

Gets a custom domain name configuration.

Syntax
aws apigateway get-domain-name \
    --domain-name <value>
Parameters
  • --domain-name (string): Custom domain name
Return Values
  • domainName: Domain name
  • certificateArn: ACM certificate ARN
  • distributionDomainName: CloudFront distribution
Example 1: Get domain configuration

Retrieve domain details.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway get-domain-name \
    --domain-name api.anihpj.com
Output: { "domainName": "api.anihpj.com", "certificateArn": "arn:aws:acm:us-east-1:123456789012:certificate/abc123", "distributionDomainName": "d1234567890.cloudfront.net", "endpointConfiguration": {"types": ["EDGE"]} }
Django Context: # Get domain config # JobPost.objects custom domain # DNS configuration
Example 2: Get CloudFront distribution

Find CloudFront domain for DNS.

#!/bin/bash
CF_DOMAIN=$(aws apigateway get-domain-name \
    --domain-name api.anihpj.com \
    --query 'distributionDomainName' --output text)

echo "CNAME target: $CF_DOMAIN"
Output: CNAME target: d1234567890.cloudfront.net
Django Context: # CloudFront distribution # User.objects DNS setup # CNAME record target
Example 3: Get certificate ARN

Find associated ACM certificate.

#!/bin/bash
CERT=$(aws apigateway get-domain-name \
    --domain-name api.anihpj.com \
    --query 'certificateArn' --output text)

echo "Certificate: $CERT"
Output: Certificate: arn:aws:acm:us-east-1:123456789012:certificate/abc123
Django Context: # Get SSL certificate # Skills.objects HTTPS config # Certificate management
Example 4: Check endpoint type

Verify if edge or regional.

#!/bin/bash
TYPE=$(aws apigateway get-domain-name \
    --domain-name api.anihpj.com \
    --query 'endpointConfiguration.types[0]' --output text)

echo "Endpoint type: $TYPE"
Output: Endpoint type: EDGE
Django Context: # Check endpoint type # Author.objects edge/regional # Performance configuration
Example 5: Full domain summary

Display complete domain info.

#!/bin/bash
echo "Custom Domain Configuration"
aws apigateway get-domain-name \
    --domain-name api.anihpj.com \
    --output yaml
Output: Custom Domain Configuration domainName: api.anihpj.com certificateArn: arn:aws:acm:us-east-1:123456789012:certificate/abc123 distributionDomainName: d1234567890.cloudfront.net endpointConfiguration: types: - EDGE
Django Context: # Full domain config # Location.objects DNS setup # Infrastructure documentation

get-domain-name-access-associations

Gets access associations for a private custom domain.

Syntax
aws apigateway get-domain-name-access-associations \
    [--domain-name <value>]
Parameters
  • --domain-name (string): Custom domain name filter
Return Values
  • items: List of access associations
Example 1: List access associations

Get all VPC access associations.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway get-domain-name-access-associations \
    --domain-name internal.anihpj.com
Output: { "items": [ {"domainNameAccessAssociationArn": "arn:aws:apigateway:us-east-1::/domainnames/internal.anihpj.com/accessassociations/assoc001"}, {"domainNameAccessAssociationArn": "arn:aws:apigateway:us-east-1::/domainnames/internal.anihpj.com/accessassociations/assoc002"} ] }
Django Context: # List VPC associations # JobPost.objects private access # Internal API configuration
Example 2: Count associations

Get number of VPC accesses.

#!/bin/bash
COUNT=$(aws apigateway get-domain-name-access-associations \
    --domain-name internal.anihpj.com \
    --query 'items | length(@)')

echo "VPC access associations: $COUNT"
Output: VPC access associations: 2
Django Context: # Count VPC accesses # User.objects private domains # Network inventory
Example 3: List all associations

Get associations across all domains.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway get-domain-name-access-associations
Output: { "items": [ {"domainNameAccessAssociationArn": "...assoc001"}, {"domainNameAccessAssociationArn": "...assoc002"}, {"domainNameAccessAssociationArn": "...assoc003"} ] }

    --domain-name internal.anihpj.com \
    --query 'items[].domainNameAccessAssociationArn' --output text
Output: arn:aws:apigateway:us-east-1::/domainnames/internal.anihpj.com/accessassociations/assoc001 arn:aws:apigateway:us-east-1::/domainnames/internal.anihpj.com/accessassociations/assoc002
Django Context: # Export association ARNs # Author.objects scripting # Automation support
Example 5: Association summary

Display associations in table.

#!/bin/bash
echo "Private Domain Access Associations"
aws apigateway get-domain-name-access-associations \
    --domain-name internal.anihpj.com \
    --output yaml
Output: Private Domain Access Associations items: - domainNameAccessAssociationArn: arn:aws:apigateway:us-east-1::/domainnames/internal.anihpj.com/accessassociations/assoc001 - domainNameAccessAssociationArn: arn:aws:apigateway:us-east-1::/domainnames/internal.anihpj.com/accessassociations/assoc002
Django Context: # Association overview # Location.objects VPC access # Network documentation

get-domain-names

Gets all custom domain names in the account.

Syntax
aws apigateway get-domain-names
Parameters
  • No required parameters
Return Values
  • items: List of custom domain names
Example 1: List all domains

Get all custom domains.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway get-domain-names
Output: { "items": [ {"domainName": "api.anihpj.com"}, {"domainName": "staging-api.anihpj.com"}, {"domainName": "internal.anihpj.com"} ] }
Django Context: # List all domains # JobPost.objects custom domains # DNS inventory

Get total domain count.

#!/bin/bash
COUNT=$(aws apigateway get-domain-names \
    --query 'items | length(@)')

echo "Custom domains: $COUNT"
Output: Custom domains: 3

Extract just domain names.

#!/bin/bash

    --query 'items[].domainName' --output text

Example 4: Domain summary table

Display domains with details.

#!/bin/bash

    --query 'items[].{Domain: domainName, Endpoint: endpointConfiguration.types[0]}' \
    --output table
Output: -------------------------------------- | GetDomainNames | +-------------------------+----------+ | Domain | Endpoint | +-------------------------+----------+ | api.anihpj.com | EDGE | | staging-api.anihpj.com | EDGE | | internal.anihpj.com | REGIONAL | +-------------------------+----------+
Django Context: # Domain overview # Author.objects API domains # Infrastructure documentation
Example 5: Find production domains

Filter for production domains.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway get-domain-names \
    --query 'items[?!contains(domainName, `staging`) && !contains(domainName, `test`)].domainName' \
    --output text
Output: api.anihpj.com internal.anihpj.com
Django Context: # Production domains only # Location.objects live APIs # Production inventory

get-export

Exports a REST API in a specified format.

Syntax
aws apigateway get-export \
    --rest-api-id <value> \
    --stage-name <value> \
    --export-type <value> \
    [--parameters <value>] \
    [--accepts <value>] \
    <outfile>
Parameters
  • --rest-api-id (string): REST API identifier
  • --stage-name (string): Stage to export
  • --export-type (string): Export format (oas30, swagger)
  • --accepts (string): Content type (application/json, application/yaml)
  • outfile (string): Output file path
Return Values
  • API specification written to output file
Example 1: Export OpenAPI 3.0

Export API as OpenAPI 3.0 JSON.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway get-export \
    --rest-api-id abc123def4 \
    --stage-name prod \
    --export-type oas30 \
    --accepts application/json \
    api-spec.json

echo "API exported to api-spec.json"
Output: API exported to api-spec.json
Django Context: # Export OpenAPI spec # JobPost.objects API documentation # Client SDK generation
Example 2: Export as YAML

Export API as OpenAPI YAML.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway get-export \
    --rest-api-id abc123def4 \
    --stage-name prod \
    --export-type oas30 \
    --accepts application/yaml \
    api-spec.yaml
Output: (No output, file created)
Django Context: # Export as YAML # User.objects human-readable spec # Developer documentation
Example 3: Export Swagger 2.0

Export legacy Swagger format.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway get-export \
    --rest-api-id abc123def4 \
    --stage-name prod \
    --export-type swagger \
    swagger.json
Output: (No output, file created)
Django Context: # Swagger 2.0 export # Skills.objects legacy tooling # Backwards compatibility
Example 4: Export with extensions

Include API Gateway extensions.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway get-export \
    --rest-api-id abc123def4 \
    --stage-name prod \
    --export-type oas30 \
    --parameters extensions='integrations' \
    api-with-integrations.json
Output: (No output, file created)
Django Context: # Export with integrations # Author.objects full API config # Infrastructure as code
Example 5: Export staging for comparison

Export staging API for diff.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway get-export \
    --rest-api-id abc123def4 \
    --stage-name staging \
    --export-type oas30 \
    staging-spec.json

aws apigateway get-export \
    --rest-api-id abc123def4 \
    --stage-name prod \
    --export-type oas30 \
    prod-spec.json

echo "Compare: diff staging-spec.json prod-spec.json"
Output: Compare: diff staging-spec.json prod-spec.json
Django Context: # Export for comparison # Location.objects API diff # Release verification

get-gateway-response

Gets a gateway response configuration.

Syntax
aws apigateway get-gateway-response \
    --rest-api-id <value> \
    --response-type <value>
Parameters
  • --rest-api-id (string): REST API identifier
  • --response-type (string): Response type
Return Values
  • responseType: Response type
  • statusCode: HTTP status code
  • responseParameters: Response headers
  • responseTemplates: Response body templates
Example 1: Get 4XX response

Get default 4XX error config.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway get-gateway-response \
    --rest-api-id abc123def4 \
    --response-type DEFAULT_4XX
Output: { "responseType": "DEFAULT_4XX", "statusCode": "400", "responseParameters": { "gatewayresponse.header.Access-Control-Allow-Origin": "'*'" }, "responseTemplates": { "application/json": "{\"error\": \"$context.error.message\"}" } }
Django Context: # Get 4XX response config # JobPost.objects error handling # CORS headers
Example 2: Get unauthorized response

Check 401 response config.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway get-gateway-response \
    --rest-api-id abc123def4 \
    --response-type UNAUTHORIZED
Output: { "responseType": "UNAUTHORIZED", "statusCode": "401", "responseTemplates": { "application/json": "{\"message\": \"Authentication required\"}" } }
Django Context: # Get 401 config # User.objects auth errors # Custom error messages
Example 3: Check 5XX response

Get server error configuration.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway get-gateway-response \
    --rest-api-id abc123def4 \
    --response-type DEFAULT_5XX
Output: { "responseType": "DEFAULT_5XX", "statusCode": "500", "responseTemplates": { "application/json": "{\"message\": \"Internal server error\"}" } }
Django Context: # Get 5XX config # Skills.objects server errors # Error handling
Example 4: Get response headers

Check CORS headers.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway get-gateway-response \
    --rest-api-id abc123def4 \
    --response-type DEFAULT_4XX \
    --query 'responseParameters'
Output: { "gatewayresponse.header.Access-Control-Allow-Origin": "'*'", "gatewayresponse.header.Access-Control-Allow-Headers": "'*'" }
Django Context: # CORS headers config # Author.objects cross-origin # Browser security
Example 5: Get quota exceeded response

Check throttling error config.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway get-gateway-response \
    --rest-api-id abc123def4 \
    --response-type QUOTA_EXCEEDED
Output: { "responseType": "QUOTA_EXCEEDED", "statusCode": "429", "responseTemplates": { "application/json": "{\"message\": \"Rate limit exceeded\"}" } }
Django Context: # Quota exceeded config # Location.objects rate limits # Throttling messages

get-gateway-responses

Gets all gateway responses for a REST API.

Syntax
aws apigateway get-gateway-responses \
    --rest-api-id <value>
Parameters
  • --rest-api-id (string): REST API identifier
Return Values
  • items: List of gateway responses
Example 1: List all gateway responses

Get all error response configs.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway get-gateway-responses \
    --rest-api-id abc123def4
Output: { "items": [ {"responseType": "DEFAULT_4XX", "statusCode": "400"}, {"responseType": "DEFAULT_5XX", "statusCode": "500"}, {"responseType": "UNAUTHORIZED", "statusCode": "401"} ] }
Django Context: # All gateway responses # JobPost.objects error config # Error handling overview
Example 2: Count configured responses

Get number of custom responses.

#!/bin/bash
COUNT=$(aws apigateway get-gateway-responses \
    --rest-api-id abc123def4 \
    --query 'items | length(@)')

echo "Configured responses: $COUNT"
Output: Configured responses: 8
Django Context: # Count responses # User.objects error types # Configuration audit
Example 3: List response types

Get all response type names.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway get-gateway-responses \
    --rest-api-id abc123def4 \
    --query 'items[].responseType' --output text
Output: DEFAULT_4XX DEFAULT_5XX UNAUTHORIZED ACCESS_DENIED QUOTA_EXCEEDED THROTTLED
Django Context: # List response types # Skills.objects error catalog # Documentation
Example 4: Response summary table

Display responses in table.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway get-gateway-responses \
    --rest-api-id abc123def4 \
    --query 'items[].{Type: responseType, Status: statusCode}' \
    --output table
Output: -------------------------------------- | GetGatewayResponses | +------------------+-----------------+ | Type | Status | +------------------+-----------------+ | DEFAULT_4XX | 400 | | DEFAULT_5XX | 500 | | UNAUTHORIZED | 401 | +------------------+-----------------+
Django Context: # Response overview # Author.objects error config # API documentation
Example 5: Find custom responses

List non-default responses.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway get-gateway-responses \
    --rest-api-id abc123def4 \
    --query 'items[?defaultResponse==`false`]'
Output: [ {"responseType": "UNAUTHORIZED", "defaultResponse": false} ]
Django Context: # Find custom responses # Location.objects customizations # Configuration review

get-integration

Gets an integration configuration for an API method.

Syntax
aws apigateway get-integration \
    --rest-api-id <value> \
    --resource-id <value> \
    --http-method <value>
Parameters
  • --rest-api-id (string): REST API identifier
  • --resource-id (string): Resource identifier
  • --http-method (string): HTTP method
Return Values
  • type: Integration type
  • uri: Integration URI
  • httpMethod: HTTP method
Example 1: Get Lambda integration

Retrieve Lambda integration config.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway get-integration \
    --rest-api-id abc123def4 \
    --resource-id res789 \
    --http-method GET
Output: { "type": "AWS_PROXY", "uri": "arn:aws:apigateway:us-east-1:lambda:path/2015-03-31/functions/arn:aws:lambda:us-east-1:123456789012:function:jobpost-handler/invocations", "httpMethod": "POST", "passthroughBehavior": "WHEN_NO_MATCH" }
Django Context: # Lambda integration # JobPost.objects handler # Serverless backend
Example 2: Get HTTP integration

Check HTTP proxy config.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway get-integration \
    --rest-api-id abc123def4 \
    --resource-id res456 \
    --http-method GET
Output: { "type": "HTTP_PROXY", "uri": "https://backend.anihpj.com/api/jobs", "httpMethod": "GET", "connectionType": "INTERNET" }
Django Context: # HTTP proxy integration # User.objects Django backend # Direct proxy to Django
Example 3: Check integration type

Get just the integration type.

#!/bin/bash
TYPE=$(aws apigateway get-integration \
    --rest-api-id abc123def4 \
    --resource-id res789 \
    --http-method GET \
    --query 'type' --output text)

echo "Integration type: $TYPE"
Output: Integration type: AWS_PROXY
Django Context: # Check integration type # Skills.objects backend type # Architecture documentation
Example 4: Get VPC link integration

Check private integration.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway get-integration \
    --rest-api-id abc123def4 \
    --resource-id resvpc \
    --http-method GET \
    --query '{type: type, connectionId: connectionId}'
Output: { "type": "HTTP_PROXY", "connectionId": "vpc123link" }
Django Context: # VPC link integration # Author.objects private backend # Network configuration
Example 5: Full integration details

Display complete integration config.

#!/bin/bash
echo "Integration Configuration"
aws apigateway get-integration \
    --rest-api-id abc123def4 \
    --resource-id res789 \
    --http-method GET \
    --output yaml
Output: Integration Configuration type: AWS_PROXY uri: arn:aws:apigateway:us-east-1:lambda:path/... httpMethod: POST passthroughBehavior: WHEN_NO_MATCH contentHandling: CONVERT_TO_TEXT
Django Context: # Full integration config # Location.objects backend setup # Infrastructure documentation

get-integration-response

Gets an integration response configuration.

Syntax
aws apigateway get-integration-response \
    --rest-api-id <value> \
    --resource-id <value> \
    --http-method <value> \
    --status-code <value>
Parameters
  • --rest-api-id (string): REST API identifier
  • --resource-id (string): Resource identifier
  • --http-method (string): HTTP method
  • --status-code (string): Status code
Return Values
  • statusCode: HTTP status code
  • selectionPattern: Response selection regex
  • responseTemplates: Response body templates
Example 1: Get 200 response config

Retrieve success response config.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway get-integration-response \
    --rest-api-id abc123def4 \
    --resource-id res789 \
    --http-method GET \
    --status-code 200
Output: { "statusCode": "200", "selectionPattern": "", "responseParameters": { "method.response.header.Content-Type": "'application/json'" }, "responseTemplates": {} }
Django Context: # 200 response config # JobPost.objects success response # Content type headers
Example 2: Get error response

Check 400 error response.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway get-integration-response \
    --rest-api-id abc123def4 \
    --resource-id res456 \
    --http-method POST \
    --status-code 400
Output: { "statusCode": "400", "selectionPattern": ".*[Bb]ad[Rr]equest.*", "responseTemplates": { "application/json": "{\"error\": \"$input.path('$.errorMessage')\"}" } }
Django Context: # 400 error response # User.objects validation errors # Error transformation
Example 3: Check selection pattern

Get regex for response selection.

#!/bin/bash
PATTERN=$(aws apigateway get-integration-response \
    --rest-api-id abc123def4 \
    --resource-id res789 \
    --http-method GET \
    --status-code 500 \
    --query 'selectionPattern' --output text)

echo "Selection pattern: $PATTERN"
Output: Selection pattern: .*[Ee]rror.*
Django Context: # Response selection # Skills.objects error matching # Regex configuration
Example 4: Get response template

Check response body template.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway get-integration-response \
    --rest-api-id abc123def4 \
    --resource-id res789 \
    --http-method GET \
    --status-code 200 \
    --query 'responseTemplates'
Output: { "application/json": "#set($root=$input.path('$'))$root" }
Django Context: # Response template # Author.objects body mapping # VTL transformation
Example 5: Full response details

Display complete response config.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway get-integration-response \
    --rest-api-id abc123def4 \
    --resource-id res789 \
    --http-method GET \
    --status-code 200 \
    --output yaml
Output: statusCode: '200' selectionPattern: '' responseParameters: method.response.header.Content-Type: "'application/json'" responseTemplates: {}
Django Context: # Full response config # Location.objects response setup # Documentation

get-method

Gets information about an API method.

Syntax
aws apigateway get-method \
    --rest-api-id <value> \
    --resource-id <value> \
    --http-method <value>
Parameters
  • --rest-api-id (string): REST API identifier
  • --resource-id (string): Resource identifier
  • --http-method (string): HTTP method
Return Values
  • httpMethod: HTTP method
  • authorizationType: Auth type
  • apiKeyRequired: Whether API key is required
  • methodResponses: Response definitions
Example 1: Get method configuration

Retrieve method details.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway get-method \
    --rest-api-id abc123def4 \
    --resource-id res789 \
    --http-method GET
Output: { "httpMethod": "GET", "authorizationType": "COGNITO_USER_POOLS", "authorizerId": "auth123", "apiKeyRequired": true, "requestParameters": {}, "methodResponses": { "200": {"statusCode": "200"} } }
Django Context: # Method configuration # JobPost.objects GET endpoint # Auth and API key config
Example 2: Check authorization

Get auth configuration for method.

#!/bin/bash
AUTH=$(aws apigateway get-method \
    --rest-api-id abc123def4 \
    --resource-id res789 \
    --http-method GET \
    --query 'authorizationType' --output text)

echo "Authorization: $AUTH"
Output: Authorization: COGNITO_USER_POOLS
Django Context: # Check auth type # User.objects security config # Endpoint protection
Example 3: Check API key requirement

Verify if API key is required.

#!/bin/bash
KEY_REQUIRED=$(aws apigateway get-method \
    --rest-api-id abc123def4 \
    --resource-id res789 \
    --http-method GET \
    --query 'apiKeyRequired' --output text)

echo "API key required: $KEY_REQUIRED"
Output: API key required: True
Django Context: # Check API key # Skills.objects access control # Usage tracking
Example 4: Get request parameters

Check required parameters.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway get-method \
    --rest-api-id abc123def4 \
    --resource-id res789 \
    --http-method GET \
    --query 'requestParameters'
Output: { "method.request.querystring.page": true, "method.request.querystring.limit": false }
Django Context: # Request parameters # Author.objects pagination # Query string validation
Example 5: Full method details

Display complete method config.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway get-method \
    --rest-api-id abc123def4 \
    --resource-id res789 \
    --http-method GET \
    --output yaml
Output: httpMethod: GET authorizationType: COGNITO_USER_POOLS authorizerId: auth123 apiKeyRequired: true methodResponses: '200': statusCode: '200'
Django Context: # Full method config # Location.objects endpoint setup # API documentation

get-method-response

Gets a method response configuration.

Syntax
aws apigateway get-method-response \
    --rest-api-id <value> \
    --resource-id <value> \
    --http-method <value> \
    --status-code <value>
Parameters
  • --rest-api-id (string): REST API identifier
  • --resource-id (string): Resource identifier
  • --http-method (string): HTTP method
  • --status-code (string): Status code
Return Values
  • statusCode: HTTP status code
  • responseParameters: Response header definitions
  • responseModels: Response body models
Example 1: Get 200 method response

Retrieve success response definition.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway get-method-response \
    --rest-api-id abc123def4 \
    --resource-id res789 \
    --http-method GET \
    --status-code 200
Output: { "statusCode": "200", "responseParameters": { "method.response.header.Content-Type": true }, "responseModels": { "application/json": "JobPostList" } }
Django Context: # 200 response definition # JobPost.objects success response # Response schema
Example 2: Get 404 response

Check not found response config.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway get-method-response \
    --rest-api-id abc123def4 \
    --resource-id res789 \
    --http-method GET \
    --status-code 404
Output: { "statusCode": "404", "responseModels": { "application/json": "Error" } }
Django Context: # 404 response definition # User.objects not found # Error model
Example 3: Get response model

Check which model is used.

#!/bin/bash
MODEL=$(aws apigateway get-method-response \
    --rest-api-id abc123def4 \
    --resource-id res789 \
    --http-method GET \
    --status-code 200 \
    --query 'responseModels."application/json"' --output text)

echo "Response model: $MODEL"
Output: Response model: JobPostList
Django Context: # Response model name # Skills.objects schema # API documentation
Example 4: Check response headers

Get header definitions.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway get-method-response \
    --rest-api-id abc123def4 \
    --resource-id res789 \
    --http-method GET \
    --status-code 200 \
    --query 'responseParameters'
Output: { "method.response.header.Content-Type": true, "method.response.header.X-Request-Id": true }
Django Context: # Response headers # Author.objects header config # API contract
Example 5: Full response config

Display complete response definition.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway get-method-response \
    --rest-api-id abc123def4 \
    --resource-id res789 \
    --http-method GET \
    --status-code 200 \
    --output yaml
Output: statusCode: '200' responseParameters: method.response.header.Content-Type: true responseModels: application/json: JobPostList
Django Context: # Full response definition # Location.objects API contract # OpenAPI generation

get-model

Gets information about a data model.

Syntax
aws apigateway get-model \
    --rest-api-id <value> \
    --model-name <value> \
    [--flatten]
Parameters
  • --rest-api-id (string): REST API identifier
  • --model-name (string): Model name
  • --flatten (boolean): Include resolved schema
Return Values
  • id: Model identifier
  • name: Model name
  • schema: JSON Schema
  • contentType: Content type
Example 1: Get model schema

Retrieve model definition.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway get-model \
    --rest-api-id abc123def4 \
    --model-name JobPost
Output: { "id": "model123", "name": "JobPost", "contentType": "application/json", "schema": "{\"type\":\"object\",\"properties\":{\"title\":{\"type\":\"string\"},\"company\":{\"type\":\"string\"}}}" }
Django Context: # Get JobPost model # JobPost.objects schema # API contract definition
Example 2: Get flattened schema

Resolve all schema references.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway get-model \
    --rest-api-id abc123def4 \
    --model-name JobPost \
    --flatten
Output: { "name": "JobPost", "schema": "{\"type\":\"object\",\"properties\":{...expanded...}}" }
Django Context: # Flattened schema # User.objects full definition # No $ref references
Example 3: Extract schema only

Get just the JSON schema.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway get-model \
    --rest-api-id abc123def4 \
    --model-name JobPost \
    --query 'schema' --output text | jq .
Output: { "type": "object", "properties": { "title": {"type": "string"}, "company": {"type": "string"} } }
Django Context: # Extract schema # Skills.objects JSON Schema # Validation rules
Example 4: Get error model

Check error response model.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway get-model \
    --rest-api-id abc123def4 \
    --model-name Error
Output: { "name": "Error", "schema": "{\"type\":\"object\",\"properties\":{\"message\":{\"type\":\"string\"},\"code\":{\"type\":\"integer\"}}}" }
Django Context: # Error model schema # Author.objects error format # Consistent errors
Example 5: Model summary

Display model info.

#!/bin/bash
echo "Model: JobPost"
aws apigateway get-model \
    --rest-api-id abc123def4 \
    --model-name JobPost \
    --query '{Name: name, ContentType: contentType}' \
    --output yaml
Output: Model: JobPost Name: JobPost ContentType: application/json
Django Context: # Model overview # Location.objects schema info # Documentation

get-models

Gets all models for a REST API.

Syntax
aws apigateway get-models \
    --rest-api-id <value>
Parameters
  • --rest-api-id (string): REST API identifier
Return Values
  • items: List of models
Example 1: List all models

Get all data models.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway get-models \
    --rest-api-id abc123def4
Output: { "items": [ {"id": "m001", "name": "JobPost"}, {"id": "m002", "name": "User"}, {"id": "m003", "name": "Error"}, {"id": "m004", "name": "Empty"} ] }
Django Context: # List all models # JobPost.objects schema inventory # API contract models
Example 2: Count models

Get number of defined models.

#!/bin/bash
COUNT=$(aws apigateway get-models \
    --rest-api-id abc123def4 \
    --query 'items | length(@)')

echo "Models defined: $COUNT"
Output: Models defined: 8
Django Context: # Count models # User.objects schema count # API complexity
Example 3: List model names

Get all model names.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway get-models \
    --rest-api-id abc123def4 \
    --query 'items[].name' --output text
Output: JobPost User Error Empty Skills Author Location
Django Context: # Model names # Skills.objects schema list # Documentation index
Example 4: Models table

Display models in table.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway get-models \
    --rest-api-id abc123def4 \
    --query 'items[].{ID: id, Name: name, ContentType: contentType}' \
    --output table
Output: ------------------------------------------------- | GetModels | +--------+------------+------------------------+ | ID | Name | ContentType | +--------+------------+------------------------+ | m001 | JobPost | application/json | | m002 | User | application/json | | m003 | Error | application/json | +--------+------------+------------------------+
Django Context: # Models overview # Author.objects schema inventory # API documentation
Example 5: Export model IDs

Get model IDs for scripting.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway get-models \
    --rest-api-id abc123def4 \
    --query 'items[].{name: name, id: id}' --output json
Output: [ {"name": "JobPost", "id": "m001"}, {"name": "User", "id": "m002"} ]
Django Context: # Export model IDs # Location.objects automation # Scripting support

get-model-template

Gets the model template for a specified model.

Syntax
aws apigateway get-model-template \
    --rest-api-id <value> \
    --model-name <value>
Parameters
  • --rest-api-id (string): REST API identifier
  • --model-name (string): Model name
Return Values
  • value: Template string
Example 1: Get model template

Retrieve mapping template.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway get-model-template \
    --rest-api-id abc123def4 \
    --model-name JobPost
Output: { "value": "{\"title\": \"\", \"company\": \"\", \"location\": \"\", \"skills\": []}" }
Django Context: # Model template # JobPost.objects request template # Request body structure
Example 2: Extract template value

Get just the template string.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway get-model-template \
    --rest-api-id abc123def4 \
    --model-name JobPost \
    --query 'value' --output text | jq .
Output: { "title": "", "company": "", "location": "", "skills": [] }
Django Context: # Template structure # User.objects request format # Client documentation
Example 3: Save template to file

Export template for client use.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway get-model-template \
    --rest-api-id abc123def4 \
    --model-name JobPost \
    --query 'value' --output text > jobpost-template.json

echo "Template saved to jobpost-template.json"
Output: Template saved to jobpost-template.json
Django Context: # Export template # Skills.objects request sample # SDK generation
Example 4: Generate all templates

Export templates for all models.

#!/bin/bash
API_ID="abc123def4"

for MODEL in JobPost User Skills; do
    aws apigateway get-model-template \
        --rest-api-id $API_ID \
        --model-name $MODEL \
        --query 'value' --output text > "${MODEL,,}-template.json"
    echo "Generated: ${MODEL,,}-template.json"
done
Output: Generated: jobpost-template.json Generated: user-template.json Generated: skills-template.json
Django Context: # Generate all templates # Author.objects batch export # Client SDK support
Example 5: Verify template structure

Check template is valid JSON.

#!/bin/bash
TEMPLATE=$(aws apigateway get-model-template \
    --rest-api-id abc123def4 \
    --model-name JobPost \
    --query 'value' --output text)

if echo "$TEMPLATE" | jq . > /dev/null 2>&1; then
    echo "Valid JSON template"
else
    echo "Invalid template format"
fi
Output: Valid JSON template
Django Context: # Validate template # Location.objects JSON check # Quality assurance

get-request-validator

Retrieves a specific request validator from an API Gateway REST API. Request validators validate incoming requests before they reach your backend integration.

Syntax
aws apigateway get-request-validator \
    --rest-api-id <value> \
    --request-validator-id <value>
Parameters
  • --rest-api-id (string): The REST API identifier
  • --request-validator-id (string): The request validator identifier
Return Value
  • id (string): Validator identifier
  • name (string): Validator name
  • validateRequestBody (boolean): Whether body is validated
  • validateRequestParameters (boolean): Whether parameters are validated
Example 1: Get JobPost body validator

Retrieve validator for JobPost creation requests.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway get-request-validator \
    --rest-api-id abc123def4 \
    --request-validator-id rv123abc
Output: { "id": "rv123abc", "name": "jobpost-body-validator", "validateRequestBody": true, "validateRequestParameters": false }
Django Context: # Validate JobPost.objects.create() body # Skills.objects validation # Required fields check
Example 2: Get parameter validator

Retrieve validator for query parameters.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway get-request-validator \
    --rest-api-id abc123def4 \
    --request-validator-id rv456def
Output: { "id": "rv456def", "name": "param-validator", "validateRequestBody": false, "validateRequestParameters": true }
Django Context: # Parameter validation # User.objects.filter() params # Location.objects query check
