Output: { "clientStatus": 200, "principalId": "user123", "latency": 120 }
Django Context: # Request authorizer # User.objects.get(pk=123) # Multi-source auth
Example 4: Test with stage variables

Test authorizer with env variables.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway test-invoke-authorizer \
    --rest-api-id abc123def4 \
    --authorizer-id auth123lambda \
    --headers Authorization="Bearer testtoken" \
    --stage-variables environment=dev,authEndpoint=dev-auth.anihpj.com
Output: { "clientStatus": 200, "principalId": "dev-user", "latency": 100 }
Django Context: # Stage variables test # Author.objects dev env # Environment-specific auth
Example 5: Check authorization policy

Verify generated IAM policy.

#!/bin/bash
RESULT=$(aws apigateway test-invoke-authorizer \
    --rest-api-id abc123def4 \
    --authorizer-id auth123lambda \
    --headers Authorization="Bearer validtoken")

echo "$RESULT" | jq '.policy' | jq -r '.' | jq .
Output: { "Version": "2012-10-17", "Statement": [ { "Effect": "Allow", "Action": "execute-api:Invoke", "Resource": "arn:aws:execute-api:us-east-1:123456789012:abc123def4/*/GET/jobposts" } ] }
Django Context: # Policy verification # Location.objects permissions # IAM policy check

test-invoke-method

Tests an API method by simulating a request. Validates method configuration before deployment.

Syntax
aws apigateway test-invoke-method \
    --rest-api-id <value> \
    --resource-id <value> \
    --http-method <value> \
    [--path-with-query-string <value>] \
    [--body <value>] \
    [--headers <value>] \
    [--stage-variables <value>]
Parameters
  • --rest-api-id (string): REST API identifier
  • --resource-id (string): Resource identifier
  • --http-method (string): HTTP method to test
  • --path-with-query-string (string): Path with query params
  • --body (string): Request body
  • --headers (map): Request headers
Return Value
  • status (integer): HTTP status code
  • body (string): Response body
  • headers (object): Response headers
  • latency (long): Execution latency
  • log (string): Execution log
Example 1: Test GET method

Test listing jobposts endpoint.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway test-invoke-method \
    --rest-api-id abc123def4 \
    --resource-id res123abc \
    --http-method GET
Output: { "status": 200, "body": "[{\"id\": 1, \"title\": \"Django Developer\"}, {\"id\": 2, \"title\": \"Python Engineer\"}]", "headers": { "Content-Type": "application/json" }, "latency": 250, "log": "Execution log..." }
Django Context: # Test GET # JobPost.objects.all() # List endpoint test
Example 2: Test POST method

Test creating a jobpost.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway test-invoke-method \
    --rest-api-id abc123def4 \
    --resource-id res123abc \
    --http-method POST \
    --body '{"title": "New Django Job", "skills": ["Python", "Django"]}' \
    --headers Content-Type=application/json
Output: { "status": 201, "body": "{\"id\": 3, \"title\": \"New Django Job\", \"created\": true}", "latency": 300 }
Django Context: # Test POST # JobPost.objects.create() # Create endpoint test
Example 3: Test with path parameter

Test getting single jobpost by ID.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway test-invoke-method \
    --rest-api-id abc123def4 \
    --resource-id res789id \
    --http-method GET \
    --path-with-query-string "/jobposts/123"
Output: { "status": 200, "body": "{\"id\": 123, \"title\": \"Senior Python Developer\"}", "latency": 150 }
Django Context: # Test with ID # JobPost.objects.get(pk=123) # Detail endpoint test
Example 4: Test with query parameters

Test filtering with query params.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway test-invoke-method \
    --rest-api-id abc123def4 \
    --resource-id res123abc \
    --http-method GET \
    --path-with-query-string "/jobposts?skill=Django&location=NYC"
Output: { "status": 200, "body": "[{\"id\": 1, \"title\": \"Django Developer NYC\"}]", "latency": 200 }
Django Context: # Query params test # Skills.objects.filter() # Location filtering
Example 5: Test with stage variables

Test method with environment config.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway test-invoke-method \
    --rest-api-id abc123def4 \
    --resource-id res123abc \
    --http-method GET \
    --stage-variables environment=staging,dbHost=staging-db.anihpj.com
Output: { "status": 200, "body": "[{\"source\": \"staging\"}]", "latency": 180 }
Django Context: # Stage variables test # Author.objects staging # Environment config

untag-resource

Removes tags from an API Gateway resource. Deletes specified tag keys from the resource.

Syntax
aws apigateway untag-resource \
    --resource-arn <value> \
    --tag-keys <value>
Parameters
  • --resource-arn (string): ARN of the resource
  • --tag-keys (list): List of tag keys to remove
Return Value

No return value on success.

Example 1: Remove single tag

Remove environment tag from API.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway untag-resource \
    --resource-arn arn:aws:apigateway:us-east-1::/restapis/abc123def4 \
    --tag-keys Environment

echo "Environment tag removed"
Output: Environment tag removed
Django Context: # Remove tag # JobPost.objects cleanup # Tag management
Example 2: Remove multiple tags

Remove several tags at once.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway untag-resource \
    --resource-arn arn:aws:apigateway:us-east-1::/restapis/abc123def4 \
    --tag-keys Environment Project Team

echo "Multiple tags removed"
Output: Multiple tags removed
Django Context: # Remove multiple # Skills.objects batch # Bulk cleanup
Example 3: Remove stage tags

Remove tags from deployment stage.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway untag-resource \
    --resource-arn arn:aws:apigateway:us-east-1::/restapis/abc123def4/stages/prod \
    --tag-keys Version DeployDate

echo "Stage tags removed"
Output: Stage tags removed
Django Context: # Stage untag # User.objects cleanup # Deployment metadata
Example 4: Remove deprecated tags

Clean up old or invalid tags.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway untag-resource \
    --resource-arn arn:aws:apigateway:us-east-1::/restapis/abc123def4 \
    --tag-keys OldTag DeprecatedTag LegacyProject

echo "Deprecated tags cleaned up"
Output: Deprecated tags cleaned up
Django Context: # Cleanup deprecated # Author.objects maintenance # Tag housekeeping
Example 5: Remove VPC link tags

Remove tags from VPC link.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway untag-resource \
    --resource-arn arn:aws:apigateway:us-east-1::/vpclinks/vpcl123abc \
    --tag-keys Purpose Network

echo "VPC link tags removed"
Output: VPC link tags removed
Django Context: # VPC link untag # Location.objects cleanup # Network metadata

update-account

Updates the API Gateway account settings. Configures CloudWatch logging role and other account-level settings.

Syntax
aws apigateway update-account \
    --patch-operations <value>
Parameters
  • --patch-operations (list): List of patch operations to apply
Return Value
  • cloudwatchRoleArn (string): CloudWatch IAM role ARN
  • throttleSettings (object): Account throttle settings
  • features (array): Enabled features
Example 1: Set CloudWatch role

Configure logging role for API Gateway.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway update-account \
    --patch-operations op=replace,path=/cloudwatchRoleArn,value=arn:aws:iam::123456789012:role/api-gateway-cloudwatch-role
Output: { "cloudwatchRoleArn": "arn:aws:iam::123456789012:role/api-gateway-cloudwatch-role", "throttleSettings": { "rateLimit": 10000.0, "burstLimit": 5000 } }
Django Context: # CloudWatch role # JobPost.objects logging # Account configuration
Example 2: Remove CloudWatch role

Disable CloudWatch logging.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway update-account \
    --patch-operations op=replace,path=/cloudwatchRoleArn,value=

echo "CloudWatch logging disabled"
Output: { "cloudwatchRoleArn": "" } CloudWatch logging disabled
Django Context: # Remove logging # Skills.objects disable # Cost optimization
Example 3: Check current settings

Update and verify account settings.

#!/bin/bash
RESULT=$(aws apigateway update-account \
    --patch-operations op=replace,path=/cloudwatchRoleArn,value=arn:aws:iam::123456789012:role/api-logging)

echo "$RESULT" | jq '.cloudwatchRoleArn'
Output: "arn:aws:iam::123456789012:role/api-logging"
Django Context: # Verify settings # User.objects config # Account validation
Example 4: Update with new role

Replace existing role with new one.

#!/bin/bash
NEW_ROLE="arn:aws:iam::123456789012:role/enhanced-api-logging"

aws apigateway update-account \
    --patch-operations "op=replace,path=/cloudwatchRoleArn,value=$NEW_ROLE"

echo "Logging role updated"
Output: { "cloudwatchRoleArn": "arn:aws:iam::123456789012:role/enhanced-api-logging" } Logging role updated
Django Context: # Role replacement # Author.objects logging # Enhanced monitoring
Example 5: View throttle settings

Get account throttle configuration.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway update-account \
    --patch-operations op=replace,path=/cloudwatchRoleArn,value=arn:aws:iam::123456789012:role/api-role \
    --query 'throttleSettings'
Output: { "rateLimit": 10000.0, "burstLimit": 5000 }
Django Context: # Throttle settings # Location.objects limits # Account quotas

update-api-key

Updates an existing API key. Modifies key properties like name, description, and enabled status.

Syntax
aws apigateway update-api-key \
    --api-key <value> \
    --patch-operations <value>
Parameters
  • --api-key (string): API key identifier
  • --patch-operations (list): Patch operations to apply
Return Value
  • id (string): API key identifier
  • name (string): Updated name
  • enabled (boolean): Enabled status
  • description (string): Updated description
Example 1: Update key name

Rename an API key.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway update-api-key \
    --api-key key123abc \
    --patch-operations op=replace,path=/name,value=anihpj-premium-customer-key
Output: { "id": "key123abc", "name": "anihpj-premium-customer-key", "enabled": true }
Django Context: # Rename key # JobPost.objects customer # Key management
Example 2: Disable API key

Temporarily disable a key.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway update-api-key \
    --api-key key123abc \
    --patch-operations op=replace,path=/enabled,value=false
Output: { "id": "key123abc", "enabled": false }
Django Context: # Disable key # Skills.objects revoke # Access control
Example 3: Enable API key

Re-enable a disabled key.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway update-api-key \
    --api-key key123abc \
    --patch-operations op=replace,path=/enabled,value=true

echo "API key re-enabled"
Output: { "id": "key123abc", "enabled": true } API key re-enabled
Django Context: # Enable key # User.objects activate # Restore access
Example 4: Update description

Change key description.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway update-api-key \
    --api-key key123abc \
    --patch-operations op=replace,path=/description,value="Enterprise customer key for JobPost API"
Output: { "id": "key123abc", "description": "Enterprise customer key for JobPost API" }
Django Context: # Update description # Author.objects metadata # Documentation
Example 5: Multiple updates

Update name and description together.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway update-api-key \
    --api-key key123abc \
    --patch-operations \
        op=replace,path=/name,value=enterprise-key \
        op=replace,path=/description,value="Updated enterprise key"
Output: { "id": "key123abc", "name": "enterprise-key", "description": "Updated enterprise key" }
Django Context: # Multiple updates # Location.objects batch # Combined operations

update-authorizer

Updates an existing authorizer. Modifies Lambda authorizer or Cognito settings.

Syntax
aws apigateway update-authorizer \
    --rest-api-id <value> \
    --authorizer-id <value> \
    --patch-operations <value>
Parameters
  • --rest-api-id (string): REST API identifier
  • --authorizer-id (string): Authorizer identifier
  • --patch-operations (list): Patch operations
Return Value
  • id (string): Authorizer identifier
  • name (string): Updated name
  • authorizerResultTtlInSeconds (integer): Cache TTL
Example 1: Update cache TTL

Increase authorization cache time.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway update-authorizer \
    --rest-api-id abc123def4 \
    --authorizer-id auth123lambda \
    --patch-operations op=replace,path=/authorizerResultTtlInSeconds,value=600
Output: { "id": "auth123lambda", "name": "anihpj-jwt-authorizer", "authorizerResultTtlInSeconds": 600 }
Django Context: # Update cache TTL # JobPost.objects auth cache # Performance optimization
Example 2: Update Lambda function

Change authorizer Lambda ARN.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway update-authorizer \
    --rest-api-id abc123def4 \
    --authorizer-id auth123lambda \
    --patch-operations op=replace,path=/authorizerUri,value=arn:aws:apigateway:us-east-1:lambda:path/2015-03-31/functions/arn:aws:lambda:us-east-1:123456789012:function:new-authorizer/invocations
Output: { "id": "auth123lambda", "authorizerUri": "arn:aws:apigateway:us-east-1:lambda:path/..." }
Django Context: # Update Lambda # Skills.objects new auth # Function migration
Example 3: Rename authorizer

Change authorizer name.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway update-authorizer \
    --rest-api-id abc123def4 \
    --authorizer-id auth123lambda \
    --patch-operations op=replace,path=/name,value=anihpj-enhanced-authorizer
Output: { "id": "auth123lambda", "name": "anihpj-enhanced-authorizer" }
Django Context: # Rename authorizer # User.objects auth name # Documentation update
Example 4: Update identity source

Change token extraction header.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway update-authorizer \
    --rest-api-id abc123def4 \
    --authorizer-id auth123lambda \
    --patch-operations op=replace,path=/identitySource,value=method.request.header.X-Auth-Token
Output: { "id": "auth123lambda", "identitySource": "method.request.header.X-Auth-Token" }
Django Context: # Identity source # Author.objects header # Token location
Example 5: Disable caching

Set TTL to 0 to disable caching.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway update-authorizer \
    --rest-api-id abc123def4 \
    --authorizer-id auth123lambda \
    --patch-operations op=replace,path=/authorizerResultTtlInSeconds,value=0

echo "Authorizer caching disabled"
Output: { "id": "auth123lambda", "authorizerResultTtlInSeconds": 0 } Authorizer caching disabled
Django Context: # Disable cache # Location.objects real-time # Always authenticate

update-base-path-mapping

Updates a base path mapping for a custom domain. Changes the API and stage association.

Syntax
aws apigateway update-base-path-mapping \
    --domain-name <value> \
    --base-path <value> \
    --patch-operations <value>
Parameters
  • --domain-name (string): Custom domain name
  • --base-path (string): Base path (use "(none)" for empty)
  • --patch-operations (list): Patch operations
Return Value
  • basePath (string): Base path
  • restApiId (string): REST API ID
  • stage (string): Stage name
Example 1: Change stage

Update mapping to new stage.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway update-base-path-mapping \
    --domain-name api.anihpj.com \
    --base-path v1 \
    --patch-operations op=replace,path=/stage,value=prod
Output: { "basePath": "v1", "restApiId": "abc123def4", "stage": "prod" }
Django Context: # Change stage # JobPost.objects production # Deployment update
Example 2: Change API

Point mapping to different API.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway update-base-path-mapping \
    --domain-name api.anihpj.com \
    --base-path v1 \
    --patch-operations op=replace,path=/restApiId,value=newapi456
Output: { "basePath": "v1", "restApiId": "newapi456", "stage": "prod" }
Django Context: # Change API # Skills.objects new backend # API migration
Example 3: Update root mapping

Update mapping without base path.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway update-base-path-mapping \
    --domain-name api.anihpj.com \
    --base-path "(none)" \
    --patch-operations op=replace,path=/stage,value=v2-prod
Output: { "basePath": "(none)", "restApiId": "abc123def4", "stage": "v2-prod" }
Django Context: # Root mapping # User.objects root path # Domain root
Example 4: Update both API and stage

Change API and stage together.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway update-base-path-mapping \
    --domain-name api.anihpj.com \
    --base-path v2 \
    --patch-operations \
        op=replace,path=/restApiId,value=apiV2123 \
        op=replace,path=/stage,value=prod
Output: { "basePath": "v2", "restApiId": "apiV2123", "stage": "prod" }
Django Context: # Update both # Author.objects v2 migration # Complete update
Example 5: Staging to production

Promote staging to production.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway update-base-path-mapping \
    --domain-name api.anihpj.com \
    --base-path jobposts \
    --patch-operations op=replace,path=/stage,value=prod

echo "Promoted to production"
Output: { "basePath": "jobposts", "stage": "prod" } Promoted to production
Django Context: # Stage promotion # Location.objects live # Production deployment

update-client-certificate

Updates an existing client certificate. Modifies certificate description and metadata.

Syntax
aws apigateway update-client-certificate \
    --client-certificate-id <value> \
    --patch-operations <value>
Parameters
  • --client-certificate-id (string): Certificate identifier
  • --patch-operations (list): Patch operations
Return Value
  • clientCertificateId (string): Certificate ID
  • description (string): Updated description
  • pemEncodedCertificate (string): Certificate content
Example 1: Update description

Change certificate description.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway update-client-certificate \
    --client-certificate-id cert123abc \
    --patch-operations op=replace,path=/description,value="Production backend certificate for JobPost API"
Output: { "clientCertificateId": "cert123abc", "description": "Production backend certificate for JobPost API", "expirationDate": "2026-01-17T00:00:00Z" }
Django Context: # Update description # JobPost.objects cert # Certificate metadata
Example 2: Add environment info

Update description with environment.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway update-client-certificate \
    --client-certificate-id cert123abc \
    --patch-operations op=replace,path=/description,value="[PROD] Django backend mTLS cert"
Output: { "clientCertificateId": "cert123abc", "description": "[PROD] Django backend mTLS cert" }
Django Context: # Environment tag # Skills.objects production # Environment labeling
Example 3: Clear description

Remove certificate description.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway update-client-certificate \
    --client-certificate-id cert123abc \
    --patch-operations op=replace,path=/description,value=""

echo "Description cleared"
Output: { "clientCertificateId": "cert123abc", "description": "" } Description cleared
Django Context: # Clear description # User.objects cleanup # Metadata removal
Example 4: Update with timestamp

Add last updated info to description.

#!/bin/bash
TIMESTAMP=$(date -I)
aws apigateway update-client-certificate \
    --client-certificate-id cert123abc \
    --patch-operations "op=replace,path=/description,value=Updated: $TIMESTAMP"
Output: { "clientCertificateId": "cert123abc", "description": "Updated: 2025-01-17" }
Django Context: # Timestamp update # Author.objects date # Audit trail
Example 5: Add contact info

Include owner contact in description.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway update-client-certificate \
    --client-certificate-id cert123abc \
    --patch-operations op=replace,path=/description,value="Owner: backend-team@anihpj.com | Purpose: mTLS"
Output: { "clientCertificateId": "cert123abc", "description": "Owner: backend-team@anihpj.com | Purpose: mTLS" }
Django Context: # Contact info # Location.objects owner # Documentation

update-deployment

Updates an existing deployment. Modifies deployment description metadata.

Syntax
aws apigateway update-deployment \
    --rest-api-id <value> \
    --deployment-id <value> \
    --patch-operations <value>
Parameters
  • --rest-api-id (string): REST API identifier
  • --deployment-id (string): Deployment identifier
  • --patch-operations (list): Patch operations
Return Value
  • id (string): Deployment ID
  • description (string): Updated description
  • createdDate (timestamp): Creation date
Example 1: Update deployment description

Add release notes to deployment.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway update-deployment \
    --rest-api-id abc123def4 \
    --deployment-id dep123abc \
    --patch-operations op=replace,path=/description,value="Release v2.1.0 - Added Skills filtering"
Output: { "id": "dep123abc", "description": "Release v2.1.0 - Added Skills filtering", "createdDate": "2025-01-17T10:00:00Z" }
Django Context: # Update description # JobPost.objects release # Release notes
Example 2: Add version tag

Tag deployment with version.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway update-deployment \
    --rest-api-id abc123def4 \
    --deployment-id dep123abc \
    --patch-operations op=replace,path=/description,value="v2.1.0"
Output: { "id": "dep123abc", "description": "v2.1.0" }
Django Context: # Version tag # Skills.objects version # Deployment labeling
Example 3: Add timestamp

Include deployment timestamp.

#!/bin/bash
TIMESTAMP=$(date -Iseconds)
aws apigateway update-deployment \
    --rest-api-id abc123def4 \
    --deployment-id dep123abc \
    --patch-operations "op=replace,path=/description,value=Deployed: $TIMESTAMP"
Output: { "id": "dep123abc", "description": "Deployed: 2025-01-17T10:30:00+00:00" }
Django Context: # Timestamp # User.objects deploy time # Audit trail
Example 4: Add rollback marker

Mark deployment as rollback target.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway update-deployment \
    --rest-api-id abc123def4 \
    --deployment-id dep789stable \
    --patch-operations op=replace,path=/description,value="[STABLE] Last known good - safe for rollback"
Output: { "id": "dep789stable", "description": "[STABLE] Last known good - safe for rollback" }
Django Context: # Rollback marker # Author.objects stable # Recovery point
Example 5: Clear description

Remove deployment description.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway update-deployment \
    --rest-api-id abc123def4 \
    --deployment-id dep123abc \
    --patch-operations op=replace,path=/description,value=""

echo "Deployment description cleared"
Output: { "id": "dep123abc", "description": "" } Deployment description cleared
Django Context: # Clear description # Location.objects cleanup # Metadata removal

update-documentation-part

Updates an existing documentation part. Modifies API documentation content and properties.

Syntax
aws apigateway update-documentation-part \
    --rest-api-id <value> \
    --documentation-part-id <value> \
    --patch-operations <value>
Parameters
  • --rest-api-id (string): REST API identifier
  • --documentation-part-id (string): Documentation part ID
  • --patch-operations (list): Patch operations
Return Value
  • id (string): Documentation part ID
  • location (object): Location specifier
  • properties (string): Updated documentation content
Example 1: Update API description

Change API-level documentation.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway update-documentation-part \
    --rest-api-id abc123def4 \
    --documentation-part-id doc123api \
    --patch-operations 'op=replace,path=/properties,value="{\"description\": \"JobPost Management API v2.0\"}"'
Output: { "id": "doc123api", "location": {"type": "API"}, "properties": "{\"description\": \"JobPost Management API v2.0\"}" }
Django Context: # Update API docs # JobPost.objects description # API-level documentation
Example 2: Update resource docs

Change resource documentation.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway update-documentation-part \
    --rest-api-id abc123def4 \
    --documentation-part-id doc456res \
    --patch-operations 'op=replace,path=/properties,value="{\"description\": \"JobPost collection with Skills filtering\"}"'
Output: { "id": "doc456res", "location": {"type": "RESOURCE", "path": "/jobposts"}, "properties": "{\"description\": \"JobPost collection with Skills filtering\"}" }
Django Context: # Resource docs # Skills.objects endpoint # Path documentation
Example 3: Update method docs

Change method documentation.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway update-documentation-part \
    --rest-api-id abc123def4 \
    --documentation-part-id doc789method \
    --patch-operations 'op=replace,path=/properties,value="{\"summary\": \"Create new JobPost\", \"description\": \"Creates a JobPost with required Skills\"}"'
Output: { "id": "doc789method", "location": {"type": "METHOD", "path": "/jobposts", "method": "POST"}, "properties": "{\"summary\": \"Create new JobPost\"}" }
Django Context: # Method docs # User.objects POST # Operation documentation
Example 4: Update parameter docs

Change query parameter documentation.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway update-documentation-part \
    --rest-api-id abc123def4 \
    --documentation-part-id doc101param \
    --patch-operations 'op=replace,path=/properties,value="{\"description\": \"Filter by skill name (e.g., Python, Django)\"}"'
Output: { "id": "doc101param", "location": {"type": "QUERY_PARAMETER", "name": "skill"}, "properties": "{\"description\": \"Filter by skill name\"}" }
Django Context: # Parameter docs # Author.objects query # Query parameter help
Example 5: Update model docs

Change model documentation.

    --rest-api-id abc123def4 \
    --documentation-part-id doc202model \
    --patch-operations 'op=replace,path=/properties,value="{\"description\": \"JobPost model representing a job listing\"}"'
Output: { "id": "doc202model", "location": {"type": "MODEL", "name": "JobPost"} }

    --rest-api-id abc123def4 \

    --documentation-version 2.0.0 \

Tag version as deprecated.

update-domain-name

Updates a custom domain name configuration. Modifies certificate, security policy, and other settings.

Syntax
aws apigateway update-domain-name \
    --domain-name <value> \
    --patch-operations <value>
Parameters
  • --domain-name (string): Custom domain name
  • --patch-operations (list): Patch operations
Return Value
  • domainName (string): Domain name
  • certificateArn (string): ACM certificate ARN
  • securityPolicy (string): TLS version policy
Example 1: Update certificate

Change ACM certificate for domain.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway update-domain-name \
    --domain-name api.anihpj.com \
    --patch-operations op=replace,path=/certificateArn,value=arn:aws:acm:us-east-1:123456789012:certificate/new-cert-id
Output: { "domainName": "api.anihpj.com", "certificateArn": "arn:aws:acm:us-east-1:123456789012:certificate/new-cert-id", "distributionDomainName": "d123abc.cloudfront.net" }
Django Context: # Update certificate # JobPost.objects SSL # Certificate rotation
Example 2: Update security policy

Upgrade TLS version policy.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway update-domain-name \
    --domain-name api.anihpj.com \
    --patch-operations op=replace,path=/securityPolicy,value=TLS_1_2
Output: { "domainName": "api.anihpj.com", "securityPolicy": "TLS_1_2" }
Django Context: # Security policy # Skills.objects TLS # Security upgrade
Example 3: Update regional certificate

Change regional endpoint certificate.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway update-domain-name \
    --domain-name api.anihpj.com \
    --patch-operations op=replace,path=/regionalCertificateArn,value=arn:aws:acm:us-east-1:123456789012:certificate/regional-cert
Output: { "domainName": "api.anihpj.com", "regionalCertificateArn": "arn:aws:acm:us-east-1:123456789012:certificate/regional-cert" }
Django Context: # Regional cert # User.objects endpoint # Regional SSL
Example 4: Update mutual TLS

Configure mTLS settings.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway update-domain-name \
    --domain-name api.anihpj.com \
    --patch-operations op=replace,path=/mutualTlsAuthentication/truststoreUri,value=s3://anihpj-certs/truststore.pem
Output: { "domainName": "api.anihpj.com", "mutualTlsAuthentication": { "truststoreUri": "s3://anihpj-certs/truststore.pem" } }
Django Context: # mTLS update # Author.objects auth # Client certificates
Example 5: Enable ownership verification

Update domain ownership settings.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway update-domain-name \
    --domain-name api.anihpj.com \
    --patch-operations op=replace,path=/ownershipVerificationCertificateArn,value=arn:aws:acm:us-east-1:123456789012:certificate/ownership-cert
Output: { "domainName": "api.anihpj.com", "ownershipVerificationCertificateArn": "arn:aws:acm:us-east-1:123456789012:certificate/ownership-cert" }
Django Context: # Ownership verification # Location.objects domain # Domain validation

update-gateway-response

Updates a gateway response configuration. Modifies error response templates and headers.

Syntax
aws apigateway update-gateway-response \
    --rest-api-id <value> \
    --response-type <value> \
    --patch-operations <value>
Parameters
  • --rest-api-id (string): REST API identifier
  • --response-type (string): Response type to update
  • --patch-operations (list): Patch operations
Return Value
  • responseType (string): Response type
  • statusCode (string): Status code
  • responseTemplates (object): Updated templates
Example 1: Update 401 message

Change unauthorized error message.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway update-gateway-response \
    --rest-api-id abc123def4 \
    --response-type UNAUTHORIZED \
    --patch-operations 'op=replace,path=/responseTemplates/application~1json,value="{\"error\": \"Authentication required for JobPost API\"}"'
Output: { "responseType": "UNAUTHORIZED", "statusCode": "401", "responseTemplates": { "application/json": "{\"error\": \"Authentication required for JobPost API\"}" } }
Django Context: # Update 401 # JobPost.objects auth error # Error message update
Example 2: Add CORS header

Add CORS header to error response.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway update-gateway-response \
    --rest-api-id abc123def4 \
    --response-type DEFAULT_4XX \
    --patch-operations "op=add,path=/responseParameters/gatewayresponse.header.Access-Control-Allow-Origin,value='*'"
Output: { "responseType": "DEFAULT_4XX", "responseParameters": { "gatewayresponse.header.Access-Control-Allow-Origin": "'*'" } }
Django Context: # Add CORS # Skills.objects cross-origin # Error CORS headers
Example 3: Update rate limit message

Change quota exceeded message.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway update-gateway-response \
    --rest-api-id abc123def4 \
    --response-type QUOTA_EXCEEDED \
    --patch-operations 'op=replace,path=/responseTemplates/application~1json,value="{\"error\": \"Rate limit exceeded\", \"retryAfter\": 60}"'
Output: { "responseType": "QUOTA_EXCEEDED", "statusCode": "429" }
Django Context: # Rate limit message # User.objects throttle # Retry guidance
Example 4: Update status code

Change response status code.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway update-gateway-response \
    --rest-api-id abc123def4 \
    --response-type BAD_REQUEST_BODY \
    --patch-operations op=replace,path=/statusCode,value=422
Output: { "responseType": "BAD_REQUEST_BODY", "statusCode": "422" }
Django Context: # Status code update # Author.objects validation # HTTP code change
Example 5: Remove header

Remove response header.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway update-gateway-response \
    --rest-api-id abc123def4 \
    --response-type DEFAULT_5XX \
    --patch-operations op=remove,path=/responseParameters/gatewayresponse.header.X-Debug
Output: { "responseType": "DEFAULT_5XX", "responseParameters": {} }
Django Context: # Remove header # Location.objects cleanup # Header removal

update-integration

Updates an integration configuration. Modifies backend connection settings and request mappings.

Syntax
aws apigateway update-integration \
    --rest-api-id <value> \
    --resource-id <value> \
    --http-method <value> \
    --patch-operations <value>
Parameters
  • --rest-api-id (string): REST API identifier
  • --resource-id (string): Resource identifier
  • --http-method (string): HTTP method
  • --patch-operations (list): Patch operations
Return Value
  • type (string): Integration type
  • uri (string): Backend URI
  • httpMethod (string): Backend method
Example 1: Update Lambda function

Change Lambda backend.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway update-integration \
    --rest-api-id abc123def4 \
    --resource-id res123abc \
    --http-method GET \
    --patch-operations op=replace,path=/uri,value="arn:aws:apigateway:us-east-1:lambda:path/2015-03-31/functions/arn:aws:lambda:us-east-1:123456789012:function:new-get-jobposts/invocations"
Output: { "type": "AWS_PROXY", "uri": "arn:aws:apigateway:us-east-1:lambda:path/..." }
Django Context: # Update Lambda # JobPost.objects new function # Backend migration
Example 2: Update timeout

Change integration timeout.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway update-integration \
    --rest-api-id abc123def4 \
    --resource-id res123abc \
    --http-method GET \
    --patch-operations op=replace,path=/timeoutInMillis,value=15000
Output: { "type": "AWS_PROXY", "timeoutInMillis": 15000 }
Django Context: # Update timeout # Skills.objects longer wait # Performance tuning
Example 3: Update HTTP endpoint

Change HTTP backend URL.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway update-integration \
    --rest-api-id abc123def4 \
    --resource-id res123abc \
    --http-method GET \
    --patch-operations op=replace,path=/uri,value="https://api-v2.anihpj.com/jobposts"
Output: { "type": "HTTP_PROXY", "uri": "https://api-v2.anihpj.com/jobposts" }
Django Context: # HTTP endpoint # User.objects new backend # URL update
Example 4: Add request parameter

Add parameter mapping.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway update-integration \
    --rest-api-id abc123def4 \
    --resource-id res123abc \
    --http-method GET \
    --patch-operations "op=add,path=/requestParameters/integration.request.querystring.api_version,value='method.request.header.X-API-Version'"
Output: { "requestParameters": { "integration.request.querystring.api_version": "method.request.header.X-API-Version" } }
Django Context: # Parameter mapping # Author.objects headers # Request transformation
Example 5: Update VPC link

Change VPC link connection.

    --rest-api-id abc123def4 \
    --resource-id res123abc \
    --http-method GET \

Output: { "connectionType": "VPC_LINK", "connectionId": "vpcl456new" }

update-integration-response

Updates an integration response. Modifies response mappings and transformations.

Syntax
aws apigateway update-integration-response \
    --rest-api-id <value> \
    --resource-id <value> \
    --http-method <value> \
    --status-code <value> \
    --patch-operations <value>
Parameters
  • --rest-api-id (string): REST API identifier
  • --resource-id (string): Resource identifier
  • --http-method (string): HTTP method
  • --status-code (string): Status code
  • --patch-operations (list): Patch operations
Return Value
  • statusCode (string): Status code
  • responseTemplates (object): Updated templates
  • responseParameters (object): Updated headers
Example 1: Update response template

Change response body transformation.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway update-integration-response \
    --rest-api-id abc123def4 \
    --resource-id res123abc \
    --http-method GET \
    --status-code 200 \
    --patch-operations 'op=replace,path=/responseTemplates/application~1json,value="{\"jobs\": $input.json('\''$.Items'\'')}"'
Output: { "statusCode": "200", "responseTemplates": { "application/json": "{\"jobs\": $input.json('$.Items')}" } }
Django Context: # Response template # JobPost.objects transform # Body mapping
Example 2: Add header mapping

Add response header.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway update-integration-response \
    --rest-api-id abc123def4 \
    --resource-id res123abc \
    --http-method GET \
    --status-code 200 \
    --patch-operations "op=add,path=/responseParameters/method.response.header.X-Total-Count,value='integration.response.header.X-Total-Count'"
Output: { "statusCode": "200", "responseParameters": { "method.response.header.X-Total-Count": "integration.response.header.X-Total-Count" } }
Django Context: # Header mapping # Skills.objects count # Pagination header
Example 3: Update selection pattern

Change error pattern matching.

aws apigateway update-integration-response \
    --rest-api-id abc123def4 \
    --resource-id res123abc \
    --http-method GET \
    --status-code 404 \
    --patch-operations op=replace,path=/selectionPattern,value=".*NotFound.*|.*DoesNotExist.*"
Output: { "statusCode": "404", "selectionPattern": ".*NotFound.*|.*DoesNotExist.*" }
Django Context: # Selection pattern # User.objects error match # Django exception handling

Remove mapped header.

#!/bin/bash

    --rest-api-id abc123def4 \
    --resource-id res123abc \
    --http-method GET \
    --status-code 200 \
    --patch-operations op=remove,path=/responseParameters/method.response.header.X-Debug
Output: { "statusCode": "200" }

Set empty pass-through template.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway update-integration-response \
    --rest-api-id abc123def4 \
    --resource-id res123abc \
    --http-method GET \

Updates an API method configuration. Modifies authorization, parameters, and request settings.

Syntax
aws apigateway update-method \
    --rest-api-id <value> \
    --resource-id <value> \
    --http-method <value> \
    --patch-operations <value>
Parameters
  • --rest-api-id (string): REST API identifier
  • --resource-id (string): Resource identifier
  • --http-method (string): HTTP method
  • --patch-operations (list): Patch operations
Return Value
  • httpMethod (string): HTTP method
  • authorizationType (string): Updated auth type
  • apiKeyRequired (boolean): API key setting

Require API key for method.

aws apigateway update-method \
    --rest-api-id abc123def4 \
    --resource-id res123abc \
    --http-method GET \
    --patch-operations op=replace,path=/apiKeyRequired,value=true
Output: { "httpMethod": "GET", "authorizationType": "NONE", "apiKeyRequired": true }
Django Context: # Require API key # JobPost.objects auth # Access control

    --rest-api-id abc123def4 \
    --resource-id res123abc \
    --http-method POST \
    --patch-operations op=replace,path=/authorizationType,value=AWS_IAM
Output: { "httpMethod": "POST", "authorizationType": "AWS_IAM" }
Django Context: # IAM authorization # Skills.objects secure # Auth upgrade

    --resource-id res123abc \
    --http-method POST \
    --patch-operations op=replace,path=/requestValidatorId,value=rv123abc
Output: { "httpMethod": "POST", "requestValidatorId": "rv123abc" }

    --rest-api-id abc123def4 \
    --resource-id res123abc \
    --http-method GET \

        op=replace,path=/authorizationType,value=CUSTOM \

    --http-method GET \
    --status-code 200 \
    --patch-operations op=add,path=/responseParameters/method.response.header.Access-Control-Allow-Origin,value=false
Output: { "statusCode": "200", "responseParameters": { "method.response.header.Access-Control-Allow-Origin": false } }
Django Context: # CORS header # User.objects cross-origin # Browser access
Example 4: Remove header

Remove response header definition.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway update-method-response \
    --rest-api-id abc123def4 \
    --resource-id res123abc \
    --http-method GET \
    --status-code 200 \
    --patch-operations op=remove,path=/responseParameters/method.response.header.X-Debug
Output: { "statusCode": "200" }
Django Context: # Remove header # Author.objects cleanup # Production cleanup
Example 5: Clear model

Remove response model.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway update-method-response \
    --rest-api-id abc123def4 \
    --resource-id res123abc \
    --http-method GET \
    --status-code 200 \
    --patch-operations op=remove,path=/responseModels/application~1json
Output: { "statusCode": "200", "responseModels": {} }
Django Context: # Clear model # Location.objects flexible # Schema removal

update-model

Updates a model definition. Modifies schema and description.

Syntax
aws apigateway update-model \
    --rest-api-id <value> \
    --model-name <value> \
    --patch-operations <value>
Parameters
  • --rest-api-id (string): REST API identifier
  • --model-name (string): Model name
  • --patch-operations (list): Patch operations
Return Value
  • id (string): Model identifier
  • name (string): Model name
  • schema (string): Updated schema
  • description (string): Updated description
Example 1: Update model schema

Change model JSON schema.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway update-model \
    --rest-api-id abc123def4 \
    --model-name JobPost \
    --patch-operations 'op=replace,path=/schema,value="{\"type\":\"object\",\"properties\":{\"title\":{\"type\":\"string\"},\"skills\":{\"type\":\"array\"}}}"'
Output: { "id": "model123", "name": "JobPost", "schema": "{\"type\":\"object\",\"properties\":{\"title\":{\"type\":\"string\"},\"skills\":{\"type\":\"array\"}}}" }
Django Context: # Update schema # JobPost.objects model # Schema evolution
Example 2: Update description

Change model description.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway update-model \
    --rest-api-id abc123def4 \
    --model-name JobPost \
    --patch-operations op=replace,path=/description,value="JobPost model with Skills array support"
Output: { "id": "model123", "name": "JobPost", "description": "JobPost model with Skills array support" }
Django Context: # Update description # Skills.objects docs # Documentation update
Example 3: Add required field

Update schema with required fields.

#!/bin/bash
SCHEMA='{"type":"object","required":["title","skills"],"properties":{"title":{"type":"string"},"skills":{"type":"array"}}}'

aws apigateway update-model \
    --rest-api-id abc123def4 \
    --model-name JobPost \
    --patch-operations "op=replace,path=/schema,value='$SCHEMA'"
Output: { "id": "model123", "name": "JobPost" }
Django Context: # Required fields # User.objects validation # Schema constraints
Example 4: Update content type

Change model content type.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway update-model \
    --rest-api-id abc123def4 \
    --model-name JobPost \
    --patch-operations op=replace,path=/contentType,value=application/json
Output: { "id": "model123", "name": "JobPost", "contentType": "application/json" }
Django Context: # Content type # Author.objects format # Media type
Example 5: Clear description

Remove model description.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway update-model \
    --rest-api-id abc123def4 \
    --model-name JobPost \
    --patch-operations op=replace,path=/description,value=""

echo "Model description cleared"
Output: { "id": "model123", "name": "JobPost", "description": "" } Model description cleared
Django Context: # Clear description # Location.objects cleanup # Metadata removal

update-request-validator

Updates a request validator configuration. Modifies validation settings for request body and parameters.

Syntax
aws apigateway update-request-validator \
    --rest-api-id <value> \
    --request-validator-id <value> \
    --patch-operations <value>
Parameters
  • --rest-api-id (string): REST API identifier
  • --request-validator-id (string): Validator identifier
  • --patch-operations (list): Patch operations
Return Value
  • id (string): Validator identifier
  • name (string): Validator name
  • validateRequestBody (boolean): Body validation
  • validateRequestParameters (boolean): Parameter validation
Example 1: Enable body validation

Turn on request body validation.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway update-request-validator \
    --rest-api-id abc123def4 \
    --request-validator-id rv123abc \
    --patch-operations op=replace,path=/validateRequestBody,value=true
Output: { "id": "rv123abc", "name": "jobpost-validator", "validateRequestBody": true, "validateRequestParameters": false }
Django Context: # Enable body validation # JobPost.objects schema check # Request validation
Example 2: Enable parameter validation

Turn on parameter validation.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway update-request-validator \
    --rest-api-id abc123def4 \
    --request-validator-id rv123abc \
    --patch-operations op=replace,path=/validateRequestParameters,value=true
Output: { "id": "rv123abc", "validateRequestParameters": true }
Django Context: # Parameter validation # Skills.objects query # Input checking
Example 3: Enable full validation

Enable both body and parameter validation.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway update-request-validator \
    --rest-api-id abc123def4 \
    --request-validator-id rv123abc \
    --patch-operations \
        op=replace,path=/validateRequestBody,value=true \
        op=replace,path=/validateRequestParameters,value=true
Output: { "id": "rv123abc", "validateRequestBody": true, "validateRequestParameters": true }
Django Context: # Full validation # User.objects complete # Comprehensive check
Example 4: Rename validator

Change validator name.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway update-request-validator \
    --rest-api-id abc123def4 \
    --request-validator-id rv123abc \
    --patch-operations op=replace,path=/name,value=jobpost-full-validator
Output: { "id": "rv123abc", "name": "jobpost-full-validator" }
Django Context: # Rename validator # Author.objects naming # Documentation
Example 5: Disable validation

Turn off all validation.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway update-request-validator \
    --rest-api-id abc123def4 \
    --request-validator-id rv123abc \
    --patch-operations \
        op=replace,path=/validateRequestBody,value=false \
        op=replace,path=/validateRequestParameters,value=false

echo "Validation disabled"
Output: { "id": "rv123abc", "validateRequestBody": false, "validateRequestParameters": false } Validation disabled
Django Context: # Disable validation # Location.objects pass-through # Development mode

update-resource

Updates a resource configuration. Modifies resource path and parent relationships.

Syntax
aws apigateway update-resource \
    --rest-api-id <value> \
    --resource-id <value> \
    --patch-operations <value>
Parameters
  • --rest-api-id (string): REST API identifier
  • --resource-id (string): Resource identifier
  • --patch-operations (list): Patch operations
Return Value
  • id (string): Resource identifier
  • parentId (string): Parent resource ID
  • pathPart (string): Path segment
  • path (string): Full path
Example 1: Update path part

Change resource path segment.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway update-resource \
    --rest-api-id abc123def4 \
    --resource-id res123abc \
    --patch-operations op=replace,path=/pathPart,value=jobs
Output: { "id": "res123abc", "parentId": "rootres", "pathPart": "jobs", "path": "/jobs" }
Django Context: # Update path # JobPost.objects endpoint # URL change
Example 2: Rename to v2 path

Update resource for API versioning.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway update-resource \
    --rest-api-id abc123def4 \
    --resource-id res123abc \
    --patch-operations op=replace,path=/pathPart,value=v2-jobposts
Output: { "id": "res123abc", "pathPart": "v2-jobposts", "path": "/v2-jobposts" }
Django Context: # Version path # Skills.objects v2 # API versioning
Example 3: Update parameter path

Change path parameter name.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway update-resource \
    --rest-api-id abc123def4 \
    --resource-id res789id \
    --patch-operations op=replace,path=/pathPart,value='{jobId}'
Output: { "id": "res789id", "pathPart": "{jobId}", "path": "/jobposts/{jobId}" }
Django Context: # Parameter rename # User.objects.get(pk=jobId) # Path variable
Example 4: Move resource

Change parent resource.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway update-resource \
    --rest-api-id abc123def4 \
    --resource-id res456skills \
    --patch-operations op=replace,path=/parentId,value=resV2
Output: { "id": "res456skills", "parentId": "resV2", "path": "/v2/skills" }
Django Context: # Move resource # Author.objects reorganize # API restructure
Example 5: Verify update

Update and verify new path.

#!/bin/bash
RESULT=$(aws apigateway update-resource \
    --rest-api-id abc123def4 \
    --resource-id res123abc \
    --patch-operations op=replace,path=/pathPart,value=listings)

echo "New path: $(echo $RESULT | jq -r '.path')"
Output: New path: /listings
Django Context: # Verify path # Location.objects endpoint # URL confirmation

update-rest-api

Updates a REST API configuration. Modifies name, description, and API settings.

Syntax
aws apigateway update-rest-api \
    --rest-api-id <value> \
    --patch-operations <value>
Parameters
  • --rest-api-id (string): REST API identifier
  • --patch-operations (list): Patch operations
Return Value
  • id (string): API identifier
  • name (string): Updated name
  • description (string): Updated description
Example 1: Update API name

Rename the REST API.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway update-rest-api \
    --rest-api-id abc123def4 \
    --patch-operations op=replace,path=/name,value=anihpj-jobpost-api-v2
Output: { "id": "abc123def4", "name": "anihpj-jobpost-api-v2" }
Django Context: # Rename API # JobPost.objects new name # API evolution
Example 2: Update description

Change API description.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway update-rest-api \
    --rest-api-id abc123def4 \
    --patch-operations op=replace,path=/description,value="JobPost Management API v2.0 with Skills filtering"
Output: { "id": "abc123def4", "description": "JobPost Management API v2.0 with Skills filtering" }
Django Context: # Update description # Skills.objects docs # API documentation
Example 3: Enable binary media

Add binary media type support.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway update-rest-api \
    --rest-api-id abc123def4 \
    --patch-operations op=add,path=/binaryMediaTypes/-,value="image/png"
Output: { "id": "abc123def4", "binaryMediaTypes": ["image/png"] }
Django Context: # Binary media # User.objects images # File upload support
Example 4: Update API key source

Change where API key is read from.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway update-rest-api \
    --rest-api-id abc123def4 \
    --patch-operations op=replace,path=/apiKeySource,value=AUTHORIZER
Output: { "id": "abc123def4", "apiKeySource": "AUTHORIZER" }
Django Context: # API key source # Author.objects auth # Key configuration
Example 5: Disable execute-api endpoint

Disable default API endpoint.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway update-rest-api \
    --rest-api-id abc123def4 \
    --patch-operations op=replace,path=/disableExecuteApiEndpoint,value=true
Output: { "id": "abc123def4", "disableExecuteApiEndpoint": true }
Django Context: # Disable default # Location.objects custom domain # Security hardening

update-stage

Updates a deployment stage configuration. Modifies caching, throttling, and stage variables.

Syntax
aws apigateway update-stage \
    --rest-api-id <value> \
    --stage-name <value> \
    --patch-operations <value>
Parameters
  • --rest-api-id (string): REST API identifier
  • --stage-name (string): Stage name
  • --patch-operations (list): Patch operations
Return Value
  • stageName (string): Stage name
  • variables (object): Stage variables
  • cacheClusterEnabled (boolean): Cache status
Example 1: Enable caching

Turn on API caching.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway update-stage \
    --rest-api-id abc123def4 \
    --stage-name prod \
    --patch-operations op=replace,path=/cacheClusterEnabled,value=true
Output: { "stageName": "prod", "cacheClusterEnabled": true }
Django Context: # Enable cache # JobPost.objects cache # Performance boost
Example 2: Update stage variable

Change environment variable.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway update-stage \
    --rest-api-id abc123def4 \
    --stage-name prod \
    --patch-operations op=replace,path=/variables/dbHost,value=prod-db.anihpj.com
Output: { "stageName": "prod", "variables": { "dbHost": "prod-db.anihpj.com" } }
Django Context: # Stage variable # Skills.objects database # Environment config
Example 3: Update description

Change stage description.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway update-stage \
    --rest-api-id abc123def4 \
    --stage-name prod \
    --patch-operations op=replace,path=/description,value="Production environment v2.1.0"
Output: { "stageName": "prod", "description": "Production environment v2.1.0" }
Django Context: # Stage description # User.objects version # Environment labeling
Example 4: Update throttling

Change method throttling settings.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway update-stage \
    --rest-api-id abc123def4 \
    --stage-name prod \
    --patch-operations \
        'op=replace,path=/*/GET/throttling/rateLimit,value=1000' \
        'op=replace,path=/*/GET/throttling/burstLimit,value=500'
Output: { "stageName": "prod", "methodSettings": { "*/*": { "throttlingRateLimit": 1000, "throttlingBurstLimit": 500 } } }
Django Context: # Throttling update # Author.objects rate limit # Performance tuning
Example 5: Enable logging

Turn on CloudWatch logging.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway update-stage \
    --rest-api-id abc123def4 \
    --stage-name prod \
    --patch-operations \
        op=replace,path/*/*/logging/loglevel,value=INFO
Output: { "stageName": "prod" }
Django Context: # Enable logging # Location.objects monitoring # Observability

update-usage

Updates API usage data for a key. Grants additional quota to API consumers.

Syntax
aws apigateway update-usage \
    --usage-plan-id <value> \
    --key-id <value> \
    --patch-operations <value>
Parameters
  • --usage-plan-id (string): Usage plan identifier
  • --key-id (string): API key identifier
  • --patch-operations (list): Patch operations
Return Value
  • usagePlanId (string): Usage plan ID
  • items (object): Updated usage data
Example 1: Grant extra quota

Add extra requests to customer quota.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway update-usage \
    --usage-plan-id up123premium \
    --key-id key123abc \
    --patch-operations op=add,path=/remaining,value=5000
Output: { "usagePlanId": "up123premium", "items": { "key123abc": [[6500, 10000]] } }
Django Context: # Grant quota # JobPost.objects extra # Customer bonus
Example 2: Reduce remaining quota

Decrease available requests.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway update-usage \
    --usage-plan-id up123premium \
    --key-id key123abc \
    --patch-operations op=add,path=/remaining,value=-1000
Output: { "usagePlanId": "up123premium", "items": { "key123abc": [[5500, 10000]] } }
Django Context: # Reduce quota # Skills.objects penalty # Usage adjustment
Example 3: Reset to full quota

Restore full quota allowance.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway update-usage \
    --usage-plan-id up123premium \
    --key-id key123abc \
    --patch-operations op=replace,path=/remaining,value=10000
Output: { "usagePlanId": "up123premium", "items": { "key123abc": [[10000, 10000]] } }
Django Context: # Reset quota # User.objects restore # Full allowance
Example 4: Emergency quota boost

Double quota for special event.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway update-usage \
    --usage-plan-id up123premium \
    --key-id key123abc \
    --patch-operations op=add,path=/remaining,value=10000

echo "Emergency quota boost applied"
Output: { "usagePlanId": "up123premium", "items": { "key123abc": [[20000, 10000]] } } Emergency quota boost applied
Django Context: # Emergency boost # Author.objects event # Special allocation
Example 5: Verify quota update

Update and confirm new quota.

#!/bin/bash
RESULT=$(aws apigateway update-usage \
    --usage-plan-id up123premium \
    --key-id key123abc \
    --patch-operations op=add,path=/remaining,value=1000)

echo "Updated quota: $(echo $RESULT | jq '.items')"
Output: Updated quota: {"key123abc": [[7500, 10000]]}
Django Context: # Verify update # Location.objects confirm # Quota validation

update-usage-plan

Updates a usage plan configuration. Modifies quota limits, throttling, and API associations.

Syntax
aws apigateway update-usage-plan \
    --usage-plan-id <value> \
    --patch-operations <value>
Parameters
  • --usage-plan-id (string): Usage plan identifier
  • --patch-operations (list): Patch operations
Return Value
  • id (string): Usage plan ID
  • name (string): Plan name
  • quota (object): Quota settings
  • throttle (object): Throttle settings
Example 1: Update quota limit

Increase monthly quota.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway update-usage-plan \
    --usage-plan-id up123premium \
    --patch-operations op=replace,path=/quota/limit,value=200000
Output: { "id": "up123premium", "quota": { "limit": 200000, "period": "MONTH" } }
Django Context: # Update quota # JobPost.objects limit # Plan upgrade
Example 2: Update throttle rate

Increase rate limit.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway update-usage-plan \
    --usage-plan-id up123premium \
    --patch-operations op=replace,path=/throttle/rateLimit,value=2000
Output: { "id": "up123premium", "throttle": { "rateLimit": 2000, "burstLimit": 4000 } }
Django Context: # Update rate # Skills.objects throttle # Performance upgrade
Example 3: Rename plan

Change plan name.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway update-usage-plan \
    --usage-plan-id up123premium \
    --patch-operations op=replace,path=/name,value=anihpj-enterprise-plan
Output: { "id": "up123premium", "name": "anihpj-enterprise-plan" }
Django Context: # Rename plan # User.objects tier # Plan branding
Example 4: Add API stage

Associate new API stage with plan.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway update-usage-plan \
    --usage-plan-id up123premium \
    --patch-operations 'op=add,path=/apiStages,value={"apiId":"xyz789","stage":"prod"}'
Output: { "id": "up123premium", "apiStages": [ {"apiId": "abc123", "stage": "prod"}, {"apiId": "xyz789", "stage": "prod"} ] }
Django Context: # Add API stage # Author.objects multi-API # Plan expansion
Example 5: Update description

Change plan description.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway update-usage-plan \
    --usage-plan-id up123premium \
    --patch-operations op=replace,path=/description,value="Enterprise tier with 200K requests/month"
Output: { "id": "up123premium", "description": "Enterprise tier with 200K requests/month" }
Django Context: # Update description # Location.objects docs # Plan documentation

update-vpc-link

Updates a VPC link configuration. Modifies name, description, and target settings.

Syntax
aws apigateway update-vpc-link \
    --vpc-link-id <value> \
    --patch-operations <value>
Parameters
  • --vpc-link-id (string): VPC link identifier
  • --patch-operations (list): Patch operations
Return Value
  • id (string): VPC link identifier
  • name (string): Updated name
  • description (string): Updated description
  • status (string): Link status
Example 1: Rename VPC link

Change VPC link name.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway update-vpc-link \
    --vpc-link-id vpcl123abc \
    --patch-operations op=replace,path=/name,value=anihpj-production-link
Output: { "id": "vpcl123abc", "name": "anihpj-production-link", "status": "AVAILABLE" }
Django Context: # Rename link # JobPost.objects connection # Network naming
Example 2: Update description

Change VPC link description.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway update-vpc-link \
    --vpc-link-id vpcl123abc \
    --patch-operations op=replace,path=/description,value="Production VPC link to Django backend"
Output: { "id": "vpcl123abc", "description": "Production VPC link to Django backend" }
Django Context: # Update description # Skills.objects docs # Documentation
Example 3: Add environment tag

Add environment to name.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway update-vpc-link \
    --vpc-link-id vpcl123abc \
    --patch-operations op=replace,path=/name,value="[PROD] anihpj-backend-link"
Output: { "id": "vpcl123abc", "name": "[PROD] anihpj-backend-link" }
Django Context: # Environment tag # User.objects production # Environment labeling
Example 4: Clear description

Remove VPC link description.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway update-vpc-link \
    --vpc-link-id vpcl123abc \
    --patch-operations op=replace,path=/description,value=""

echo "Description cleared"
Output: { "id": "vpcl123abc", "description": "" } Description cleared
Django Context: # Clear description # Author.objects cleanup # Metadata removal
Example 5: Full update

Update name and description together.

#!/bin/bash
aws apigateway update-vpc-link \
    --vpc-link-id vpcl123abc \
    --patch-operations \
        op=replace,path=/name,value=anihpj-v2-backend \
        op=replace,path=/description,value="V2 Django backend connection"
Output: { "id": "vpcl123abc", "name": "anihpj-v2-backend", "description": "V2 Django backend connection" }
Django Context: # Full update # Location.objects v2 # Complete metadata

AWS Step Functions

Orchestrate distributed applications and microservices with visual workflows

📋 Step Functions Overview

AWS Step Functions is a serverless orchestration service that lets you combine AWS Lambda functions and other AWS services to build business-critical applications. Use it to create complex jobpost workflows like multi-step job approval, skill matching pipelines, and batch processing.

Core Step Functions Operations

create-state-machine - Create workflow
list-state-machines - List workflows
start-execution - Start workflow run
describe-execution - Get execution status
stop-execution - Stop running workflow
list-executions - List workflow runs

create-activity

Creates an activity that can be used as a task in a state machine workflow.

Syntax
aws stepfunctions create-activity \
    --name <value> \
    [--tags <value>]
Parameters
  • --name (string): Name of the activity (required)
  • --tags (list): Tags to attach to the activity
Example 1: Create JobPost Review Activity

Create an activity for manual job post review tasks.

#!/bin/bash
# Create activity for human review of job posts
aws stepfunctions create-activity \
    --name "anihpj-jobpost-manual-review"
Output: { "activityArn": "arn:aws:states:us-east-1:123456789012:activity:anihpj-jobpost-manual-review", "creationDate": "2025-01-15T10:30:00Z" }
Django Context: # Activity for JobPost.objects.filter(status='pending_review') # Manual review step before publishing # Author.objects verification required
Example 2: Create Skills Verification Activity

Create activity for verifying job skills requirements.

#!/bin/bash
# Activity for skills verification process
aws stepfunctions create-activity \
    --name "anihpj-skills-verification" \
    --tags key=Environment,value=production key=App,value=anihpj
Output: { "activityArn": "arn:aws:states:us-east-1:123456789012:activity:anihpj-skills-verification", "creationDate": "2025-01-15T10:35:00Z" }
Django Context: # Activity validates Skills.objects linked to JobPost # Checks skill_name, proficiency_level fields # Related to JobPost.skills ManyToMany relationship
Example 3: Create Author Approval Activity

Create activity for author account approval workflow.

#!/bin/bash
# Activity for new author approvals
aws stepfunctions create-activity \
    --name "anihpj-author-approval" \
    --tags key=Team,value=hr key=Process,value=onboarding
Output: { "activityArn": "arn:aws:states:us-east-1:123456789012:activity:anihpj-author-approval", "creationDate": "2025-01-15T10:40:00Z" }
Django Context: # Approval activity for Author.objects.create() # Validates author credentials and company info # Links to User.objects for authentication
Example 4: Create Location Validation Activity

Create activity for validating job locations.

#!/bin/bash
# Activity for location data validation
aws stepfunctions create-activity \
    --name "anihpj-location-validation" \
    --tags key=DataQuality,value=high key=Service,value=geocoding
Output: { "activityArn": "arn:aws:states:us-east-1:123456789012:activity:anihpj-location-validation", "creationDate": "2025-01-15T10:45:00Z" }
Django Context: # Validates Location.objects address data # Checks city, state, country fields # Geocoding verification for JobPost.location FK
Example 5: Create Batch Processing Activity

Create activity for batch job post processing.

#!/bin/bash
# Activity for batch processing expired job posts
aws stepfunctions create-activity \
    --name "anihpj-batch-expiry-check" \
    --tags key=Type,value=batch key=Schedule,value=daily
Output: { "activityArn": "arn:aws:states:us-east-1:123456789012:activity:anihpj-batch-expiry-check", "creationDate": "2025-01-15T10:50:00Z" }
Django Context: # Batch check: JobPost.objects.filter(expiry_date__lt=now()) # Updates status field to 'expired' # Notifies Author.objects about expired listings

create-state-machine

Creates a new state machine (workflow) definition.

Syntax
aws stepfunctions create-state-machine \
    --name <value> \
    --definition <value> \
    --role-arn <value> \
    [--type STANDARD|EXPRESS]
Parameters
  • --name (string): Name of the state machine
  • --definition (string): ASL definition in JSON
  • --role-arn (string): IAM role for execution
  • --type (string): STANDARD or EXPRESS workflow
Example 1: Create JobPost Approval Workflow

Create multi-step workflow for job post approval process.

#!/bin/bash
cat > jobpost-approval.json << 'EOF'
{
  "Comment": "JobPost approval workflow for anihpj Django app",
  "StartAt": "ValidateJobPost",
  "States": {
    "ValidateJobPost": {
      "Type": "Task",
      "Resource": "arn:aws:lambda:us-east-1:123456789012:function:validate-jobpost",
      "Next": "CheckSkillsMatch"
    },
    "CheckSkillsMatch": {
      "Type": "Task",
      "Resource": "arn:aws:lambda:us-east-1:123456789012:function:check-skills",
      "Next": "ApprovalDecision"
    },
    "ApprovalDecision": {
      "Type": "Choice",
      "Choices": [
        {
          "Variable": "$.approved",
          "BooleanEquals": true,
          "Next": "PublishJobPost"
        }
      ],
      "Default": "SendRejectionNotification"
    },
    "PublishJobPost": {
      "Type": "Task",
      "Resource": "arn:aws:lambda:us-east-1:123456789012:function:publish-jobpost",
      "End": true
    },
    "SendRejectionNotification": {
      "Type": "Task",
      "Resource": "arn:aws:lambda:us-east-1:123456789012:function:send-rejection",
      "End": true
    }
  }
}
EOF

aws stepfunctions create-state-machine \
    --name "anihpj-jobpost-approval" \
    --definition file://jobpost-approval.json \
    --role-arn arn:aws:iam::123456789012:role/stepfunctions-role \
    --type STANDARD
Output: { "stateMachineArn": "arn:aws:states:us-east-1:123456789012:stateMachine:anihpj-jobpost-approval", "creationDate": "2025-01-11T17:00:00Z" }
Django Context: # Workflow for JobPost.objects.create() approval: # 1. ValidateJobPost: Check required fields # 2. CheckSkillsMatch: Verify Skills.objects exist # 3. ApprovalDecision: Auto or manual approval # 4. PublishJobPost: JobPost.status = 'published' # 5. Or SendRejection: Notify Author.user
Example 2: Create Skill Matching Pipeline

Process skill matching for job recommendations.

#!/bin/bash
cat > skill-matching.json << 'EOF'
{
  "StartAt": "FetchUserSkills",
  "States": {
    "FetchUserSkills": {
      "Type": "Task",
      "Resource": "arn:aws:lambda:us-east-1:123456789012:function:fetch-user-skills",
      "Next": "FetchJobRequirements"
    },
    "FetchJobRequirements": {
      "Type": "Task",
      "Resource": "arn:aws:lambda:us-east-1:123456789012:function:fetch-job-requirements",
      "Next": "CalculateMatch"
    },
    "CalculateMatch": {
      "Type": "Task",
      "Resource": "arn:aws:lambda:us-east-1:123456789012:function:calculate-match",
      "End": true
    }
  }
}
EOF

aws stepfunctions create-state-machine \
    --name "anihpj-skill-matching" \
    --definition file://skill-matching.json \
    --role-arn arn:aws:iam::123456789012:role/stepfunctions-role
Output: { "stateMachineArn": "arn:aws:states:us-east-1:123456789012:stateMachine:anihpj-skill-matching" }
Django Context: # Skill matching workflow: # 1. User.skills.all() → fetch user's Skills # 2. JobPost.required_skills.all() → get job requirements # 3. Calculate percentage match between user and job
Example 3: Create Express workflow for real-time

High-volume, short-duration workflow.

#!/bin/bash
aws stepfunctions create-state-machine \
    --name "anihpj-quick-validate" \
    --definition '{"StartAt":"Validate","States":{"Validate":{"Type":"Task","Resource":"arn:aws:lambda:us-east-1:123456789012:function:quick-validate","End":true}}}' \
    --role-arn arn:aws:iam::123456789012:role/stepfunctions-role \
    --type EXPRESS
Output: { "stateMachineArn": "arn:aws:states:us-east-1:123456789012:stateMachine:anihpj-quick-validate", "type": "EXPRESS" }
Django Context: # Express workflow for fast JobPost validation # Sub-second response for Skills.objects.exists() # Real-time Location.objects.get() checks

create-state-machine-alias

Creates an alias for a state machine that points to one or two versions.

Syntax
aws stepfunctions create-state-machine-alias \
    --name <value> \
    --routing-configuration <value> \
    [--description <value>]
Parameters
  • --name (string): Name for the alias (required)
  • --routing-configuration (list): Version routing weights (required)
  • --description (string): Description of the alias
Example 1: Create Production Alias

Create alias pointing to stable production version.

#!/bin/bash
# Create production alias for jobpost workflow
aws stepfunctions create-state-machine-alias \
    --name "arn:aws:states:us-east-1:123456789012:stateMachine:anihpj-jobpost-workflow#prod" \
    --routing-configuration '[{"stateMachineVersionArn":"arn:aws:states:us-east-1:123456789012:stateMachine:anihpj-jobpost-workflow:1","weight":100}]' \
    --description "Production alias for JobPost approval workflow"
Output: { "stateMachineAliasArn": "arn:aws:states:us-east-1:123456789012:stateMachine:anihpj-jobpost-workflow:prod", "creationDate": "2025-01-15T11:00:00Z" }
Django Context: # Production alias handles all JobPost.objects workflows # Stable version for Author.objects approval process # Used by production Django app settings
Example 2: Create Canary Deployment Alias

Create alias with traffic split between versions.

#!/bin/bash
# Canary deployment: 90% old, 10% new version
aws stepfunctions create-state-machine-alias \
    --name "arn:aws:states:us-east-1:123456789012:stateMachine:anihpj-jobpost-workflow#canary" \
    --routing-configuration '[{"stateMachineVersionArn":"arn:aws:states:us-east-1:123456789012:stateMachine:anihpj-jobpost-workflow:1","weight":90},{"stateMachineVersionArn":"arn:aws:states:us-east-1:123456789012:stateMachine:anihpj-jobpost-workflow:2","weight":10}]'
Output: { "stateMachineAliasArn": "arn:aws:states:us-east-1:123456789012:stateMachine:anihpj-jobpost-workflow:canary", "creationDate": "2025-01-15T11:05:00Z" }
Django Context: # Canary testing new JobPost validation logic # 10% of Skills.objects checks use new version # Gradual rollout for Location.objects updates
Example 3: Create Staging Alias

Create alias for staging environment testing.

#!/bin/bash
# Staging alias for pre-production testing
aws stepfunctions create-state-machine-alias \
    --name "arn:aws:states:us-east-1:123456789012:stateMachine:anihpj-skills-matcher#staging" \
    --routing-configuration '[{"stateMachineVersionArn":"arn:aws:states:us-east-1:123456789012:stateMachine:anihpj-skills-matcher:3","weight":100}]' \
    --description "Staging environment for skills matching workflow"
Output: { "stateMachineAliasArn": "arn:aws:states:us-east-1:123456789012:stateMachine:anihpj-skills-matcher:staging", "creationDate": "2025-01-15T11:10:00Z" }
Django Context: # Staging tests Skills.objects matching algorithms # Pre-production JobPost.objects.filter() validation # QA testing before User.objects impacted
Example 4: Create Blue-Green Alias

Create alias for blue-green deployment switching.

#!/bin/bash
# Blue alias - can quickly switch to green
aws stepfunctions create-state-machine-alias \
    --name "arn:aws:states:us-east-1:123456789012:stateMachine:anihpj-author-onboard#blue" \
    --routing-configuration '[{"stateMachineVersionArn":"arn:aws:states:us-east-1:123456789012:stateMachine:anihpj-author-onboard:5","weight":100}]' \
    --description "Blue deployment for author onboarding"
Output: { "stateMachineAliasArn": "arn:aws:states:us-east-1:123456789012:stateMachine:anihpj-author-onboard:blue", "creationDate": "2025-01-15T11:15:00Z" }
Django Context: # Blue deployment for Author.objects.create() flow # Instant rollback capability for User.objects # Zero-downtime deployment strategy
Example 5: Create Regional Alias

Create alias for region-specific workflow version.

#!/bin/bash
# Regional alias with compliance-specific version
aws stepfunctions create-state-machine-alias \
    --name "arn:aws:states:eu-west-1:123456789012:stateMachine:anihpj-jobpost-workflow#eu-gdpr" \
    --routing-configuration '[{"stateMachineVersionArn":"arn:aws:states:eu-west-1:123456789012:stateMachine:anihpj-jobpost-workflow:2","weight":100}]' \
    --description "EU GDPR compliant workflow version"
Output: { "stateMachineAliasArn": "arn:aws:states:eu-west-1:123456789012:stateMachine:anihpj-jobpost-workflow:eu-gdpr", "creationDate": "2025-01-15T11:20:00Z" }
Django Context: # GDPR compliant JobPost.objects handling # EU Location.objects with data residency # Author.objects privacy compliance workflow

start-execution

Starts a new execution of a state machine.

Syntax
aws stepfunctions start-execution \
    --state-machine-arn <value> \
    [--name <value>] \
    [--input <value>]
Example 1: Start JobPost approval workflow

Execute approval for new job post.

#!/bin/bash
aws stepfunctions start-execution \
    --state-machine-arn arn:aws:states:us-east-1:123456789012:stateMachine:anihpj-jobpost-approval \
    --name "jobpost-12345-approval" \
    --input '{"jobpost_id": 12345, "author_id": 67, "title": "Senior Python Developer"}'
Output: { "executionArn": "arn:aws:states:us-east-1:123456789012:execution:anihpj-jobpost-approval:jobpost-12345-approval", "startDate": "2025-01-11T17:15:00Z" }
Django Context: # Workflow started for: # JobPost.objects.get(pk=12345) # Author.objects.get(pk=67) # Processing title: "Senior Python Developer"
Example 2: Batch process multiple job posts

Start execution for batch processing.

#!/bin/bash
for jobpost_id in 101 102 103 104 105; do
    aws stepfunctions start-execution \
        --state-machine-arn arn:aws:states:us-east-1:123456789012:stateMachine:anihpj-jobpost-approval \
        --name "batch-jobpost-$jobpost_id" \
        --input "{\"jobpost_id\": $jobpost_id}"
done
Output: { "executionArn": "...execution:anihpj-jobpost-approval:batch-jobpost-101" } { "executionArn": "...execution:anihpj-jobpost-approval:batch-jobpost-102" } ...
Django Context: # Batch processing: JobPost.objects.filter(pk__in=[101,102,103,104,105]) # Each job post goes through approval workflow # Similar to Django bulk_update pattern
Example 3: Start with complex input payload

Execute workflow with detailed job post data.

#!/bin/bash
# Complex input with nested data
aws stepfunctions start-execution \
    --state-machine-arn arn:aws:states:us-east-1:123456789012:stateMachine:anihpj-jobpost-approval \
    --name "jobpost-full-789" \
    --input '{
        "jobpost_id": 789,
        "author": {"id": 45, "name": "Tech Corp"},
        "skills": ["Python", "Django", "AWS"],
        "location": {"city": "New York", "remote": true},
        "salary_range": {"min": 120000, "max": 180000}
    }'
Output: { "executionArn": "arn:aws:states:us-east-1:123456789012:execution:anihpj-jobpost-approval:jobpost-full-789", "startDate": "2025-01-15T12:00:00Z" }
Django Context: # Full JobPost.objects data submitted # Skills.objects.filter(name__in=['Python','Django','AWS']) # Location.objects with remote=True option
Example 4: Start author onboarding workflow

Execute new author registration process.

#!/bin/bash
# Start author onboarding
aws stepfunctions start-execution \
    --state-machine-arn arn:aws:states:us-east-1:123456789012:stateMachine:anihpj-author-onboard \
    --name "author-onboard-$(date +%s)" \
    --input '{"user_id": 1001, "email": "newauthor@company.com", "company": "StartupXYZ"}'
Output: { "executionArn": "arn:aws:states:us-east-1:123456789012:execution:anihpj-author-onboard:author-onboard-1705312800", "startDate": "2025-01-15T12:00:00Z" }
Django Context: # Author.objects.create() workflow started # User.objects.get(pk=1001) verification # Company profile setup for StartupXYZ
Example 5: Start skills verification workflow

Execute skill matching and verification process.

#!/bin/bash
# Skills verification for job matching
aws stepfunctions start-execution \
    --state-machine-arn arn:aws:states:us-east-1:123456789012:stateMachine:anihpj-skills-matcher \
    --name "skills-match-jobpost-555" \
    --input '{"jobpost_id": 555, "required_skills": ["AWS", "Docker", "Kubernetes"], "min_match_percent": 80}'
Output: { "executionArn": "arn:aws:states:us-east-1:123456789012:execution:anihpj-skills-matcher:skills-match-jobpost-555", "startDate": "2025-01-15T12:05:00Z" }
Django Context: # Skills matching for JobPost.objects.get(pk=555) # Skills.objects.filter(name__in=['AWS','Docker','Kubernetes']) # Minimum 80% skill match required

describe-execution

Gets the status and details of a workflow execution.

Syntax
aws stepfunctions describe-execution \
    --execution-arn <value>
Example 1: Check execution status

Get status of running workflow.

#!/bin/bash
aws stepfunctions describe-execution \
    --execution-arn arn:aws:states:us-east-1:123456789012:execution:anihpj-jobpost-approval:jobpost-12345-approval
Output: { "executionArn": "arn:aws:states:...:jobpost-12345-approval", "status": "SUCCEEDED", "startDate": "2025-01-11T17:15:00Z", "stopDate": "2025-01-11T17:15:45Z", "input": "{\"jobpost_id\": 12345}", "output": "{\"approved\": true, \"published\": true}" }
Django Context: # Workflow completed successfully # JobPost.objects.get(pk=12345).status = 'published' # Duration: 45 seconds for full approval process
Example 2: Check failed execution

Get details of failed workflow.

#!/bin/bash
aws stepfunctions describe-execution \
    --execution-arn arn:aws:states:us-east-1:123456789012:execution:anihpj-jobpost-approval:failed-exec
Output: { "status": "FAILED", "error": "ValidationError", "cause": "JobPost missing required skills" }
Django Context: # Workflow failed: JobPost validation error # Missing required_skills for JobPost # Author notified of rejection
Example 3: Check running execution progress

Monitor in-progress workflow execution.

#!/bin/bash
# Check ongoing execution
aws stepfunctions describe-execution \
    --execution-arn arn:aws:states:us-east-1:123456789012:execution:anihpj-author-onboard:author-101
Output: { "executionArn": "arn:aws:states:us-east-1:123456789012:execution:anihpj-author-onboard:author-101", "stateMachineArn": "arn:aws:states:us-east-1:123456789012:stateMachine:anihpj-author-onboard", "name": "author-101", "status": "RUNNING", "startDate": "2025-01-15T10:00:00Z", "input": "{\"user_id\": 101, \"email\": \"author@example.com\"}" }
Django Context: # Author onboarding in progress # User.objects.get(pk=101) being processed # Waiting for verification steps to complete
Example 4: Get execution output data

Retrieve workflow output for processing.

#!/bin/bash
# Get output from completed execution
aws stepfunctions describe-execution \
    --execution-arn arn:aws:states:us-east-1:123456789012:execution:anihpj-skills-matcher:match-500 \
    --query '{Status:status,Output:output}'
Output: { "Status": "SUCCEEDED", "Output": "{\"matched_candidates\": 15, \"top_match_score\": 95, \"job_post_id\": 500}" }
Django Context: # Skills matching completed for JobPost 500 # 15 candidates matched required Skills.objects # Top candidate scored 95% match
Example 5: Check aborted execution details

Review details of manually stopped workflow.

#!/bin/bash
# Check aborted execution
aws stepfunctions describe-execution \
    --execution-arn arn:aws:states:us-east-1:123456789012:execution:anihpj-jobpost-approval:cancelled-post-999
Output: { "executionArn": "arn:aws:states:us-east-1:123456789012:execution:anihpj-jobpost-approval:cancelled-post-999", "status": "ABORTED", "startDate": "2025-01-15T08:00:00Z", "stopDate": "2025-01-15T08:05:30Z", "input": "{\"jobpost_id\": 999}", "error": "UserAborted", "cause": "Author requested cancellation" }
Django Context: # JobPost.objects.get(pk=999) workflow cancelled # Author.objects requested stop via admin # Status reset to 'draft' for re-submission

list-executions

Lists all executions of a state machine.

Syntax
aws stepfunctions list-executions \
