    --state-machine-arn <value> \
    [--status-filter RUNNING|SUCCEEDED|FAILED|TIMED_OUT|ABORTED]
Example 1: List running executions

Get all currently running workflows.

#!/bin/bash
aws stepfunctions list-executions \
    --state-machine-arn arn:aws:states:us-east-1:123456789012:stateMachine:anihpj-jobpost-approval \
    --status-filter RUNNING
Output: { "executions": [ { "executionArn": "...batch-jobpost-103", "name": "batch-jobpost-103", "status": "RUNNING", "startDate": "2025-01-11T17:20:00Z" }, { "executionArn": "...batch-jobpost-104", "name": "batch-jobpost-104", "status": "RUNNING" } ] }
Django Context: # 2 JobPost approvals currently in progress # JobPost.objects.filter(pk__in=[103, 104]) # Still processing through approval states
Example 2: List failed executions

Review failed workflows for debugging.

#!/bin/bash
aws stepfunctions list-executions \
    --state-machine-arn arn:aws:states:us-east-1:123456789012:stateMachine:anihpj-jobpost-approval \
    --status-filter FAILED \
    --max-results 10
Output: { "executions": [ { "executionArn": "...failed-jobpost-999", "status": "FAILED", "stopDate": "2025-01-11T16:45:00Z" } ] }
Django Context: # Failed JobPost.objects approval to investigate # Review validation errors # Check Skills.objects requirements
Example 3: List succeeded executions with pagination

Paginate through successful workflow executions.

#!/bin/bash
# Get first page of succeeded executions
aws stepfunctions list-executions \
    --state-machine-arn arn:aws:states:us-east-1:123456789012:stateMachine:anihpj-jobpost-approval \
    --status-filter SUCCEEDED \
    --max-results 25
Output: { "executions": [ { "executionArn": "arn:aws:states:us-east-1:123456789012:execution:anihpj-jobpost-approval:jobpost-500", "name": "jobpost-500", "status": "SUCCEEDED", "startDate": "2025-01-15T09:00:00Z", "stopDate": "2025-01-15T09:00:15Z" } ], "nextToken": "eyJuZXh0VG9rZW4iOiBudWxsfQ==" }
Django Context: # Successfully approved JobPost.objects entries # JobPost.objects.filter(status='approved') # Author.objects notified of approval
Example 4: List all executions for audit

Get all executions regardless of status for audit purposes.

#!/bin/bash
# List all executions for compliance audit
aws stepfunctions list-executions \
    --state-machine-arn arn:aws:states:us-east-1:123456789012:stateMachine:anihpj-author-onboard \
    --max-results 100 \
    --query 'executions[*].{Name:name,Status:status,Start:startDate}'
Output: [ {"Name": "author-onboard-101", "Status": "SUCCEEDED", "Start": "2025-01-15T08:00:00Z"}, {"Name": "author-onboard-102", "Status": "SUCCEEDED", "Start": "2025-01-15T08:30:00Z"}, {"Name": "author-onboard-103", "Status": "FAILED", "Start": "2025-01-15T09:00:00Z"}, {"Name": "author-onboard-104", "Status": "RUNNING", "Start": "2025-01-15T09:30:00Z"} ]
Django Context: # Audit trail for Author.objects.all() onboarding # Tracks User.objects account creation status # Compliance reporting for HR processes
Example 5: List timed out executions

Find executions that exceeded timeout limits.

#!/bin/bash
# Find workflows that timed out
aws stepfunctions list-executions \
    --state-machine-arn arn:aws:states:us-east-1:123456789012:stateMachine:anihpj-skills-verification \
    --status-filter TIMED_OUT \
    --max-results 50
Output: { "executions": [ { "executionArn": "arn:aws:states:us-east-1:123456789012:execution:anihpj-skills-verification:skill-check-789", "name": "skill-check-789", "status": "TIMED_OUT", "startDate": "2025-01-14T10:00:00Z", "stopDate": "2025-01-14T10:05:00Z" } ] }
Django Context: # Skills.objects verification took too long # External API timeout for skill validation # Needs retry with JobPost.objects.get(pk=789)

delete-activity

Deletes an activity from Step Functions.

Syntax
aws stepfunctions delete-activity \
    --activity-arn <value>
Parameters
  • --activity-arn (string): ARN of the activity to delete (required)
Example 1: Delete Unused Review Activity

Remove deprecated job post review activity.

#!/bin/bash
# Delete old review activity no longer in use
aws stepfunctions delete-activity \
    --activity-arn arn:aws:states:us-east-1:123456789012:activity:anihpj-old-review
Output: {}
Django Context: # Removed deprecated JobPost review activity # No longer used in current workflows # Clean up from migration to automated approval
Example 2: Delete Test Activity

Clean up activity created for testing.

#!/bin/bash
# Remove test activity after QA
aws stepfunctions delete-activity \
    --activity-arn arn:aws:states:us-east-1:123456789012:activity:anihpj-test-skills-check
Output: {}
Django Context: # Test activity for Skills.objects validation removed # QA testing complete # Production uses automated Lambda instead
Example 3: Delete Activity with Confirmation

Delete activity after verifying it's not in use.

#!/bin/bash
# First check if activity exists
ACTIVITY_ARN="arn:aws:states:us-east-1:123456789012:activity:anihpj-manual-approval"
if aws stepfunctions describe-activity --activity-arn "$ACTIVITY_ARN" 2>/dev/null; then
    aws stepfunctions delete-activity --activity-arn "$ACTIVITY_ARN"
    echo "Activity deleted successfully"
fi
Output: Activity deleted successfully
Django Context: # Manual Author.objects approval activity removed # Confirmed no pending JobPost.objects tasks # Workflow updated to use automated process
Example 4: Batch Delete Old Activities

Clean up multiple deprecated activities.

#!/bin/bash
# Delete multiple old activities
for activity in "legacy-review" "old-validation" "deprecated-check"; do
    aws stepfunctions delete-activity \
        --activity-arn "arn:aws:states:us-east-1:123456789012:activity:anihpj-$activity" \
        2>/dev/null && echo "Deleted: $activity"
done
Output: Deleted: legacy-review Deleted: old-validation Deleted: deprecated-check
Django Context: # Cleaned up legacy JobPost.objects activities # Removed deprecated Skills.objects checks # Streamlined workflow for Author.objects
Example 5: Delete Regional Activity

Remove activity from specific region.

#!/bin/bash
# Delete activity in EU region
aws stepfunctions delete-activity \
    --activity-arn arn:aws:states:eu-west-1:123456789012:activity:anihpj-eu-location-verify \
    --region eu-west-1
Output: {}
Django Context: # EU Location.objects verification activity removed # Migrated to centralized workflow # GDPR compliance handled differently now

delete-state-machine

Deletes a state machine and all associated versions and aliases.

Syntax
aws stepfunctions delete-state-machine \
    --state-machine-arn <value>
Parameters
  • --state-machine-arn (string): ARN of the state machine to delete (required)
Example 1: Delete Deprecated Workflow

Remove old job post workflow no longer in use.

#!/bin/bash
# Delete deprecated workflow
aws stepfunctions delete-state-machine \
    --state-machine-arn arn:aws:states:us-east-1:123456789012:stateMachine:anihpj-old-approval-v1
Output: {}
Django Context: # Old JobPost.objects approval workflow removed # Replaced by v2 with Skills.objects matching # All executions completed before deletion
Example 2: Delete Test Workflow

Clean up workflow created for testing.

#!/bin/bash
# Remove test workflow after QA
aws stepfunctions delete-state-machine \
    --state-machine-arn arn:aws:states:us-east-1:123456789012:stateMachine:anihpj-test-workflow
Output: {}
Django Context: # Test workflow for Author.objects flow removed # QA testing completed successfully # Production workflow validated and deployed
Example 3: Delete with Running Check

Delete workflow after ensuring no running executions.

#!/bin/bash
STATE_MACHINE="arn:aws:states:us-east-1:123456789012:stateMachine:anihpj-legacy-skills"
# Check for running executions
RUNNING=$(aws stepfunctions list-executions \
    --state-machine-arn "$STATE_MACHINE" \
    --status-filter RUNNING \
    --query 'length(executions)')
if [ "$RUNNING" -eq 0 ]; then
    aws stepfunctions delete-state-machine --state-machine-arn "$STATE_MACHINE"
    echo "State machine deleted"
else
    echo "Cannot delete: $RUNNING executions still running"
fi
Output: State machine deleted
Django Context: # Safe deletion of Skills.objects workflow # Verified no JobPost.objects processing # Clean shutdown with zero data loss
Example 4: Delete Development Workflow

Remove development environment workflow.

#!/bin/bash
# Delete dev workflow
aws stepfunctions delete-state-machine \
    --state-machine-arn arn:aws:states:us-east-1:123456789012:stateMachine:anihpj-dev-jobpost-flow
Output: {}
Django Context: # Development JobPost.objects workflow cleaned # dev environment tear down # Resources freed for new sprint testing
Example 5: Delete with Tag-Based Selection

Delete workflows matching specific criteria.

#!/bin/bash
# Find and delete deprecated workflows by listing and filtering
aws stepfunctions list-state-machines \
    --query "stateMachines[?contains(name, 'deprecated')].[stateMachineArn]" \
    --output text | while read arn; do
    aws stepfunctions delete-state-machine --state-machine-arn "$arn"
    echo "Deleted: $arn"
done
Output: Deleted: arn:aws:states:us-east-1:123456789012:stateMachine:deprecated-author-flow Deleted: arn:aws:states:us-east-1:123456789012:stateMachine:deprecated-skills-check
Django Context: # Batch cleanup of deprecated workflows # Author.objects and Skills.objects old flows # Infrastructure cleanup for cost savings

delete-state-machine-alias

Deletes a state machine alias.

Syntax
aws stepfunctions delete-state-machine-alias \
    --state-machine-alias-arn <value>
Parameters
  • --state-machine-alias-arn (string): ARN of the alias to delete (required)
Example 1: Delete Canary Alias

Remove canary deployment alias after promotion.

#!/bin/bash
# Delete canary alias - promoted to production
aws stepfunctions delete-state-machine-alias \
    --state-machine-alias-arn arn:aws:states:us-east-1:123456789012:stateMachine:anihpj-jobpost-workflow:canary
Output: {}
Django Context: # Canary testing complete for JobPost.objects # New version promoted to production alias # Skills.objects matching validated
Example 2: Delete Staging Alias

Remove staging alias after production deployment.

#!/bin/bash
# Delete staging alias
aws stepfunctions delete-state-machine-alias \
    --state-machine-alias-arn arn:aws:states:us-east-1:123456789012:stateMachine:anihpj-author-onboard:staging
Output: {}
Django Context: # Staging Author.objects workflow alias removed # Production deployment successful # User.objects onboarding live
Example 3: Delete Old Blue-Green Alias

Clean up blue alias after green is active.

#!/bin/bash
# Delete blue alias - green is now active
aws stepfunctions delete-state-machine-alias \
    --state-machine-alias-arn arn:aws:states:us-east-1:123456789012:stateMachine:anihpj-skills-matcher:blue
Output: {}
Django Context: # Blue-green deployment complete # Skills.objects matcher on green version # Blue alias no longer needed
Example 4: Delete Test Alias

Remove testing alias from development.

#!/bin/bash
# Delete test alias
aws stepfunctions delete-state-machine-alias \
    --state-machine-alias-arn arn:aws:states:us-east-1:123456789012:stateMachine:anihpj-location-verify:test
Output: {}
Django Context: # Test Location.objects verification alias removed # QA cycle complete # Production alias remains active
Example 5: Delete Regional Alias

Remove alias from specific region.

#!/bin/bash
# Delete EU regional alias
aws stepfunctions delete-state-machine-alias \
    --state-machine-alias-arn arn:aws:states:eu-west-1:123456789012:stateMachine:anihpj-jobpost-workflow:eu-staging \
    --region eu-west-1
Output: {}
Django Context: # EU staging alias removed # JobPost.objects EU workflow consolidated # Single production alias for GDPR region

delete-state-machine-version

Deletes a specific version of a state machine.

Syntax
aws stepfunctions delete-state-machine-version \
    --state-machine-version-arn <value>
Parameters
  • --state-machine-version-arn (string): ARN of the version to delete (required)
Example 1: Delete Old Version

Remove deprecated workflow version.

#!/bin/bash
# Delete old version after migration
aws stepfunctions delete-state-machine-version \
    --state-machine-version-arn arn:aws:states:us-east-1:123456789012:stateMachine:anihpj-jobpost-workflow:1
Output: {}
Django Context: # Version 1 of JobPost.objects workflow deleted # All traffic migrated to version 3 # No active executions on old version
Example 2: Delete Failed Version

Remove version that had issues in production.

#!/bin/bash
# Delete problematic version 2
aws stepfunctions delete-state-machine-version \
    --state-machine-version-arn arn:aws:states:us-east-1:123456789012:stateMachine:anihpj-skills-matcher:2
Output: {}
Django Context: # Buggy Skills.objects version removed # Caused false negative matches # Rolled back to stable version 1
Example 3: Delete Multiple Old Versions

Clean up multiple old versions.

#!/bin/bash
# Delete versions 1-3, keep 4+
for version in 1 2 3; do
    aws stepfunctions delete-state-machine-version \
        --state-machine-version-arn "arn:aws:states:us-east-1:123456789012:stateMachine:anihpj-author-onboard:$version" \
        2>/dev/null && echo "Deleted version $version"
done
Output: Deleted version 1 Deleted version 2 Deleted version 3
Django Context: # Old Author.objects onboarding versions cleaned # Kept versions 4+ for rollback capability # User.objects flow modernized
Example 4: Delete After Verification

Delete version after confirming no aliases point to it.

#!/bin/bash
VERSION_ARN="arn:aws:states:us-east-1:123456789012:stateMachine:anihpj-location-verify:5"
# Check if any alias references this version
ALIASES=$(aws stepfunctions list-state-machine-aliases \
    --state-machine-arn "arn:aws:states:us-east-1:123456789012:stateMachine:anihpj-location-verify" \
    --query "stateMachineAliases[?contains(routingConfiguration[].stateMachineVersionArn, '$VERSION_ARN')]")
if [ "$ALIASES" == "[]" ]; then
    aws stepfunctions delete-state-machine-version --state-machine-version-arn "$VERSION_ARN"
fi
Output: {}
Django Context: # Safe deletion of Location.objects version # No aliases reference this version # Version 6 is current production
Example 5: Delete Development Version

Remove version used only in development.

#!/bin/bash
# Delete dev-only version
aws stepfunctions delete-state-machine-version \
    --state-machine-version-arn arn:aws:states:us-east-1:123456789012:stateMachine:anihpj-batch-process:99
Output: {}
Django Context: # Dev version 99 of batch JobPost.objects removed # Experimental features not promoted # Clean development environment

describe-activity

Gets information about an activity, including its creation date and ARN.

Syntax
aws stepfunctions describe-activity \
    --activity-arn <value>
Parameters
  • --activity-arn (string): ARN of the activity to describe (required)
Example 1: Describe Review Activity

Get details about manual review activity.

#!/bin/bash
aws stepfunctions describe-activity \
    --activity-arn arn:aws:states:us-east-1:123456789012:activity:anihpj-jobpost-manual-review
Output: { "activityArn": "arn:aws:states:us-east-1:123456789012:activity:anihpj-jobpost-manual-review", "name": "anihpj-jobpost-manual-review", "creationDate": "2025-01-10T09:00:00Z" }
Django Context: # Activity for JobPost.objects manual review # Created for content moderation workflow # Used by Author.objects approval process
Example 2: Describe Skills Verification Activity

Get details of skills verification activity.

#!/bin/bash
aws stepfunctions describe-activity \
    --activity-arn arn:aws:states:us-east-1:123456789012:activity:anihpj-skills-verification
Output: { "activityArn": "arn:aws:states:us-east-1:123456789012:activity:anihpj-skills-verification", "name": "anihpj-skills-verification", "creationDate": "2025-01-12T14:30:00Z" }
Django Context: # Skills.objects verification activity # Validates required skills for JobPost # Human-in-the-loop for complex validations
Example 3: Check Activity Age

Get creation date for lifecycle management.

#!/bin/bash
# Check when activity was created
aws stepfunctions describe-activity \
    --activity-arn arn:aws:states:us-east-1:123456789012:activity:anihpj-author-approval \
    --query '{Name:name,Created:creationDate}'
Output: { "Name": "anihpj-author-approval", "Created": "2024-06-15T10:00:00Z" }
Django Context: # Author.objects approval activity # Created 7 months ago # Still active in production workflow
Example 4: Describe Location Activity

Get details of location validation activity.

#!/bin/bash
aws stepfunctions describe-activity \
    --activity-arn arn:aws:states:us-east-1:123456789012:activity:anihpj-location-validation
Output: { "activityArn": "arn:aws:states:us-east-1:123456789012:activity:anihpj-location-validation", "name": "anihpj-location-validation", "creationDate": "2025-01-08T16:45:00Z" }
Django Context: # Location.objects validation activity # Geocoding and address verification # JobPost.location ForeignKey validation
Example 5: Verify Activity Exists

Check if activity exists before using in workflow.

#!/bin/bash
# Verify activity exists
ACTIVITY_ARN="arn:aws:states:us-east-1:123456789012:activity:anihpj-batch-processor"
if aws stepfunctions describe-activity --activity-arn "$ACTIVITY_ARN" 2>/dev/null; then
    echo "Activity exists and is ready"
else
    echo "Activity not found - creating..."
fi
Output: { "activityArn": "arn:aws:states:us-east-1:123456789012:activity:anihpj-batch-processor", "name": "anihpj-batch-processor", "creationDate": "2025-01-05T08:00:00Z" } Activity exists and is ready
Django Context: # Batch processor for JobPost.objects.all() # Activity ready for workflow integration # Handles bulk Skills.objects updates

describe-map-run

Gets details about a Map Run, including item counts and execution statistics.

Syntax
aws stepfunctions describe-map-run \
    --map-run-arn <value>
Parameters
  • --map-run-arn (string): ARN of the Map Run to describe (required)
Example 1: Check Batch Processing Status

Get status of parallel job post processing.

#!/bin/bash
aws stepfunctions describe-map-run \
    --map-run-arn arn:aws:states:us-east-1:123456789012:mapRun:anihpj-batch-workflow/batch-run-001:abc123
Output: { "mapRunArn": "arn:aws:states:us-east-1:123456789012:mapRun:anihpj-batch-workflow/batch-run-001:abc123", "executionArn": "arn:aws:states:us-east-1:123456789012:execution:anihpj-batch-workflow:batch-run-001", "status": "RUNNING", "startDate": "2025-01-15T10:00:00Z", "maxConcurrency": 10, "itemCounts": { "pending": 45, "running": 10, "succeeded": 145, "failed": 0, "total": 200 } }
Django Context: # Processing 200 JobPost.objects in parallel # 145 completed, 55 remaining # Max 10 concurrent Skills.objects validations
Example 2: Check Completed Map Run

Review completed batch processing results.

#!/bin/bash
aws stepfunctions describe-map-run \
    --map-run-arn arn:aws:states:us-east-1:123456789012:mapRun:anihpj-skills-batch/skills-update:def456
Output: { "mapRunArn": "arn:aws:states:us-east-1:123456789012:mapRun:anihpj-skills-batch/skills-update:def456", "status": "SUCCEEDED", "startDate": "2025-01-15T08:00:00Z", "stopDate": "2025-01-15T08:15:00Z", "itemCounts": { "pending": 0, "running": 0, "succeeded": 500, "failed": 0, "total": 500 } }
Django Context: # All 500 Skills.objects updated successfully # 15 minute total processing time # Zero failures in batch update
Example 3: Check Map Run with Failures

Analyze Map Run with some failed items.

#!/bin/bash
aws stepfunctions describe-map-run \
    --map-run-arn arn:aws:states:us-east-1:123456789012:mapRun:anihpj-author-notify/notify-batch:ghi789 \
    --query '{Status:status,Succeeded:itemCounts.succeeded,Failed:itemCounts.failed}'
Output: { "Status": "SUCCEEDED", "Succeeded": 95, "Failed": 5 }
Django Context: # Author.objects notifications: 95 sent, 5 failed # Failed likely due to invalid email addresses # User.objects email validation needed
Example 4: Monitor Long-Running Map

Check progress of large batch operation.

#!/bin/bash
# Monitor progress every 30 seconds
MAP_RUN="arn:aws:states:us-east-1:123456789012:mapRun:anihpj-migrate/full-migration:jkl012"
while true; do
    STATUS=$(aws stepfunctions describe-map-run --map-run-arn "$MAP_RUN" \
        --query '{Status:status,Done:itemCounts.succeeded,Total:itemCounts.total}')
    echo "$(date): $STATUS"
    [[ $(echo "$STATUS" | jq -r '.Status') == "SUCCEEDED" ]] && break
    sleep 30
done
Output: Mon Jan 15 10:00:00 2025: {"Status": "RUNNING", "Done": 1000, "Total": 5000} Mon Jan 15 10:00:30 2025: {"Status": "RUNNING", "Done": 1500, "Total": 5000} Mon Jan 15 10:01:00 2025: {"Status": "SUCCEEDED", "Done": 5000, "Total": 5000}
Django Context: # Full JobPost.objects migration: 5000 records # Location.objects geocoding updates # Skills.objects standardization complete
Example 5: Get Detailed Execution Stats

Get comprehensive Map Run statistics.

#!/bin/bash
aws stepfunctions describe-map-run \
    --map-run-arn arn:aws:states:us-east-1:123456789012:mapRun:anihpj-daily-process/daily-2025-01-15:mno345
Output: { "mapRunArn": "arn:aws:states:...:daily-2025-01-15:mno345", "status": "SUCCEEDED", "startDate": "2025-01-15T02:00:00Z", "stopDate": "2025-01-15T02:45:00Z", "maxConcurrency": 20, "toleratedFailurePercentage": 5.0, "toleratedFailureCount": 10, "itemCounts": { "pending": 0, "running": 0, "succeeded": 1850, "failed": 2, "timedOut": 0, "total": 1852 }, "executionCounts": { "pending": 0, "running": 0, "succeeded": 1850, "failed": 2, "total": 1852 } }
Django Context: # Daily JobPost.objects processing complete # 1850 succeeded, 2 failed (within tolerance) # 45 min runtime with 20 parallel workers

describe-state-machine

Gets information about a state machine, including its definition and configuration.

Syntax
aws stepfunctions describe-state-machine \
    --state-machine-arn <value>
Parameters
  • --state-machine-arn (string): ARN of the state machine to describe (required)
Example 1: Get Workflow Details

Describe job post approval workflow.

#!/bin/bash
aws stepfunctions describe-state-machine \
    --state-machine-arn arn:aws:states:us-east-1:123456789012:stateMachine:anihpj-jobpost-approval
Output: { "stateMachineArn": "arn:aws:states:us-east-1:123456789012:stateMachine:anihpj-jobpost-approval", "name": "anihpj-jobpost-approval", "status": "ACTIVE", "definition": "{\"StartAt\":\"ValidateJobPost\",...}", "roleArn": "arn:aws:iam::123456789012:role/StepFunctionsRole", "type": "STANDARD", "creationDate": "2025-01-01T00:00:00Z" }
Django Context: # JobPost.objects approval workflow details # STANDARD type for long-running approvals # Active and processing submissions
Example 2: Get Express Workflow Details

Describe Express workflow configuration.

#!/bin/bash
aws stepfunctions describe-state-machine \
    --state-machine-arn arn:aws:states:us-east-1:123456789012:stateMachine:anihpj-quick-validate \
    --query '{Name:name,Type:type,Status:status}'
Output: { "Name": "anihpj-quick-validate", "Type": "EXPRESS", "Status": "ACTIVE" }
Django Context: # Express workflow for fast validation # Sub-second JobPost.objects checks # Skills.objects.exists() validation
Example 3: Extract Workflow Definition

Get workflow ASL definition for review.

#!/bin/bash
# Extract and format workflow definition
aws stepfunctions describe-state-machine \
    --state-machine-arn arn:aws:states:us-east-1:123456789012:stateMachine:anihpj-skills-matcher \
    --query 'definition' --output text | jq .
Output: { "StartAt": "FetchJobPost", "States": { "FetchJobPost": { "Type": "Task", "Resource": "arn:aws:lambda:...:fetch-jobpost", "Next": "MatchSkills" }, "MatchSkills": { "Type": "Task", "Resource": "arn:aws:lambda:...:match-skills", "End": true } } }
Django Context: # Skills.objects matching workflow definition # FetchJobPost -> MatchSkills states # Lambda functions for JobPost processing
Example 4: Check Workflow IAM Role

Verify IAM role assigned to workflow.

#!/bin/bash
aws stepfunctions describe-state-machine \
    --state-machine-arn arn:aws:states:us-east-1:123456789012:stateMachine:anihpj-author-onboard \
    --query '{Name:name,Role:roleArn}'
Output: { "Name": "anihpj-author-onboard", "Role": "arn:aws:iam::123456789012:role/anihpj-stepfunctions-author-role" }
Django Context: # Author.objects onboarding workflow # IAM role with User.objects permissions # Limited to author-related resources
Example 5: Get Workflow Logging Config

Check logging configuration for workflow.

#!/bin/bash
aws stepfunctions describe-state-machine \
    --state-machine-arn arn:aws:states:us-east-1:123456789012:stateMachine:anihpj-location-verify \
    --query '{Name:name,Logging:loggingConfiguration,Tracing:tracingConfiguration}'
Output: { "Name": "anihpj-location-verify", "Logging": { "level": "ALL", "includeExecutionData": true, "destinations": [ {"cloudWatchLogsLogGroup": {"logGroupArn": "arn:aws:logs:...:anihpj-stepfunctions"}} ] }, "Tracing": {"enabled": true} }
Django Context: # Location.objects verification with full logging # X-Ray tracing enabled for debugging # All execution data captured in CloudWatch

describe-state-machine-alias

Gets information about a state machine alias, including routing configuration.

Syntax
aws stepfunctions describe-state-machine-alias \
    --state-machine-alias-arn <value>
Parameters
  • --state-machine-alias-arn (string): ARN of the alias to describe (required)
Example 1: Describe Production Alias

Get details of production routing configuration.

#!/bin/bash
aws stepfunctions describe-state-machine-alias \
    --state-machine-alias-arn arn:aws:states:us-east-1:123456789012:stateMachine:anihpj-jobpost-workflow:prod
Output: { "stateMachineAliasArn": "arn:aws:states:us-east-1:123456789012:stateMachine:anihpj-jobpost-workflow:prod", "name": "prod", "description": "Production alias for job post workflow", "routingConfiguration": [ { "stateMachineVersionArn": "arn:aws:states:us-east-1:123456789012:stateMachine:anihpj-jobpost-workflow:3", "weight": 100 } ], "creationDate": "2025-01-01T00:00:00Z", "updateDate": "2025-01-10T00:00:00Z" }
Django Context: # Production JobPost.objects workflow alias # 100% traffic to version 3 # All Skills.objects processing stable
Example 2: Check Canary Deployment

View canary traffic split configuration.

#!/bin/bash
aws stepfunctions describe-state-machine-alias \
    --state-machine-alias-arn arn:aws:states:us-east-1:123456789012:stateMachine:anihpj-skills-matcher:canary
Output: { "stateMachineAliasArn": "arn:aws:states:...:anihpj-skills-matcher:canary", "name": "canary", "routingConfiguration": [ {"stateMachineVersionArn": "...:anihpj-skills-matcher:5", "weight": 90}, {"stateMachineVersionArn": "...:anihpj-skills-matcher:6", "weight": 10} ] }
Django Context: # Canary deployment for Skills.objects matcher # 90% on stable v5, 10% testing v6 # Gradual rollout of new matching algorithm
Example 3: Verify Alias Routing

Check which version alias points to.

#!/bin/bash
aws stepfunctions describe-state-machine-alias \
    --state-machine-alias-arn arn:aws:states:us-east-1:123456789012:stateMachine:anihpj-author-onboard:live \
    --query 'routingConfiguration[0].stateMachineVersionArn'
Output: "arn:aws:states:us-east-1:123456789012:stateMachine:anihpj-author-onboard:4"
Django Context: # Live alias points to Author.objects v4 # User.objects onboarding version confirmed # Ready for production traffic
Example 4: Check Alias Update Time

See when alias was last modified.

#!/bin/bash
aws stepfunctions describe-state-machine-alias \
    --state-machine-alias-arn arn:aws:states:us-east-1:123456789012:stateMachine:anihpj-location-verify:stable \
    --query '{Name:name,Created:creationDate,Updated:updateDate}'
Output: { "Name": "stable", "Created": "2024-12-01T00:00:00Z", "Updated": "2025-01-14T12:30:00Z" }
Django Context: # Stable Location.objects alias # Created Dec 2024, updated yesterday # Recent version update deployed
Example 5: Compare Alias Configurations

Compare prod and staging alias configurations.

#!/bin/bash
echo "Production:"
aws stepfunctions describe-state-machine-alias \
    --state-machine-alias-arn arn:aws:states:us-east-1:123456789012:stateMachine:anihpj-batch-process:prod \
    --query 'routingConfiguration'
echo "Staging:"
aws stepfunctions describe-state-machine-alias \
    --state-machine-alias-arn arn:aws:states:us-east-1:123456789012:stateMachine:anihpj-batch-process:staging \
    --query 'routingConfiguration'
Output: Production: [{"stateMachineVersionArn": "...:anihpj-batch-process:2", "weight": 100}] Staging: [{"stateMachineVersionArn": "...:anihpj-batch-process:3", "weight": 100}]
Django Context: # Prod on v2, Staging testing v3 # JobPost.objects batch processing versions # v3 ready for promotion after validation

describe-state-machine-for-execution

Gets the state machine definition used for a specific execution.

Syntax
aws stepfunctions describe-state-machine-for-execution \
    --execution-arn <value>
Parameters
  • --execution-arn (string): ARN of the execution (required)
Example 1: Get Definition for Running Execution

Get workflow definition used by current execution.

#!/bin/bash
aws stepfunctions describe-state-machine-for-execution \
    --execution-arn arn:aws:states:us-east-1:123456789012:execution:anihpj-jobpost-approval:job-12345
Output: { "stateMachineArn": "arn:aws:states:us-east-1:123456789012:stateMachine:anihpj-jobpost-approval", "name": "anihpj-jobpost-approval", "definition": "{\"StartAt\":\"ValidateJobPost\",\"States\":{...}}", "roleArn": "arn:aws:iam::123456789012:role/StepFunctionsRole", "updateDate": "2025-01-10T00:00:00Z" }
Django Context: # Definition used for JobPost.objects.get(pk=12345) # Shows exact workflow version in use # Useful for debugging Skills.objects validation
Example 2: Debug Failed Execution

Get definition to debug failed workflow.

#!/bin/bash
# Get definition for failed execution to review
aws stepfunctions describe-state-machine-for-execution \
    --execution-arn arn:aws:states:us-east-1:123456789012:execution:anihpj-skills-matcher:failed-match-999 \
    --query 'definition' --output text | jq .
Output: { "StartAt": "FetchSkills", "States": { "FetchSkills": { "Type": "Task", "Resource": "arn:aws:lambda:...:fetch-skills", "Next": "MatchCandidates", "Catch": [{"ErrorEquals": ["States.ALL"], "Next": "HandleError"}] } } }
Django Context: # Skills.objects fetch definition for debugging # Shows error handling configuration # Catch block for all States errors
Example 3: Compare Historical Execution

Get definition from past execution for comparison.

#!/bin/bash
# Get definition from old execution
aws stepfunctions describe-state-machine-for-execution \
    --execution-arn arn:aws:states:us-east-1:123456789012:execution:anihpj-author-onboard:author-old-100 \
    --query '{Name:name,Updated:updateDate}'
Output: { "Name": "anihpj-author-onboard", "Updated": "2024-12-15T00:00:00Z" }
Django Context: # Old Author.objects workflow version # Updated Dec 15, 2024 # Compare with current for User.objects changes
Example 4: Verify Express Workflow

Check Express workflow type for execution.

#!/bin/bash
aws stepfunctions describe-state-machine-for-execution \
    --execution-arn arn:aws:states:us-east-1:123456789012:express:anihpj-quick-validate:fast-check-001:abc \
    --query '{Name:name,Type:type}'
Output: { "Name": "anihpj-quick-validate", "Type": "EXPRESS" }
Django Context: # Express workflow for JobPost.objects validation # Sub-second execution type # Location.objects quick checks
Example 5: Extract Role for Troubleshooting

Get IAM role used by execution for permission debugging.

#!/bin/bash
# Get role ARN for permission troubleshooting
aws stepfunctions describe-state-machine-for-execution \
    --execution-arn arn:aws:states:us-east-1:123456789012:execution:anihpj-location-verify:loc-check-500 \
    --query 'roleArn'
Output: "arn:aws:iam::123456789012:role/anihpj-stepfunctions-location-role"
Django Context: # IAM role for Location.objects verification # Check permissions for geocoding API calls # Troubleshoot JobPost.location access

get-activity-task

Gets a task from an activity for processing by a worker.

Syntax
aws stepfunctions get-activity-task \
    --activity-arn <value> \
    [--worker-name <value>]
Parameters
  • --activity-arn (string): ARN of the activity (required)
  • --worker-name (string): Name identifying the worker
Example 1: Poll for Review Task

Worker polls for manual review task.

#!/bin/bash
aws stepfunctions get-activity-task \
    --activity-arn arn:aws:states:us-east-1:123456789012:activity:anihpj-jobpost-manual-review \
    --worker-name "review-worker-01"
Output: { "taskToken": "AAAAKgAAAAIAAAAAAAAAAX9Z...", "input": "{\"jobpost_id\": 12345, \"title\": \"Senior Developer\", \"author_id\": 67}" }
Django Context: # Task received for JobPost.objects.get(pk=12345) # Manual review required for Author.objects(pk=67) # Worker processes then sends success/failure
Example 2: Skills Verification Worker

Worker polls for skills verification task.

#!/bin/bash
# Worker continuously polls for tasks
aws stepfunctions get-activity-task \
    --activity-arn arn:aws:states:us-east-1:123456789012:activity:anihpj-skills-verification \
    --worker-name "skills-verifier-$(hostname)"
Output: { "taskToken": "AAAAKgAAAAIBBBBBBBBBBBBB...", "input": "{\"skills\": [\"Python\", \"Django\", \"AWS\"], \"jobpost_id\": 789}" }
Django Context: # Verify Skills.objects requirements # Check against JobPost.objects(pk=789) # Return verification result with taskToken
Example 3: Long Polling Worker Loop

Worker loop for continuous task processing.

#!/bin/bash
# Worker loop with long polling
ACTIVITY="arn:aws:states:us-east-1:123456789012:activity:anihpj-author-approval"
while true; do
    TASK=$(aws stepfunctions get-activity-task \
        --activity-arn "$ACTIVITY" \
        --worker-name "approval-worker-01")
    if [ -n "$TASK" ] && [ "$TASK" != "{}" ]; then
        echo "Processing task: $TASK"
        # Process task here
    fi
done
Output: Processing task: {"taskToken": "AAAA...", "input": "{\"author_id\": 101}"}
Django Context: # Continuous Author.objects approval processing # Long polling waits for tasks (up to 60s) # User.objects verification workflow
Example 4: Location Validation Worker

Worker for location data validation tasks.

#!/bin/bash
aws stepfunctions get-activity-task \
    --activity-arn arn:aws:states:us-east-1:123456789012:activity:anihpj-location-validation \
    --worker-name "geocoder-worker-east"
Output: { "taskToken": "AAAAKgAAAAICCCCCCCCCCCCC...", "input": "{\"location_id\": 456, \"address\": \"123 Main St\", \"city\": \"New York\"}" }
Django Context: # Location.objects.get(pk=456) validation # Geocode and verify address data # Update JobPost.location coordinates
Example 5: Batch Processing Worker

Worker for batch job post processing.

#!/bin/bash
# Batch worker with timeout handling
TASK=$(timeout 65 aws stepfunctions get-activity-task \
    --activity-arn arn:aws:states:us-east-1:123456789012:activity:anihpj-batch-processor \
    --worker-name "batch-worker-$(date +%s)")
if [ -n "$TASK" ] && [ "$TASK" != "{}" ]; then
    echo "Batch task received"
    TOKEN=$(echo "$TASK" | jq -r '.taskToken')
    INPUT=$(echo "$TASK" | jq -r '.input')
    echo "Processing: $INPUT"
fi
Output: Batch task received Processing: {"batch_id": "batch-2025-01-15", "jobpost_ids": [100, 101, 102]}
Django Context: # Batch JobPost.objects.filter(pk__in=[100,101,102]) # Process multiple records in single task # Skills.objects bulk validation

get-execution-history

Gets the execution history of a workflow with all state transitions.

Syntax
aws stepfunctions get-execution-history \
    --execution-arn <value> \
    [--max-results <value>] \
    [--reverse-order] \
    [--include-execution-data | --no-include-execution-data]
Parameters
  • --execution-arn (string): ARN of the execution (required)
  • --max-results (integer): Maximum events to return
  • --reverse-order (boolean): Return newest events first
  • --include-execution-data (boolean): Include input/output data
Example 1: Get Full Execution History

Get complete history of workflow execution.

#!/bin/bash
aws stepfunctions get-execution-history \
    --execution-arn arn:aws:states:us-east-1:123456789012:execution:anihpj-jobpost-approval:job-12345 \
    --include-execution-data
Output: { "events": [ { "timestamp": "2025-01-15T10:00:00Z", "type": "ExecutionStarted", "id": 1, "executionStartedEventDetails": { "input": "{\"jobpost_id\": 12345}" } }, { "timestamp": "2025-01-15T10:00:01Z", "type": "TaskStateEntered", "id": 2, "stateEnteredEventDetails": { "name": "ValidateJobPost" } } ] }
Django Context: # Full history for JobPost.objects.get(pk=12345) # Shows each state transition # Execution started -> ValidateJobPost entered
Example 2: Get Recent Events

Get most recent events in reverse order.

#!/bin/bash
aws stepfunctions get-execution-history \
    --execution-arn arn:aws:states:us-east-1:123456789012:execution:anihpj-skills-matcher:match-500 \
    --reverse-order \
    --max-results 10
Output: { "events": [ {"type": "ExecutionSucceeded", "timestamp": "2025-01-15T10:05:00Z"}, {"type": "TaskStateExited", "timestamp": "2025-01-15T10:04:59Z"}, {"type": "LambdaFunctionSucceeded", "timestamp": "2025-01-15T10:04:58Z"} ] }
Django Context: # Most recent Skills.objects matching events # Execution succeeded at 10:05 # Lambda function completed successfully
Example 3: Debug Failed Execution

Get history to find failure cause.

#!/bin/bash
# Find failure in execution history
aws stepfunctions get-execution-history \
    --execution-arn arn:aws:states:us-east-1:123456789012:execution:anihpj-author-onboard:failed-author \
    --include-execution-data \
    --query 'events[?type==`TaskFailed` || type==`ExecutionFailed`]'
Output: [ { "type": "TaskFailed", "timestamp": "2025-01-15T09:30:00Z", "taskFailedEventDetails": { "error": "ValidationError", "cause": "Author email already exists" } } ]
Django Context: # Author.objects.create() failed # ValidationError: duplicate email # User.objects.filter(email=...) conflict
Example 4: Track State Transitions

Monitor which states were visited.

#!/bin/bash
# Get state names from history
aws stepfunctions get-execution-history \
    --execution-arn arn:aws:states:us-east-1:123456789012:execution:anihpj-location-verify:loc-789 \
    --query 'events[?contains(type, `StateEntered`)].stateEnteredEventDetails.name'
Output: [ "FetchLocation", "ValidateAddress", "GeocodeAddress", "UpdateJobPost", "Success" ]
Django Context: # Location.objects workflow path # Fetch -> Validate -> Geocode -> Update -> Success # All states completed for JobPost.location
Example 5: Calculate State Duration

Analyze time spent in each state.

#!/bin/bash
# Get entered/exited pairs for timing
aws stepfunctions get-execution-history \
    --execution-arn arn:aws:states:us-east-1:123456789012:execution:anihpj-batch-process:batch-001 \
    --query 'events[?contains(type, `State`)].{Type:type,Time:timestamp,State:stateEnteredEventDetails.name||stateExitedEventDetails.name}'
Output: [ {"Type": "TaskStateEntered", "Time": "2025-01-15T10:00:00Z", "State": "ProcessBatch"}, {"Type": "TaskStateExited", "Time": "2025-01-15T10:02:30Z", "State": "ProcessBatch"}, {"Type": "TaskStateEntered", "Time": "2025-01-15T10:02:31Z", "State": "NotifyComplete"}, {"Type": "TaskStateExited", "Time": "2025-01-15T10:02:35Z", "State": "NotifyComplete"} ]
Django Context: # ProcessBatch: 2.5 minutes for JobPost.objects # NotifyComplete: 4 seconds for Author.objects # Total workflow timing analysis

list-activities

Lists all activities in the account and region.

Syntax
aws stepfunctions list-activities \
    [--max-results <value>] \
    [--next-token <value>]
Parameters
  • --max-results (integer): Maximum activities to return (1-1000)
  • --next-token (string): Pagination token
Example 1: List All Activities

Get all Step Functions activities.

#!/bin/bash
aws stepfunctions list-activities
Output: { "activities": [ { "activityArn": "arn:aws:states:us-east-1:123456789012:activity:anihpj-jobpost-manual-review", "name": "anihpj-jobpost-manual-review", "creationDate": "2025-01-10T09:00:00Z" }, { "activityArn": "arn:aws:states:us-east-1:123456789012:activity:anihpj-skills-verification", "name": "anihpj-skills-verification", "creationDate": "2025-01-12T14:30:00Z" } ] }
Django Context: # All activities for anihpj app # JobPost.objects manual review activity # Skills.objects verification activity
Example 2: List with Pagination

Paginate through large list of activities.

#!/bin/bash
# Get first page
RESULT=$(aws stepfunctions list-activities --max-results 10)
echo "$RESULT" | jq '.activities[].name'
# Get next page if exists
NEXT_TOKEN=$(echo "$RESULT" | jq -r '.nextToken // empty')
if [ -n "$NEXT_TOKEN" ]; then
    aws stepfunctions list-activities --max-results 10 --next-token "$NEXT_TOKEN"
fi
Output: "anihpj-jobpost-manual-review" "anihpj-skills-verification" "anihpj-author-approval" "anihpj-location-validation"
Django Context: # Paginated activity list # Activities for JobPost, Skills, Author, Location # Supports large activity counts
Example 3: Filter Activities by Name

Find activities matching pattern.

#!/bin/bash
# Find all anihpj activities
aws stepfunctions list-activities \
    --query "activities[?contains(name, 'anihpj')].[name,creationDate]" \
    --output table
Output: ------------------------------------------------------------ | ListActivities | +--------------------------------+-------------------------+ | anihpj-jobpost-manual-review | 2025-01-10T09:00:00Z | | anihpj-skills-verification | 2025-01-12T14:30:00Z | | anihpj-author-approval | 2025-01-08T11:00:00Z | +--------------------------------+-------------------------+
Django Context: # All anihpj Django app activities # JobPost, Skills, Author activities found # Table format for easy reading
Example 4: Count Activities

Get total count of activities.

#!/bin/bash
# Count all activities
TOTAL=0
NEXT_TOKEN=""
while true; do
    if [ -z "$NEXT_TOKEN" ]; then
        RESULT=$(aws stepfunctions list-activities --max-results 100)
    else
        RESULT=$(aws stepfunctions list-activities --max-results 100 --next-token "$NEXT_TOKEN")
    fi
    COUNT=$(echo "$RESULT" | jq '.activities | length')
    TOTAL=$((TOTAL + COUNT))
    NEXT_TOKEN=$(echo "$RESULT" | jq -r '.nextToken // empty')
    [ -z "$NEXT_TOKEN" ] && break
done
echo "Total activities: $TOTAL"
Output: Total activities: 12
Django Context: # 12 total activities in account # Mix of JobPost, Skills, Author, Location # User.objects related activities included
Example 5: Export Activity ARNs

Export activity ARNs for use in scripts.

#!/bin/bash
# Export ARNs to file
aws stepfunctions list-activities \
    --query 'activities[].activityArn' \
    --output text > activity-arns.txt
cat activity-arns.txt
Output: arn:aws:states:us-east-1:123456789012:activity:anihpj-jobpost-manual-review arn:aws:states:us-east-1:123456789012:activity:anihpj-skills-verification arn:aws:states:us-east-1:123456789012:activity:anihpj-author-approval arn:aws:states:us-east-1:123456789012:activity:anihpj-location-validation
Django Context: # All activity ARNs exported # Use in automation scripts # Reference for workflow definitions

list-map-runs

Lists all Map Runs started by a child workflow execution.

Syntax
aws stepfunctions list-map-runs \
    --execution-arn <value> \
    [--max-results <value>] \
    [--next-token <value>]
Parameters
  • --execution-arn (string): ARN of the parent execution (required)
  • --max-results (integer): Maximum map runs to return
  • --next-token (string): Pagination token
Example 1: List Map Runs for Execution

Get all Map Runs from batch processing execution.

#!/bin/bash
aws stepfunctions list-map-runs \
    --execution-arn arn:aws:states:us-east-1:123456789012:execution:anihpj-batch-workflow:batch-2025-01-15
Output: { "mapRuns": [ { "executionArn": "arn:aws:states:us-east-1:123456789012:execution:anihpj-batch-workflow:batch-2025-01-15", "mapRunArn": "arn:aws:states:us-east-1:123456789012:mapRun:anihpj-batch-workflow/batch-2025-01-15:abc123", "stateMachineArn": "arn:aws:states:us-east-1:123456789012:stateMachine:anihpj-batch-workflow", "status": "SUCCEEDED", "startDate": "2025-01-15T02:00:00Z", "stopDate": "2025-01-15T02:45:00Z" } ] }
Django Context: # Map Run for JobPost.objects batch processing # Completed in 45 minutes # All Skills.objects validations successful
Example 2: Find Running Map Runs

List Map Runs that are still in progress.

#!/bin/bash
aws stepfunctions list-map-runs \
    --execution-arn arn:aws:states:us-east-1:123456789012:execution:anihpj-migrate-workflow:full-migration \
    --query "mapRuns[?status=='RUNNING']"
Output: [ { "mapRunArn": "arn:aws:states:...:mapRun:anihpj-migrate-workflow/full-migration:run001", "status": "RUNNING", "startDate": "2025-01-15T10:00:00Z" } ]
Django Context: # Active migration for JobPost.objects # Location.objects updates in progress # Author.objects migration running
Example 3: Get Map Run Count

Count Map Runs for an execution.

#!/bin/bash
aws stepfunctions list-map-runs \
    --execution-arn arn:aws:states:us-east-1:123456789012:execution:anihpj-multi-batch:multi-2025-01-15 \
    --query 'length(mapRuns)'
Output: 3
Django Context: # 3 Map Runs in multi-batch execution # Parallel JobPost, Skills, Location processing # Nested distributed Map states
Example 4: List Map Runs with Status Summary

Get status summary of all Map Runs.

#!/bin/bash
aws stepfunctions list-map-runs \
    --execution-arn arn:aws:states:us-east-1:123456789012:execution:anihpj-daily-process:daily-run \
    --query 'mapRuns[].{ARN:mapRunArn,Status:status,Start:startDate}'
Output: [ {"ARN": "...:mapRun:process-jobposts:abc", "Status": "SUCCEEDED", "Start": "2025-01-15T02:00:00Z"}, {"ARN": "...:mapRun:process-skills:def", "Status": "SUCCEEDED", "Start": "2025-01-15T02:30:00Z"}, {"ARN": "...:mapRun:notify-authors:ghi", "Status": "SUCCEEDED", "Start": "2025-01-15T03:00:00Z"} ]
Django Context: # Daily processing Map Runs # JobPost -> Skills -> Author notifications # All stages completed successfully
Example 5: Monitor Map Run Progress

Track Map Run completion over time.

#!/bin/bash
EXEC_ARN="arn:aws:states:us-east-1:123456789012:execution:anihpj-batch:current-batch"
# Check Map Runs status periodically
while true; do
    STATUS=$(aws stepfunctions list-map-runs --execution-arn "$EXEC_ARN" \
        --query 'mapRuns[0].status' --output text)
    echo "$(date): Map Run status: $STATUS"
    [ "$STATUS" == "SUCCEEDED" ] || [ "$STATUS" == "FAILED" ] && break
    sleep 30
done
Output: Fri Jan 15 10:00:00 2025: Map Run status: RUNNING Fri Jan 15 10:00:30 2025: Map Run status: RUNNING Fri Jan 15 10:01:00 2025: Map Run status: SUCCEEDED
Django Context: # Monitoring JobPost.objects batch progress # 1 minute to completion # Skills.objects validation complete

list-state-machine-aliases

Lists all aliases for a state machine.

Syntax
aws stepfunctions list-state-machine-aliases \
    --state-machine-arn <value> \
    [--max-results <value>] \
    [--next-token <value>]
Parameters
  • --state-machine-arn (string): ARN of the state machine (required)
  • --max-results (integer): Maximum aliases to return
  • --next-token (string): Pagination token
Example 1: List All Aliases

Get all aliases for a workflow.

#!/bin/bash
aws stepfunctions list-state-machine-aliases \
    --state-machine-arn arn:aws:states:us-east-1:123456789012:stateMachine:anihpj-jobpost-approval
Output: { "stateMachineAliases": [ { "stateMachineAliasArn": "arn:aws:states:us-east-1:123456789012:stateMachine:anihpj-jobpost-approval:prod", "name": "prod", "creationDate": "2025-01-01T00:00:00Z" }, { "stateMachineAliasArn": "arn:aws:states:us-east-1:123456789012:stateMachine:anihpj-jobpost-approval:staging", "name": "staging", "creationDate": "2025-01-05T00:00:00Z" } ] }
Django Context: # JobPost.objects workflow aliases # prod and staging environments # Different versions for Skills.objects testing
Example 2: Get Alias Names Only

Extract just alias names for scripting.

#!/bin/bash
aws stepfunctions list-state-machine-aliases \
    --state-machine-arn arn:aws:states:us-east-1:123456789012:stateMachine:anihpj-skills-matcher \
    --query 'stateMachineAliases[].name' \
    --output text
Output: prod staging canary dev
Django Context: # Skills.objects matcher has 4 aliases # prod, staging, canary, dev environments # Different deployment stages
Example 3: Check for Production Alias

Verify production alias exists.

#!/bin/bash
PROD_EXISTS=$(aws stepfunctions list-state-machine-aliases \
    --state-machine-arn arn:aws:states:us-east-1:123456789012:stateMachine:anihpj-author-onboard \
    --query "stateMachineAliases[?name=='prod'] | length(@)")
if [ "$PROD_EXISTS" -gt 0 ]; then
    echo "Production alias exists"
else
    echo "No production alias - creating..."
fi
Output: Production alias exists
Django Context: # Author.objects onboarding has prod alias # User.objects workflow ready for production # Deployment validation check
Example 4: List Aliases with Creation Dates

Get aliases sorted by creation date.

#!/bin/bash
aws stepfunctions list-state-machine-aliases \
    --state-machine-arn arn:aws:states:us-east-1:123456789012:stateMachine:anihpj-location-verify \
    --query 'sort_by(stateMachineAliases, &creationDate)[].{Name:name,Created:creationDate}' \
    --output table
Output: ----------------------------------------- | ListStateMachineAliases | +---------+-----------------------------+ | Name | Created | +---------+-----------------------------+ | prod | 2024-12-01T00:00:00Z | | staging| 2025-01-01T00:00:00Z | | canary | 2025-01-10T00:00:00Z | +---------+-----------------------------+
Django Context: # Location.objects verification aliases # prod oldest, canary newest # Progressive deployment history
Example 5: Export Alias ARNs

Export all alias ARNs for infrastructure scripts.

#!/bin/bash
aws stepfunctions list-state-machine-aliases \
    --state-machine-arn arn:aws:states:us-east-1:123456789012:stateMachine:anihpj-batch-process \
    --query 'stateMachineAliases[].stateMachineAliasArn' \
    --output json > aliases.json
cat aliases.json
Output: [ "arn:aws:states:us-east-1:123456789012:stateMachine:anihpj-batch-process:prod", "arn:aws:states:us-east-1:123456789012:stateMachine:anihpj-batch-process:staging" ]
Django Context: # Batch JobPost.objects workflow aliases # Export for Terraform/CloudFormation # Infrastructure as code integration

list-state-machine-versions

Lists all versions of a state machine.

Syntax
aws stepfunctions list-state-machine-versions \
    --state-machine-arn <value> \
    [--max-results <value>] \
    [--next-token <value>]
Parameters
  • --state-machine-arn (string): ARN of the state machine (required)
  • --max-results (integer): Maximum versions to return
  • --next-token (string): Pagination token
Example 1: List All Versions

Get all published versions of a workflow.

#!/bin/bash
aws stepfunctions list-state-machine-versions \
    --state-machine-arn arn:aws:states:us-east-1:123456789012:stateMachine:anihpj-jobpost-approval
Output: { "stateMachineVersions": [ { "stateMachineVersionArn": "arn:aws:states:us-east-1:123456789012:stateMachine:anihpj-jobpost-approval:1", "creationDate": "2025-01-01T00:00:00Z" }, { "stateMachineVersionArn": "arn:aws:states:us-east-1:123456789012:stateMachine:anihpj-jobpost-approval:2", "creationDate": "2025-01-08T00:00:00Z" }, { "stateMachineVersionArn": "arn:aws:states:us-east-1:123456789012:stateMachine:anihpj-jobpost-approval:3", "creationDate": "2025-01-15T00:00:00Z" } ] }
Django Context: # 3 versions of JobPost.objects approval # Weekly version releases # Skills.objects matching improvements
Example 2: Get Latest Version

Find the most recent version number.

#!/bin/bash
aws stepfunctions list-state-machine-versions \
    --state-machine-arn arn:aws:states:us-east-1:123456789012:stateMachine:anihpj-skills-matcher \
    --query 'stateMachineVersions[-1].stateMachineVersionArn' \
    --output text
Output: arn:aws:states:us-east-1:123456789012:stateMachine:anihpj-skills-matcher:5
Django Context: # Latest Skills.objects matcher is version 5 # Use for new alias routing # Most recent workflow improvements
Example 3: Count Versions

Get total number of published versions.

#!/bin/bash
VERSION_COUNT=$(aws stepfunctions list-state-machine-versions \
    --state-machine-arn arn:aws:states:us-east-1:123456789012:stateMachine:anihpj-author-onboard \
    --query 'length(stateMachineVersions)')
echo "Total versions: $VERSION_COUNT"
Output: Total versions: 4
Django Context: # 4 versions of Author.objects onboarding # User.objects workflow iterations # Version history for rollback capability
Example 4: List Versions with Dates

Get version history with creation timestamps.

#!/bin/bash
aws stepfunctions list-state-machine-versions \
    --state-machine-arn arn:aws:states:us-east-1:123456789012:stateMachine:anihpj-location-verify \
    --query 'stateMachineVersions[].{Version:stateMachineVersionArn,Created:creationDate}' \
    --output table
Output: --------------------------------------------------------------------- | ListStateMachineVersions | +--------------------------------------------------+-----------------+ | Version | Created | +--------------------------------------------------+-----------------+ | ...:anihpj-location-verify:1 | 2024-11-01 | | ...:anihpj-location-verify:2 | 2024-12-01 | | ...:anihpj-location-verify:3 | 2025-01-01 | +--------------------------------------------------+-----------------+
Django Context: # Location.objects verification history # Monthly version releases # Progressive JobPost.location improvements
Example 5: Find Version for Rollback

Identify previous version for rollback.

#!/bin/bash
# Get second-to-last version for potential rollback
aws stepfunctions list-state-machine-versions \
    --state-machine-arn arn:aws:states:us-east-1:123456789012:stateMachine:anihpj-batch-process \
    --query 'stateMachineVersions[-2]'
Output: { "stateMachineVersionArn": "arn:aws:states:us-east-1:123456789012:stateMachine:anihpj-batch-process:2", "creationDate": "2025-01-08T00:00:00Z" }
Django Context: # Previous version for JobPost.objects batch # Rollback target if v3 has issues # Skills.objects processing fallback

list-state-machines

Lists all state machines in the account and region.

Syntax
aws stepfunctions list-state-machines \
    [--max-results <value>] \
    [--next-token <value>]
Parameters
  • --max-results (integer): Maximum state machines to return (1-1000)
  • --next-token (string): Pagination token
Example 1: List All State Machines

Get all workflows in the account.

#!/bin/bash
aws stepfunctions list-state-machines
Output: { "stateMachines": [ { "stateMachineArn": "arn:aws:states:us-east-1:123456789012:stateMachine:anihpj-jobpost-approval", "name": "anihpj-jobpost-approval", "type": "STANDARD", "creationDate": "2025-01-01T00:00:00Z" }, { "stateMachineArn": "arn:aws:states:us-east-1:123456789012:stateMachine:anihpj-skills-matcher", "name": "anihpj-skills-matcher", "type": "STANDARD", "creationDate": "2025-01-05T00:00:00Z" } ] }
Django Context: # All workflows for anihpj Django app # JobPost approval and Skills matching # STANDARD type for long-running workflows
Example 2: Filter by Name Pattern

Find all anihpj-related workflows.

#!/bin/bash
aws stepfunctions list-state-machines \
    --query "stateMachines[?contains(name, 'anihpj')].[name,type]" \
    --output table
Output: ------------------------------------------ | ListStateMachines | +-----------------------------+----------+ | anihpj-jobpost-approval | STANDARD | | anihpj-skills-matcher | STANDARD | | anihpj-author-onboard | STANDARD | | anihpj-quick-validate | EXPRESS | +-----------------------------+----------+
Django Context: # 4 workflows for anihpj application # 3 STANDARD, 1 EXPRESS type # Covers JobPost, Skills, Author, validation
Example 3: List Express Workflows

Find all Express-type state machines.

#!/bin/bash
aws stepfunctions list-state-machines \
    --query "stateMachines[?type=='EXPRESS']"
Output: [ { "stateMachineArn": "arn:aws:states:us-east-1:123456789012:stateMachine:anihpj-quick-validate", "name": "anihpj-quick-validate", "type": "EXPRESS", "creationDate": "2025-01-10T00:00:00Z" } ]
Django Context: # Express workflows for fast processing # Quick JobPost.objects validation # Sub-second Skills.objects checks
Example 4: Count State Machines

Get total number of workflows.

#!/bin/bash
TOTAL=$(aws stepfunctions list-state-machines \
    --query 'length(stateMachines)')
echo "Total state machines: $TOTAL"
# Count by type
EXPRESS=$(aws stepfunctions list-state-machines \
    --query "length(stateMachines[?type=='EXPRESS'])")
STANDARD=$((TOTAL - EXPRESS))
echo "STANDARD: $STANDARD, EXPRESS: $EXPRESS"
Output: Total state machines: 8 STANDARD: 7, EXPRESS: 1
Django Context: # 8 total workflows for anihpj # 7 long-running JobPost/Author flows # 1 fast validation for Skills.objects
Example 5: Export for Infrastructure

Export workflow ARNs for deployment scripts.

#!/bin/bash
# Export all state machine ARNs to JSON
aws stepfunctions list-state-machines \
    --query 'stateMachines[].{name:name,arn:stateMachineArn,type:type}' \
    --output json > state-machines.json
echo "Exported $(jq length state-machines.json) state machines"
Output: Exported 8 state machines
Django Context: # All JobPost, Skills, Author, Location workflows # Export for CI/CD pipeline reference # Infrastructure documentation

list-tags-for-resource

Lists tags attached to a Step Functions resource.

Syntax
aws stepfunctions list-tags-for-resource \
    --resource-arn <value>
Parameters
  • --resource-arn (string): ARN of the resource (required)
Example 1: List State Machine Tags

Get tags for a workflow.

#!/bin/bash
aws stepfunctions list-tags-for-resource \
    --resource-arn arn:aws:states:us-east-1:123456789012:stateMachine:anihpj-jobpost-approval
Output: { "tags": [ {"key": "Environment", "value": "production"}, {"key": "Application", "value": "anihpj"}, {"key": "Team", "value": "backend"}, {"key": "CostCenter", "value": "engineering"} ] }
Django Context: # Tags for JobPost.objects approval workflow # Production environment, anihpj app # Backend team ownership for cost tracking
Example 2: List Activity Tags

Get tags for an activity.

#!/bin/bash
aws stepfunctions list-tags-for-resource \
    --resource-arn arn:aws:states:us-east-1:123456789012:activity:anihpj-skills-verification
Output: { "tags": [ {"key": "Environment", "value": "production"}, {"key": "ProcessType", "value": "manual-review"} ] }
Django Context: # Skills.objects verification activity tags # Manual review process type # Production environment activity
Example 3: Check for Required Tags

Verify compliance tags exist.

#!/bin/bash
TAGS=$(aws stepfunctions list-tags-for-resource \
    --resource-arn arn:aws:states:us-east-1:123456789012:stateMachine:anihpj-author-onboard \
    --query 'tags[?key==`CostCenter`].value' --output text)
if [ -n "$TAGS" ]; then
    echo "CostCenter tag: $TAGS"
else
    echo "WARNING: Missing required CostCenter tag"
fi
Output: CostCenter tag: engineering
Django Context: # Author.objects workflow compliance check # CostCenter tag present for billing # User.objects onboarding costs tracked
Example 4: Export Tags to JSON

Export tags for documentation.

#!/bin/bash
aws stepfunctions list-tags-for-resource \
    --resource-arn arn:aws:states:us-east-1:123456789012:stateMachine:anihpj-location-verify \
    --query 'tags' --output json > workflow-tags.json
cat workflow-tags.json
Output: [ {"key": "Environment", "value": "production"}, {"key": "DataClassification", "value": "pii"}, {"key": "GDPRCompliant", "value": "true"} ]
Django Context: # Location.objects verification tags # PII data classification for addresses # GDPR compliance for JobPost.location
Example 5: Compare Tags Across Resources

Audit tags across multiple workflows.

#!/bin/bash
for sm in "jobpost-approval" "skills-matcher" "author-onboard"; do
    echo "=== anihpj-$sm ==="
    aws stepfunctions list-tags-for-resource \
        --resource-arn "arn:aws:states:us-east-1:123456789012:stateMachine:anihpj-$sm" \
        --query 'tags[].{Key:key,Value:value}' --output table
done
Output: === anihpj-jobpost-approval === ----------------------------------- | Key | Value | +---------------+-----------------+ | Environment | production | | Application | anihpj | ----------------------------------- === anihpj-skills-matcher === ...
Django Context: # Tag audit across JobPost, Skills, Author # Consistent tagging for governance # Infrastructure compliance report

publish-state-machine-version

Publishes a new version of the state machine from the current revision.

Syntax
aws stepfunctions publish-state-machine-version \
    --state-machine-arn <value> \
    [--revision-id <value>] \
    [--description <value>]
Parameters
  • --state-machine-arn (string): ARN of the state machine (required)
  • --revision-id (string): Revision ID for optimistic concurrency
  • --description (string): Description of the version
Example 1: Publish New Version

Publish current workflow as new version.

#!/bin/bash
aws stepfunctions publish-state-machine-version \
    --state-machine-arn arn:aws:states:us-east-1:123456789012:stateMachine:anihpj-jobpost-approval \
    --description "Added Skills validation step for JobPost approval"
Output: { "stateMachineVersionArn": "arn:aws:states:us-east-1:123456789012:stateMachine:anihpj-jobpost-approval:4", "creationDate": "2025-01-15T12:00:00Z" }
Django Context: # Version 4 published for JobPost.objects # New Skills.objects validation added # Ready for alias routing update
Example 2: Publish with Revision Check

Publish version with optimistic concurrency.

#!/bin/bash
# Get current revision ID
REVISION=$(aws stepfunctions describe-state-machine \
    --state-machine-arn arn:aws:states:us-east-1:123456789012:stateMachine:anihpj-skills-matcher \
    --query 'revisionId' --output text)
# Publish with revision check
aws stepfunctions publish-state-machine-version \
    --state-machine-arn arn:aws:states:us-east-1:123456789012:stateMachine:anihpj-skills-matcher \
    --revision-id "$REVISION" \
    --description "Improved matching algorithm"
Output: { "stateMachineVersionArn": "arn:aws:states:us-east-1:123456789012:stateMachine:anihpj-skills-matcher:6", "creationDate": "2025-01-15T12:05:00Z" }
Django Context: # Skills.objects matcher version 6 # Concurrency-safe publish # No race condition with other updates
Example 3: Publish for Deployment Pipeline

Publish version as part of CI/CD.

#!/bin/bash
# CI/CD publish step
VERSION_ARN=$(aws stepfunctions publish-state-machine-version \
    --state-machine-arn arn:aws:states:us-east-1:123456789012:stateMachine:anihpj-author-onboard \
    --description "Release $(date +%Y-%m-%d): User validation updates" \
    --query 'stateMachineVersionArn' --output text)
echo "Published: $VERSION_ARN"
# Store for later alias update
echo "$VERSION_ARN" > published-version.txt
Output: Published: arn:aws:states:us-east-1:123456789012:stateMachine:anihpj-author-onboard:5
Django Context: # Author.objects onboarding version 5 # CI/CD automated publishing # User.objects validation improvements
Example 4: Publish After Update

Update workflow then publish new version.

#!/bin/bash
SM_ARN="arn:aws:states:us-east-1:123456789012:stateMachine:anihpj-location-verify"
# Update state machine (definition changed externally)
aws stepfunctions update-state-machine \
    --state-machine-arn "$SM_ARN" \
    --definition file://new-definition.json
# Publish the update as new version
aws stepfunctions publish-state-machine-version \
    --state-machine-arn "$SM_ARN" \
    --description "Added geocoding retry logic"
Output: { "stateMachineVersionArn": "arn:aws:states:us-east-1:123456789012:stateMachine:anihpj-location-verify:4", "creationDate": "2025-01-15T12:10:00Z" }
Django Context: # Location.objects verification version 4 # Geocoding retry for JobPost.location # Improved reliability for address validation
Example 5: Publish and Update Canary

Publish version and route canary traffic.

#!/bin/bash
SM_ARN="arn:aws:states:us-east-1:123456789012:stateMachine:anihpj-batch-process"
# Publish new version
NEW_VERSION=$(aws stepfunctions publish-state-machine-version \
    --state-machine-arn "$SM_ARN" \
    --description "Batch performance optimization" \
    --query 'stateMachineVersionArn' --output text)
# Get current prod version
PROD_VERSION="${SM_ARN}:3"
# Update canary alias with 10% new version
aws stepfunctions update-state-machine-alias \
    --state-machine-alias-arn "${SM_ARN}:canary" \
    --routing-configuration "[{\"stateMachineVersionArn\":\"$PROD_VERSION\",\"weight\":90},{\"stateMachineVersionArn\":\"$NEW_VERSION\",\"weight\":10}]"
Output: { "updateDate": "2025-01-15T12:15:00Z" }
Django Context: # Batch JobPost.objects new version # 10% canary traffic to test # Skills.objects batch optimization

redrive-execution

Restarts a failed or timed out execution from the point of failure.

Syntax
aws stepfunctions redrive-execution \
    --execution-arn <value> \
    [--client-token <value>]
Parameters
  • --execution-arn (string): ARN of the failed execution (required)
  • --client-token (string): Idempotency token
Example 1: Redrive Failed Execution

Retry failed job post approval from failure point.

#!/bin/bash
aws stepfunctions redrive-execution \
    --execution-arn arn:aws:states:us-east-1:123456789012:execution:anihpj-jobpost-approval:failed-job-123
Output: { "redriveDate": "2025-01-15T13:00:00Z" }
Django Context: # Redriving failed JobPost.objects.get(pk=123) # Continues from last failed state # Skills.objects validation retry
Example 2: Redrive with Idempotency

Safely redrive with client token.

#!/bin/bash
# Idempotent redrive - safe to call multiple times
aws stepfunctions redrive-execution \
    --execution-arn arn:aws:states:us-east-1:123456789012:execution:anihpj-skills-matcher:failed-match-456 \
    --client-token "redrive-$(date +%Y%m%d)-match-456"
Output: { "redriveDate": "2025-01-15T13:05:00Z" }
Django Context: # Safe Skills.objects match retry # Idempotent - prevents duplicate redrives # Client token ensures single retry
Example 3: Batch Redrive Failed Executions

Redrive multiple failed executions.

#!/bin/bash
# Find and redrive all failed executions
aws stepfunctions list-executions \
    --state-machine-arn arn:aws:states:us-east-1:123456789012:stateMachine:anihpj-author-onboard \
    --status-filter FAILED \
    --query 'executions[].executionArn' --output text | while read arn; do
    echo "Redriving: $arn"
    aws stepfunctions redrive-execution --execution-arn "$arn"
done
Output: Redriving: arn:aws:states:...:failed-author-101 {"redriveDate": "2025-01-15T13:10:00Z"} Redriving: arn:aws:states:...:failed-author-102 {"redriveDate": "2025-01-15T13:10:01Z"}
Django Context: # Batch retry Author.objects failures # User.objects onboarding recovery # Multiple failed registrations reprocessed
Example 4: Redrive Timed Out Execution

Retry execution that timed out.

#!/bin/bash
# Redrive timed out location verification
aws stepfunctions redrive-execution \
    --execution-arn arn:aws:states:us-east-1:123456789012:execution:anihpj-location-verify:timeout-loc-789
Output: { "redriveDate": "2025-01-15T13:15:00Z" }
Django Context: # Location.objects verification timed out # Geocoding service was slow # Retry from timeout state
Example 5: Redrive with Monitoring

Redrive and monitor completion.

#!/bin/bash
EXEC_ARN="arn:aws:states:us-east-1:123456789012:execution:anihpj-batch-process:failed-batch-001"
# Redrive the execution
aws stepfunctions redrive-execution --execution-arn "$EXEC_ARN"
echo "Redriven, monitoring..."
# Monitor until complete
while true; do
    STATUS=$(aws stepfunctions describe-execution \
        --execution-arn "$EXEC_ARN" \
        --query 'status' --output text)
    echo "Status: $STATUS"
    [ "$STATUS" == "SUCCEEDED" ] || [ "$STATUS" == "FAILED" ] && break
    sleep 10
done
Output: Redriven, monitoring... Status: RUNNING Status: RUNNING Status: SUCCEEDED
Django Context: # Batch JobPost.objects redrive monitored # Completed successfully on retry # Skills.objects batch recovered

send-task-failure

Reports that an activity task has failed to Step Functions.

Syntax
aws stepfunctions send-task-failure \
    --task-token <value> \
    [--error <value>] \
    [--cause <value>]
Parameters
  • --task-token (string): Token from get-activity-task (required)
  • --error (string): Error code for the failure
  • --cause (string): Detailed cause of failure
Example 1: Report Validation Failure

Report job post validation failure.

#!/bin/bash
aws stepfunctions send-task-failure \
    --task-token "AAAAKgAAAAIAAAAAAAAAAX9Z..." \
    --error "ValidationError" \
    --cause "JobPost missing required skills: Python, Django"
Output: {}
Django Context: # JobPost.objects validation failed # Missing required Skills.objects entries # Workflow will handle error state
Example 2: Report External Service Error

Report failure from external API.

#!/bin/bash
aws stepfunctions send-task-failure \
    --task-token "$TASK_TOKEN" \
    --error "ExternalServiceError" \
    --cause "Geocoding API returned 503 Service Unavailable"
Output: {}
Django Context: # Location.objects geocoding failed # External API unavailable # JobPost.location verification retry needed
Example 3: Report Timeout in Worker

Worker reports processing timeout.

#!/bin/bash
# Worker processing exceeded time limit
aws stepfunctions send-task-failure \
    --task-token "$TASK_TOKEN" \
    --error "WorkerTimeout" \
    --cause "Skills verification took longer than 5 minutes"
Output: {}
Django Context: # Skills.objects verification timeout # Worker exceeded processing limit # Complex skill matching took too long
Example 4: Report Data Error

Report invalid data format error.

#!/bin/bash
aws stepfunctions send-task-failure \
    --task-token "$TASK_TOKEN" \
    --error "DataFormatError" \
    --cause "Author email format invalid: author@invalid"
Output: {}
Django Context: # Author.objects email validation failed # Invalid email format detected # User.objects creation blocked
Example 5: Report in Worker Error Handler

Structured error handling in worker.

#!/bin/bash
# Worker error handler
process_task() {
    local TOKEN="$1"
    local INPUT="$2"
    
    # Try processing
    if ! validate_jobpost "$INPUT"; then
        aws stepfunctions send-task-failure \
            --task-token "$TOKEN" \
            --error "ProcessingFailed" \
            --cause "$(cat /tmp/error.log)"
        return 1
    fi
}
# Example call
process_task "$TASK_TOKEN" '{"jobpost_id": 999}'
Output: {}
Django Context: # Worker JobPost.objects processing failed # Error logged and reported to Step Functions # Workflow error handling triggered

send-task-heartbeat

Sends a heartbeat to keep an activity task alive.

Syntax
aws stepfunctions send-task-heartbeat \
    --task-token <value>
Parameters
  • --task-token (string): Token from get-activity-task (required)
Example 1: Send Heartbeat During Processing

Keep task alive during long processing.

#!/bin/bash
aws stepfunctions send-task-heartbeat \
    --task-token "AAAAKgAAAAIAAAAAAAAAAX9Z..."
Output: {}
Django Context: # Heartbeat sent for JobPost.objects task # Prevents timeout during long validation # Skills.objects processing continues
Example 2: Heartbeat in Processing Loop

Send heartbeats during batch processing.

#!/bin/bash
# Process job posts with heartbeat
process_batch() {
    local TOKEN="$1"
    local JOBPOST_IDS="$2"
    
    for id in $JOBPOST_IDS; do
        echo "Processing JobPost $id"
        # Send heartbeat every iteration
        aws stepfunctions send-task-heartbeat --task-token "$TOKEN"
        sleep 2  # Simulate processing
    done
}
process_batch "$TASK_TOKEN" "101 102 103 104 105"
Output: Processing JobPost 101 Processing JobPost 102 Processing JobPost 103 ...
Django Context: # Batch JobPost.objects.filter(pk__in=[...]) # Heartbeat keeps task from timing out # Each iteration signals activity
Example 3: Heartbeat Thread

Background heartbeat while processing.

#!/bin/bash
# Start heartbeat in background
send_heartbeats() {
    while true; do
        aws stepfunctions send-task-heartbeat --task-token "$1" 2>/dev/null || break
        sleep 30
    done
}
# Start background heartbeats
send_heartbeats "$TASK_TOKEN" &
HEARTBEAT_PID=$!
# Do actual work
process_skills_verification
# Stop heartbeats
kill $HEARTBEAT_PID 2>/dev/null
Output: {}
Django Context: # Background heartbeat for Skills.objects work # 30-second intervals prevent timeout # Clean shutdown after processing
Example 4: Heartbeat with Progress Logging

Log progress with each heartbeat.

#!/bin/bash
# Heartbeat with progress tracking
TOTAL=100
PROCESSED=0
while [ $PROCESSED -lt $TOTAL ]; do
    # Process item
    ((PROCESSED++))
    # Heartbeat every 10 items
    if [ $((PROCESSED % 10)) -eq 0 ]; then
        aws stepfunctions send-task-heartbeat --task-token "$TASK_TOKEN"
        echo "Progress: $PROCESSED/$TOTAL"
    fi
done
Output: Progress: 10/100 Progress: 20/100 Progress: 30/100 ...
Django Context: # Location.objects batch validation # Heartbeat every 10% progress # JobPost.location updates tracked
Example 5: Heartbeat in Author Verification

Keep alive during manual verification steps.

#!/bin/bash
# Long-running author verification
verify_author() {
    local TOKEN="$1"
    local AUTHOR_ID="$2"
    
    # Check documents (slow)
    aws stepfunctions send-task-heartbeat --task-token "$TOKEN"
    verify_documents "$AUTHOR_ID"
    
    # Verify company (slow)
    aws stepfunctions send-task-heartbeat --task-token "$TOKEN"
    verify_company "$AUTHOR_ID"
    
    # Background check (slow)
    aws stepfunctions send-task-heartbeat --task-token "$TOKEN"
    background_check "$AUTHOR_ID"
}
verify_author "$TASK_TOKEN" "67"
Output: {}
Django Context: # Author.objects.get(pk=67) verification # Multiple slow steps with heartbeats # User.objects background check process

send-task-success

Reports that an activity task has completed successfully.

Syntax
aws stepfunctions send-task-success \
    --task-token <value> \
    --output <value>
Parameters
  • --task-token (string): Token from get-activity-task (required)
  • --output (string): JSON output to return (required)
Example 1: Report Successful Validation

Report job post validated successfully.

#!/bin/bash
aws stepfunctions send-task-success \
    --task-token "AAAAKgAAAAIAAAAAAAAAAX9Z..." \
    --output '{"validated": true, "jobpost_id": 12345, "skills_matched": 5}'
Output: {}
Django Context: # JobPost.objects.get(pk=12345) validated # 5 Skills.objects requirements matched # Workflow continues to next state
Example 2: Report Skills Verification Success

Complete skills verification task.

#!/bin/bash
aws stepfunctions send-task-success \
    --task-token "$TASK_TOKEN" \
    --output '{
        "verified": true,
        "skills": ["Python", "Django", "AWS"],
        "match_score": 95,
        "recommendation": "highly_qualified"
    }'
Output: {}
Django Context: # Skills.objects verification complete # 95% match score calculated # JobPost candidate highly qualified
Example 3: Report Author Approval

Complete author approval task.

#!/bin/bash
aws stepfunctions send-task-success \
    --task-token "$TASK_TOKEN" \
    --output '{
        "approved": true,
        "author_id": 67,
        "approval_date": "2025-01-15",
        "approved_by": "admin@anihpj.com"
    }'
Output: {}
Django Context: # Author.objects.get(pk=67) approved # User.objects account activated # Ready for JobPost.objects creation
Example 4: Report Location Validation

Complete location geocoding task.

#!/bin/bash
aws stepfunctions send-task-success \
    --task-token "$TASK_TOKEN" \
    --output '{
        "location_id": 456,
        "validated": true,
        "coordinates": {"lat": 40.7128, "lng": -74.0060},
        "formatted_address": "New York, NY 10001, USA"
    }'
Output: {}
Django Context: # Location.objects.get(pk=456) validated # Geocoding completed successfully # JobPost.location FK updated
Example 5: Report Batch Processing Complete

Complete batch processing task.

#!/bin/bash
# After processing batch
aws stepfunctions send-task-success \
    --task-token "$TASK_TOKEN" \
    --output '{
        "batch_id": "batch-2025-01-15",
        "processed": 100,
        "succeeded": 98,
        "failed": 2,
        "duration_seconds": 120,
        "failed_ids": [999, 1000]
    }'
Output: {}
Django Context: # Batch JobPost.objects processing done # 98% success rate (98/100) # Failed IDs: 999, 1000 for review

start-sync-execution

Starts a synchronous Express workflow execution and waits for completion.

Syntax
aws stepfunctions start-sync-execution \
    --state-machine-arn <value> \
    [--name <value>] \
    [--input <value>] \
    [--trace-header <value>]
Parameters
  • --state-machine-arn (string): ARN of Express state machine (required)
  • --name (string): Name for the execution
  • --input (string): JSON input data
  • --trace-header (string): X-Ray trace header
Example 1: Quick JobPost Validation

Synchronous validation returning immediately.

#!/bin/bash
aws stepfunctions start-sync-execution \
    --state-machine-arn arn:aws:states:us-east-1:123456789012:stateMachine:anihpj-quick-validate \
    --input '{"jobpost_id": 12345, "check_skills": true}'
Output: { "executionArn": "arn:aws:states:us-east-1:123456789012:express:anihpj-quick-validate:exec-123:abc", "stateMachineArn": "arn:aws:states:us-east-1:123456789012:stateMachine:anihpj-quick-validate", "name": "exec-123", "startDate": "2025-01-15T14:00:00Z", "stopDate": "2025-01-15T14:00:00.500Z", "status": "SUCCEEDED", "output": "{\"valid\": true, \"jobpost_id\": 12345}" }
Django Context: # Instant JobPost.objects.get(pk=12345) validation # 500ms response time # Skills.objects check completed synchronously
Example 2: Real-time Skills Check

Synchronous skills matching for API response.

#!/bin/bash
# Called from API endpoint for instant response
aws stepfunctions start-sync-execution \
    --state-machine-arn arn:aws:states:us-east-1:123456789012:stateMachine:anihpj-express-skills \
    --name "api-check-$(date +%s%N)" \
    --input '{"skills": ["Python", "AWS"], "min_match": 2}'
Output: { "status": "SUCCEEDED", "output": "{\"matches\": 15, \"top_match\": {\"candidate_id\": 101, \"score\": 98}}" }
Django Context: # Real-time Skills.objects matching # 15 candidates found with Python + AWS # Top match: 98% score for API response
Example 3: Author Eligibility Check

Quick eligibility verification.

#!/bin/bash
aws stepfunctions start-sync-execution \
    --state-machine-arn arn:aws:states:us-east-1:123456789012:stateMachine:anihpj-author-eligible \
    --input '{"author_id": 67, "action": "post_job"}'
Output: { "status": "SUCCEEDED", "output": "{\"eligible\": true, \"author_id\": 67, \"remaining_posts\": 5}" }
Django Context: # Author.objects.get(pk=67) eligibility # Can post jobs, 5 remaining in quota # User.objects permissions verified
Example 4: Location Quick Lookup

Synchronous location validation.

#!/bin/bash
aws stepfunctions start-sync-execution \
    --state-machine-arn arn:aws:states:us-east-1:123456789012:stateMachine:anihpj-location-quick \
    --input '{"city": "New York", "state": "NY", "country": "USA"}'
Output: { "status": "SUCCEEDED", "output": "{\"location_id\": 456, \"exists\": true, \"timezone\": \"America/New_York\"}" }
Django Context: # Location.objects lookup for NY # Existing location_id: 456 found # JobPost.location FK ready to use
Example 5: With X-Ray Tracing

Synchronous execution with distributed tracing.

#!/bin/bash
