# With X-Ray trace header for distributed tracing
aws stepfunctions start-sync-execution \
    --state-machine-arn arn:aws:states:us-east-1:123456789012:stateMachine:anihpj-quick-validate \
    --input '{"jobpost_id": 789}' \
    --trace-header "Root=1-5f84c7a7-sample12345;Parent=parent123;Sampled=1"
Output: { "status": "SUCCEEDED", "output": "{\"valid\": true}", "traceHeader": "Root=1-5f84c7a7-sample12345;Parent=abc456;Sampled=1" }
Django Context: # Traced JobPost.objects validation # X-Ray shows full request path # Django -> API Gateway -> Step Functions

stop-execution

Stops a running state machine execution.

Syntax
aws stepfunctions stop-execution \
    --execution-arn <value> \
    [--error <value>] \
    [--cause <value>]
Parameters
  • --execution-arn (string): ARN of the execution to stop (required)
  • --error (string): Error code for the stop
  • --cause (string): Reason for stopping
Example 1: Stop Running Execution

Cancel job post approval in progress.

#!/bin/bash
aws stepfunctions stop-execution \
    --execution-arn arn:aws:states:us-east-1:123456789012:execution:anihpj-jobpost-approval:job-12345 \
    --error "UserCancelled" \
    --cause "Author requested cancellation of job post submission"
Output: { "stopDate": "2025-01-15T14:30:00Z" }
Django Context: # JobPost.objects.get(pk=12345) cancelled # Author.objects requested stop # Workflow aborted cleanly
Example 2: Stop Due to Data Error

Stop execution when invalid data detected.

#!/bin/bash
aws stepfunctions stop-execution \
    --execution-arn arn:aws:states:us-east-1:123456789012:execution:anihpj-skills-matcher:match-500 \
    --error "DataValidationFailed" \
    --cause "Skills list contains invalid entries"
Output: { "stopDate": "2025-01-15T14:35:00Z" }
Django Context: # Skills.objects matching stopped # Invalid skill names detected # JobPost requirements need correction
Example 3: Stop Batch Before Completion

Cancel batch processing job.

#!/bin/bash
aws stepfunctions stop-execution \
    --execution-arn arn:aws:states:us-east-1:123456789012:execution:anihpj-batch-process:batch-large \
    --error "MaintenanceWindow" \
    --cause "Stopping for scheduled maintenance"
Output: { "stopDate": "2025-01-15T14:40:00Z" }
Django Context: # Batch JobPost.objects processing stopped # Maintenance window approaching # Will resume after maintenance
Example 4: Stop All Running Executions

Emergency stop all active workflows.

#!/bin/bash
# Emergency stop all running executions
aws stepfunctions list-executions \
    --state-machine-arn arn:aws:states:us-east-1:123456789012:stateMachine:anihpj-author-onboard \
    --status-filter RUNNING \
    --query 'executions[].executionArn' --output text | while read arn; do
    aws stepfunctions stop-execution \
        --execution-arn "$arn" \
        --error "EmergencyStop" \
        --cause "System-wide emergency stop initiated"
    echo "Stopped: $arn"
done
Output: Stopped: arn:aws:states:...:author-101 Stopped: arn:aws:states:...:author-102
Django Context: # All Author.objects onboarding stopped # Emergency User.objects workflow halt # System maintenance or incident response
Example 5: Stop with Cleanup Trigger

Stop execution triggering cleanup handler.

#!/bin/bash
# Stop with specific error to trigger cleanup
aws stepfunctions stop-execution \
    --execution-arn arn:aws:states:us-east-1:123456789012:execution:anihpj-location-verify:loc-789 \
    --error "CleanupRequired" \
    --cause "Location data needs rollback"
Output: { "stopDate": "2025-01-15T14:45:00Z" }
Django Context: # Location.objects verification stopped # CleanupRequired triggers rollback handler # JobPost.location reverted to previous

tag-resource

Adds tags to a Step Functions resource.

Syntax
aws stepfunctions tag-resource \
    --resource-arn <value> \
    --tags <value>
Parameters
  • --resource-arn (string): ARN of the resource to tag (required)
  • --tags (list): Tags to add (required)
Example 1: Tag State Machine

Add environment and application tags.

#!/bin/bash
aws stepfunctions tag-resource \
    --resource-arn arn:aws:states:us-east-1:123456789012:stateMachine:anihpj-jobpost-approval \
    --tags key=Environment,value=production key=Application,value=anihpj key=Team,value=backend
Output: {}
Django Context: # JobPost.objects workflow tagged # Production environment, anihpj app # Backend team ownership assigned
Example 2: Tag Activity

Add tags to an activity resource.

#!/bin/bash
aws stepfunctions tag-resource \
    --resource-arn arn:aws:states:us-east-1:123456789012:activity:anihpj-skills-verification \
    --tags key=ProcessType,value=manual-review key=SLA,value=24h
Output: {}
Django Context: # Skills.objects verification activity tagged # Manual review process, 24h SLA # Tracking for performance metrics
Example 3: Add Cost Allocation Tags

Tag for cost tracking and billing.

#!/bin/bash
aws stepfunctions tag-resource \
    --resource-arn arn:aws:states:us-east-1:123456789012:stateMachine:anihpj-author-onboard \
    --tags key=CostCenter,value=HR-001 key=Project,value=author-onboarding key=BudgetCode,value=2025-HR
Output: {}
Django Context: # Author.objects onboarding cost tracking # HR department budget allocation # User.objects creation costs tracked
Example 4: Tag for Compliance

Add compliance and data classification tags.

#!/bin/bash
aws stepfunctions tag-resource \
    --resource-arn arn:aws:states:us-east-1:123456789012:stateMachine:anihpj-location-verify \
    --tags key=DataClassification,value=PII key=Compliance,value=GDPR key=DataRetention,value=90days
Output: {}
Django Context: # Location.objects workflow compliance tags # PII classification for address data # GDPR compliance for JobPost.location
Example 5: Batch Tag Multiple Resources

Apply standard tags to all workflows.

#!/bin/bash
# Tag all anihpj state machines
for sm in "jobpost-approval" "skills-matcher" "author-onboard" "location-verify"; do
    aws stepfunctions tag-resource \
        --resource-arn "arn:aws:states:us-east-1:123456789012:stateMachine:anihpj-$sm" \
        --tags key=Application,value=anihpj key=ManagedBy,value=terraform
    echo "Tagged: anihpj-$sm"
done
Output: Tagged: anihpj-jobpost-approval Tagged: anihpj-skills-matcher Tagged: anihpj-author-onboard Tagged: anihpj-location-verify
Django Context: # All Django app workflows tagged # JobPost, Skills, Author, Location # Terraform management tracking

test-state

Tests a single state definition by running it with mock input.

Syntax
aws stepfunctions test-state \
    --definition <value> \
    --role-arn <value> \
    [--input <value>] \
    [--inspection-level INFO|DEBUG|TRACE]
Parameters
  • --definition (string): State definition in ASL JSON (required)
  • --role-arn (string): IAM role for execution (required)
  • --input (string): JSON input for the test
  • --inspection-level (string): Level of detail in response
Example 1: Test Pass State

Test a simple Pass state transformation.

#!/bin/bash
aws stepfunctions test-state \
    --definition '{
        "Type": "Pass",
        "Result": {"status": "validated"},
        "ResultPath": "$.validation"
    }' \
    --role-arn arn:aws:iam::123456789012:role/StepFunctionsRole \
    --input '{"jobpost_id": 12345}'
Output: { "output": "{\"jobpost_id\": 12345, \"validation\": {\"status\": \"validated\"}}", "status": "SUCCEEDED" }
Django Context: # Test Pass state for JobPost.objects # Adds validation status to output # Safe testing before deployment
Example 2: Test Choice State

Test conditional branching logic.

#!/bin/bash
aws stepfunctions test-state \
    --definition '{
        "Type": "Choice",
        "Choices": [
            {
                "Variable": "$.skills_count",
                "NumericGreaterThan": 3,
                "Next": "HighMatch"
            }
        ],
        "Default": "LowMatch"
    }' \
    --role-arn arn:aws:iam::123456789012:role/StepFunctionsRole \
    --input '{"skills_count": 5}'
Output: { "output": "{\"skills_count\": 5}", "nextState": "HighMatch", "status": "SUCCEEDED" }
Django Context: # Test Skills.objects count condition # 5 > 3 so routes to HighMatch # JobPost matching logic validated
Example 3: Test Task State with Lambda

Test Lambda invocation state.

#!/bin/bash
aws stepfunctions test-state \
    --definition '{
        "Type": "Task",
        "Resource": "arn:aws:lambda:us-east-1:123456789012:function:validate-jobpost",
        "TimeoutSeconds": 30
    }' \
    --role-arn arn:aws:iam::123456789012:role/StepFunctionsRole \
    --input '{"jobpost_id": 789}' \
    --inspection-level DEBUG
Output: { "output": "{\"valid\": true, \"jobpost_id\": 789}", "status": "SUCCEEDED", "inspectionData": { "input": "{\"jobpost_id\": 789}", "afterInputPath": "..." } }
Django Context: # Test Lambda for JobPost.objects validation # Debug output shows state processing # 30 second timeout configured
Example 4: Test Wait State

Test time-based wait state.

#!/bin/bash
aws stepfunctions test-state \
    --definition '{
        "Type": "Wait",
        "Seconds": 5,
        "Next": "ContinueProcessing"
    }' \
    --role-arn arn:aws:iam::123456789012:role/StepFunctionsRole \
    --input '{"author_id": 67}'
Output: { "output": "{\"author_id\": 67}", "nextState": "ContinueProcessing", "status": "SUCCEEDED" }
Django Context: # Test 5-second wait state # Author.objects processing delay # Simulates cooldown period
Example 5: Test with TRACE Level

Full trace inspection for debugging.

#!/bin/bash
aws stepfunctions test-state \
    --definition '{
        "Type": "Pass",
        "InputPath": "$.location",
        "ResultPath": "$.verified_location",
        "Result": {"verified": true}
    }' \
    --role-arn arn:aws:iam::123456789012:role/StepFunctionsRole \
    --input '{"location": {"city": "NYC"}, "jobpost_id": 100}' \
    --inspection-level TRACE
Output: { "output": "{\"location\":{\"city\":\"NYC\"},\"jobpost_id\":100,\"verified_location\":{\"verified\":true}}", "status": "SUCCEEDED", "inspectionData": { "input": "{\"location\":{\"city\":\"NYC\"},\"jobpost_id\":100}", "afterInputPath": "{\"city\":\"NYC\"}", "result": "{\"verified\":true}", "afterResultPath": "..." } }
Django Context: # Full trace for Location.objects state # Shows InputPath and ResultPath processing # Debugging JobPost.location verification

untag-resource

Removes tags from a Step Functions resource.

Syntax
aws stepfunctions untag-resource \
    --resource-arn <value> \
    --tag-keys <value>
Parameters
  • --resource-arn (string): ARN of the resource (required)
  • --tag-keys (list): Tag keys to remove (required)
Example 1: Remove Single Tag

Remove deprecated tag from workflow.

#!/bin/bash
aws stepfunctions untag-resource \
    --resource-arn arn:aws:states:us-east-1:123456789012:stateMachine:anihpj-jobpost-approval \
    --tag-keys "DeprecatedTag"
Output: {}
Django Context: # Removed deprecated tag from JobPost.objects workflow # Cleanup old tagging convention # Governance compliance update
Example 2: Remove Multiple Tags

Remove several tags at once.

#!/bin/bash
aws stepfunctions untag-resource \
    --resource-arn arn:aws:states:us-east-1:123456789012:stateMachine:anihpj-skills-matcher \
    --tag-keys "OldTeam" "LegacyProject" "TempTag"
Output: {}
Django Context: # Cleaned up Skills.objects workflow tags # Removed legacy project references # Updated team ownership tags
Example 3: Remove Environment Tag

Clean up environment tag during migration.

#!/bin/bash
# Remove old environment tag before adding new one
aws stepfunctions untag-resource \
    --resource-arn arn:aws:states:us-east-1:123456789012:stateMachine:anihpj-author-onboard \
    --tag-keys "Environment"
# Add updated tag
aws stepfunctions tag-resource \
    --resource-arn arn:aws:states:us-east-1:123456789012:stateMachine:anihpj-author-onboard \
    --tags key=Environment,value=production-v2
Output: {}
Django Context: # Author.objects workflow environment update # Migrated to production-v2 # User.objects onboarding tagged correctly
Example 4: Clean Activity Tags

Remove obsolete tags from activity.

#!/bin/bash
aws stepfunctions untag-resource \
    --resource-arn arn:aws:states:us-east-1:123456789012:activity:anihpj-location-validation \
    --tag-keys "TestOnly" "Sprint23"
Output: {}
Django Context: # Location.objects activity tags cleaned # Removed sprint-specific tags # Activity moved to permanent status
Example 5: Batch Untag Resources

Remove tag from multiple workflows.

#!/bin/bash
# Remove old cost center tag from all workflows
for sm in "jobpost-approval" "skills-matcher" "author-onboard"; do
    aws stepfunctions untag-resource \
        --resource-arn "arn:aws:states:us-east-1:123456789012:stateMachine:anihpj-$sm" \
        --tag-keys "OldCostCenter"
    echo "Untagged: anihpj-$sm"
done
Output: Untagged: anihpj-jobpost-approval Untagged: anihpj-skills-matcher Untagged: anihpj-author-onboard
Django Context: # Batch cleanup of old cost center # JobPost, Skills, Author workflows updated # Ready for new billing tags

update-map-run

Updates the configuration of a running Map Run.

Syntax
aws stepfunctions update-map-run \
    --map-run-arn <value> \
    [--max-concurrency <value>] \
    [--tolerated-failure-count <value>] \
    [--tolerated-failure-percentage <value>]
Parameters
  • --map-run-arn (string): ARN of the Map Run (required)
  • --max-concurrency (integer): Max concurrent iterations
  • --tolerated-failure-count (integer): Max failures allowed
  • --tolerated-failure-percentage (float): Max failure percentage
Example 1: Increase Concurrency

Speed up batch processing by increasing parallelism.

#!/bin/bash
aws stepfunctions update-map-run \
    --map-run-arn arn:aws:states:us-east-1:123456789012:mapRun:anihpj-batch/batch-001:abc123 \
    --max-concurrency 50
Output: {}
Django Context: # Increased JobPost.objects batch parallelism # Now processing 50 concurrently # Faster Skills.objects validation
Example 2: Reduce Concurrency

Slow down processing to reduce load.

#!/bin/bash
aws stepfunctions update-map-run \
    --map-run-arn arn:aws:states:us-east-1:123456789012:mapRun:anihpj-migrate/migration:def456 \
    --max-concurrency 5
Output: {}
Django Context: # Reduced Location.objects migration speed # Only 5 concurrent geocoding calls # Prevents external API rate limiting
Example 3: Increase Failure Tolerance

Allow more failures before stopping.

#!/bin/bash
aws stepfunctions update-map-run \
    --map-run-arn arn:aws:states:us-east-1:123456789012:mapRun:anihpj-notify/notify-batch:ghi789 \
    --tolerated-failure-count 100
Output: {}
Django Context: # Allow up to 100 Author.objects notification failures # Email bounces won't stop batch # More resilient User.objects notifications
Example 4: Set Failure Percentage

Configure percentage-based failure tolerance.

#!/bin/bash
aws stepfunctions update-map-run \
    --map-run-arn arn:aws:states:us-east-1:123456789012:mapRun:anihpj-validate/batch-validate:jkl012 \
    --tolerated-failure-percentage 10.0
Output: {}
Django Context: # Allow 10% JobPost.objects validation failures # Expected some invalid entries # Skills.objects check continues on errors
Example 5: Combined Update

Update multiple Map Run settings.

#!/bin/bash
aws stepfunctions update-map-run \
    --map-run-arn arn:aws:states:us-east-1:123456789012:mapRun:anihpj-process/daily-batch:mno345 \
    --max-concurrency 25 \
    --tolerated-failure-count 50 \
    --tolerated-failure-percentage 5.0
Output: {}
Django Context: # Daily JobPost.objects batch tuned # 25 concurrent, 50 max or 5% failures # Balanced speed and reliability

update-state-machine

Updates an existing state machine definition, role, or configuration.

Syntax
aws stepfunctions update-state-machine \
    --state-machine-arn <value> \
    [--definition <value>] \
    [--role-arn <value>] \
    [--logging-configuration <value>] \
    [--tracing-configuration <value>]
Parameters
  • --state-machine-arn (string): ARN of the state machine (required)
  • --definition (string): New ASL definition
  • --role-arn (string): New IAM role ARN
  • --logging-configuration (structure): CloudWatch logging config
  • --tracing-configuration (structure): X-Ray tracing config
Example 1: Update Definition

Deploy new workflow definition.

#!/bin/bash
aws stepfunctions update-state-machine \
    --state-machine-arn arn:aws:states:us-east-1:123456789012:stateMachine:anihpj-jobpost-approval \
    --definition file://new-jobpost-workflow.json
Output: { "updateDate": "2025-01-15T15:00:00Z", "revisionId": "abc123" }
Django Context: # JobPost.objects approval workflow updated # New validation steps deployed # Skills.objects matching improved
Example 2: Update IAM Role

Change execution role for permissions update.

#!/bin/bash
aws stepfunctions update-state-machine \
    --state-machine-arn arn:aws:states:us-east-1:123456789012:stateMachine:anihpj-skills-matcher \
    --role-arn arn:aws:iam::123456789012:role/anihpj-enhanced-stepfunctions-role
Output: { "updateDate": "2025-01-15T15:05:00Z", "revisionId": "def456" }
Django Context: # Skills.objects matcher role updated # Enhanced permissions for new features # Can now access additional AWS services
Example 3: Enable Logging

Add CloudWatch logging to workflow.

#!/bin/bash
aws stepfunctions update-state-machine \
    --state-machine-arn arn:aws:states:us-east-1:123456789012:stateMachine:anihpj-author-onboard \
    --logging-configuration '{
        "level": "ALL",
        "includeExecutionData": true,
        "destinations": [
            {"cloudWatchLogsLogGroup": {"logGroupArn": "arn:aws:logs:us-east-1:123456789012:log-group:anihpj-stepfunctions:*"}}
        ]
    }'
Output: { "updateDate": "2025-01-15T15:10:00Z", "revisionId": "ghi789" }
Django Context: # Author.objects onboarding logging enabled # All execution data captured # User.objects workflow debugging improved
Example 4: Enable X-Ray Tracing

Add distributed tracing to workflow.

#!/bin/bash
aws stepfunctions update-state-machine \
    --state-machine-arn arn:aws:states:us-east-1:123456789012:stateMachine:anihpj-location-verify \
    --tracing-configuration '{"enabled": true}'
Output: { "updateDate": "2025-01-15T15:15:00Z", "revisionId": "jkl012" }
Django Context: # Location.objects verification X-Ray enabled # Full request tracing available # Debug JobPost.location geocoding latency
Example 5: Full Update

Update definition, role, and configuration.

#!/bin/bash
aws stepfunctions update-state-machine \
    --state-machine-arn arn:aws:states:us-east-1:123456789012:stateMachine:anihpj-batch-process \
    --definition file://updated-batch-workflow.json \
    --role-arn arn:aws:iam::123456789012:role/anihpj-batch-role \
    --logging-configuration '{"level": "ERROR", "includeExecutionData": false, "destinations": [{"cloudWatchLogsLogGroup": {"logGroupArn": "arn:aws:logs:us-east-1:123456789012:log-group:anihpj-batch:*"}}]}' \
    --tracing-configuration '{"enabled": true}'
Output: { "updateDate": "2025-01-15T15:20:00Z", "revisionId": "mno345" }
Django Context: # Full batch JobPost.objects workflow update # New definition, role, logging, tracing # Skills.objects processing optimized

update-state-machine-alias

Updates the routing configuration of a state machine alias.

Syntax
aws stepfunctions update-state-machine-alias \
    --state-machine-alias-arn <value> \
    [--description <value>] \
    [--routing-configuration <value>]
Parameters
  • --state-machine-alias-arn (string): ARN of the alias (required)
  • --description (string): New description
  • --routing-configuration (list): New version routing weights
Example 1: Update Production Alias

Route production to new version.

#!/bin/bash
aws stepfunctions update-state-machine-alias \
    --state-machine-alias-arn arn:aws:states:us-east-1:123456789012:stateMachine:anihpj-jobpost-approval:prod \
    --routing-configuration '[{"stateMachineVersionArn":"arn:aws:states:us-east-1:123456789012:stateMachine:anihpj-jobpost-approval:4","weight":100}]'
Output: { "updateDate": "2025-01-15T16:00:00Z" }
Django Context: # Production now on JobPost.objects v4 # Full traffic to new version # Skills.objects validation upgraded
Example 2: Start Canary Deployment

Route small percentage to new version.

#!/bin/bash
aws stepfunctions update-state-machine-alias \
    --state-machine-alias-arn arn:aws:states:us-east-1:123456789012:stateMachine:anihpj-skills-matcher:prod \
    --routing-configuration '[
        {"stateMachineVersionArn":"arn:aws:states:us-east-1:123456789012:stateMachine:anihpj-skills-matcher:5","weight":90},
        {"stateMachineVersionArn":"arn:aws:states:us-east-1:123456789012:stateMachine:anihpj-skills-matcher:6","weight":10}
    ]'
Output: { "updateDate": "2025-01-15T16:05:00Z" }
Django Context: # Skills.objects matcher canary started # 90% v5, 10% v6 traffic split # Testing new matching algorithm
Example 3: Increase Canary Traffic

Gradually increase new version traffic.

#!/bin/bash
aws stepfunctions update-state-machine-alias \
    --state-machine-alias-arn arn:aws:states:us-east-1:123456789012:stateMachine:anihpj-author-onboard:prod \
    --routing-configuration '[
        {"stateMachineVersionArn":"arn:aws:states:us-east-1:123456789012:stateMachine:anihpj-author-onboard:4","weight":50},
        {"stateMachineVersionArn":"arn:aws:states:us-east-1:123456789012:stateMachine:anihpj-author-onboard:5","weight":50}
    ]'
Output: { "updateDate": "2025-01-15T16:10:00Z" }
Django Context: # Author.objects onboarding 50/50 split # Half traffic testing v5 # User.objects creation validated
Example 4: Rollback to Previous

Quick rollback to stable version.

#!/bin/bash
# Emergency rollback to v3
aws stepfunctions update-state-machine-alias \
    --state-machine-alias-arn arn:aws:states:us-east-1:123456789012:stateMachine:anihpj-location-verify:prod \
    --routing-configuration '[{"stateMachineVersionArn":"arn:aws:states:us-east-1:123456789012:stateMachine:anihpj-location-verify:3","weight":100}]' \
    --description "Rolled back due to geocoding errors"
Output: { "updateDate": "2025-01-15T16:15:00Z" }
Django Context: # Location.objects verification rolled back # v4 had geocoding issues # JobPost.location using stable v3
Example 5: Complete Deployment

Finalize deployment to new version.

#!/bin/bash
# Canary successful - full deployment
aws stepfunctions update-state-machine-alias \
    --state-machine-alias-arn arn:aws:states:us-east-1:123456789012:stateMachine:anihpj-batch-process:prod \
    --routing-configuration '[{"stateMachineVersionArn":"arn:aws:states:us-east-1:123456789012:stateMachine:anihpj-batch-process:4","weight":100}]' \
    --description "v4 fully deployed - batch optimization"
Output: { "updateDate": "2025-01-15T16:20:00Z" }
Django Context: # Batch JobPost.objects v4 fully deployed # Canary testing completed successfully # Skills.objects processing 30% faster

validate-state-machine-definition

Validates a state machine definition without creating it.

Syntax
aws stepfunctions validate-state-machine-definition \
    --definition <value> \
    [--type STANDARD|EXPRESS]
Parameters
  • --definition (string): ASL definition to validate (required)
  • --type (string): STANDARD or EXPRESS workflow type
Example 1: Validate Simple Definition

Check workflow definition syntax.

#!/bin/bash
aws stepfunctions validate-state-machine-definition \
    --definition '{
        "StartAt": "ValidateJobPost",
        "States": {
            "ValidateJobPost": {
                "Type": "Task",
                "Resource": "arn:aws:lambda:us-east-1:123456789012:function:validate-jobpost",
                "End": true
            }
        }
    }'
Output: { "result": "OK", "diagnostics": [] }
Django Context: # JobPost.objects validation workflow valid # No syntax errors found # Ready to create state machine
Example 2: Validate with Warnings

Check definition that has issues.

#!/bin/bash
aws stepfunctions validate-state-machine-definition \
    --definition '{
        "StartAt": "CheckSkills",
        "States": {
            "CheckSkills": {
                "Type": "Task",
                "Resource": "arn:aws:lambda:us-east-1:123456789012:function:check-skills"
            }
        }
    }'
Output: { "result": "FAIL", "diagnostics": [ { "severity": "ERROR", "code": "MISSING_END_STATE", "message": "State 'CheckSkills' must have 'End' or 'Next' property" } ] }
Django Context: # Skills.objects workflow has error # Missing End or Next property # Fix before deployment
Example 3: Validate from File

Validate definition stored in file.

#!/bin/bash
aws stepfunctions validate-state-machine-definition \
    --definition file://author-onboard-workflow.json \
    --type STANDARD
Output: { "result": "OK", "diagnostics": [] }
Django Context: # Author.objects onboarding definition valid # STANDARD type workflow # User.objects creation flow ready
Example 4: Validate Express Workflow

Check Express-type workflow constraints.

#!/bin/bash
aws stepfunctions validate-state-machine-definition \
    --definition '{
        "StartAt": "QuickCheck",
        "States": {
            "QuickCheck": {
                "Type": "Task",
                "Resource": "arn:aws:lambda:us-east-1:123456789012:function:quick-validate",
                "End": true
            }
        }
    }' \
    --type EXPRESS
Output: { "result": "OK", "diagnostics": [] }
Django Context: # Express workflow for fast validation # JobPost.objects quick check valid # Sub-second execution supported
Example 5: CI/CD Validation Step

Validate in deployment pipeline.

#!/bin/bash
# CI/CD validation step
RESULT=$(aws stepfunctions validate-state-machine-definition \
    --definition file://workflows/location-verify.json \
    --type STANDARD \
    --query 'result' --output text)
if [ "$RESULT" == "OK" ]; then
    echo "✓ Validation passed"
    exit 0
else
    echo "✗ Validation failed"
    aws stepfunctions validate-state-machine-definition \
        --definition file://workflows/location-verify.json \
        --query 'diagnostics'
    exit 1
fi
Output: ✓ Validation passed
Django Context: # CI/CD Location.objects workflow check # Validation passed in pipeline # Ready for JobPost.location deployment

📊 Analytics & Monitoring

AWS Analytics services help you gain insights from your data. This section covers CloudWatch Logs Insights for log analysis, X-Ray for application tracing, and Athena for SQL queries on S3 data.

CloudWatch Logs Insights

Analyze and query log data interactively for your Django application

📋 CloudWatch Logs Insights Overview

CloudWatch Logs Insights enables you to interactively search and analyze your log data in Amazon CloudWatch Logs. Use it to query Django application logs, debug jobpost model operations, and monitor user activities in the anihpj application.

Core Logs Insights Operations

start-query - Start log query
get-query-results - Get query results
describe-queries - List running queries
stop-query - Cancel running query
create-log-group - Create log group
put-log-events - Send log entries

start-query

Starts a CloudWatch Logs Insights query to analyze log data.

Syntax
aws logs start-query \
    --log-group-name <value> \
    --start-time <value> \
    --end-time <value> \
    --query-string <value>
Parameters
  • --log-group-name (string): Log group to query
  • --start-time (timestamp): Query start time (Unix epoch)
  • --end-time (timestamp): Query end time (Unix epoch)
  • --query-string (string): Logs Insights query
Example 1: Query Django error logs

Find error messages in jobpost application logs.

#!/bin/bash
# Query last 24 hours for ERROR messages
aws logs start-query \
    --log-group-name "/aws/django/anihpj-jobpost" \
    --start-time $(date -d '24 hours ago' +%s) \
    --end-time $(date +%s) \
    --query-string 'fields @timestamp, @message
        | filter @message like /ERROR/
        | sort @timestamp desc
        | limit 50'
Output: { "queryId": "query-12345-abcde" }
Django Context: # Query to find JobPost.objects errors # Catches Skills.DoesNotExist exceptions # Tracks Author.objects validation failures # Similar to Django logging: logger.error()
Example 2: Count requests by endpoint

Analyze API traffic patterns.

#!/bin/bash
aws logs start-query \
    --log-group-name "/aws/django/anihpj-jobpost" \
    --start-time $(date -d '1 hour ago' +%s) \
    --end-time $(date +%s) \
    --query-string 'fields @timestamp, @message
        | parse @message "* * * /api/*" as method, status, duration, endpoint
        | stats count(*) as requests by endpoint
        | sort requests desc'
Output: { "queryId": "query-67890-fghij" }
Django Context: # Traffic analysis for: # /api/jobposts/ → JobPost.objects.all() # /api/skills/ → Skills.objects.all() # /api/authors/ → Author.objects.all() # /api/locations/ → Location.objects.all()
Example 3: Find slow database queries

Identify slow Django ORM queries.

#!/bin/bash
aws logs start-query \
    --log-group-name "/aws/django/anihpj-jobpost" \
    --start-time $(date -d '6 hours ago' +%s) \
    --end-time $(date +%s) \
    --query-string 'fields @timestamp, @message
        | filter @message like /SELECT.*FROM/
        | parse @message "* ms *" as duration
        | filter duration > 100
        | sort duration desc
        | limit 20'
Output: { "queryId": "query-slow-db-123" }
Django Context: # Find slow queries like: # JobPost.objects.select_related('author', 'location') # Skills.objects.prefetch_related('jobpost_set') # N+1 query detection for Author.objects
Example 4: Track user login activity

Monitor authentication events.

#!/bin/bash
aws logs start-query \
    --log-group-name "/aws/django/anihpj-jobpost" \
    --start-time $(date -d '7 days ago' +%s) \
    --end-time $(date +%s) \
    --query-string 'fields @timestamp, @message
        | filter @message like /login|logout|authentication/
        | stats count(*) as events by bin(1h)
        | sort @timestamp'
Output: { "queryId": "query-auth-456" }
Django Context: # Track User.objects.get() authentication # Monitor Author login patterns # Django auth_user session tracking
Example 5: JobPost creation metrics

Analyze job post creation patterns.

#!/bin/bash
aws logs start-query \
    --log-group-name "/aws/django/anihpj-jobpost" \
    --start-time $(date -d '30 days ago' +%s) \
    --end-time $(date +%s) \
    --query-string 'fields @timestamp, @message
        | filter @message like /JobPost created/
        | parse @message "JobPost created: id=* title=*" as jobpost_id, title
        | stats count(*) as total by bin(1d)
        | sort @timestamp'
Output: { "queryId": "query-jobpost-create-789" }
Django Context: # Track JobPost.objects.create() frequency # Daily job post creation metrics # Correlates with Author activity

get-query-results

Retrieves results from a started Logs Insights query.

Syntax
aws logs get-query-results \
    --query-id <value>
Example 1: Get error query results

Retrieve completed query results.

#!/bin/bash
aws logs get-query-results \
    --query-id query-12345-abcde
Output: { "status": "Complete", "results": [ [ {"field": "@timestamp", "value": "2025-01-11T15:30:00Z"}, {"field": "@message", "value": "ERROR: JobPost.DoesNotExist - pk=99999"} ], [ {"field": "@timestamp", "value": "2025-01-11T15:25:00Z"}, {"field": "@message", "value": "ERROR: Skills validation failed for author_id=45"} ] ], "statistics": { "recordsMatched": 2, "recordsScanned": 15000 } }
Django Context: # Errors found: # 1. JobPost.objects.get(pk=99999) → DoesNotExist # 2. Skills validation for Author.objects.get(pk=45) # Review: JobPost and Skills model validations
Example 2: Get traffic analysis results

Retrieve endpoint traffic statistics.

#!/bin/bash
aws logs get-query-results \
    --query-id query-67890-fghij
Output: { "status": "Complete", "results": [ [{"field": "endpoint", "value": "jobposts"}, {"field": "requests", "value": "1523"}], [{"field": "endpoint", "value": "skills"}, {"field": "requests", "value": "892"}], [{"field": "endpoint", "value": "locations"}, {"field": "requests", "value": "456"}], [{"field": "endpoint", "value": "authors"}, {"field": "requests", "value": "234"}] ] }
Django Context: # Traffic distribution: # JobPost.objects: 1523 requests (most popular) # Skills.objects: 892 requests # Location.objects: 456 requests # Author.objects: 234 requests

create-log-group

Creates a new log group for organizing Django application logs.

Syntax
aws logs create-log-group \
    --log-group-name <value> \
    [--tags key=value,...]
Example 1: Create Django app log group

Create log group for jobpost application.

#!/bin/bash
aws logs create-log-group \
    --log-group-name "/aws/django/anihpj-jobpost" \
    --tags Environment=production,App=jobpost,Framework=Django
Output: (No output on success)
Django Context: # Log group for Django logging: # - JobPost CRUD operations # - Skills model changes # - Author activity tracking # - Location queries
Example 2: Create separate log groups by model

Organize logs by Django model.

#!/bin/bash
# Create log groups for each model
for model in jobpost skills author location user; do
    aws logs create-log-group \
        --log-group-name "/aws/django/anihpj/$model" \
        --tags Model=$model
done
Output: (Log groups created for each model)
Django Context: # Separate log groups: # /aws/django/anihpj/jobpost → JobPost.objects logs # /aws/django/anihpj/skills → Skills.objects logs # /aws/django/anihpj/author → Author.objects logs # /aws/django/anihpj/location → Location.objects logs

AWS X-Ray

Analyze and debug distributed applications with request tracing

📋 X-Ray Overview

AWS X-Ray helps developers analyze and debug production, distributed applications. Use it to trace requests through your Django jobpost application, identify performance bottlenecks, and understand how your Skills, Author, Location, and JobPost models interact with AWS services.

Core X-Ray Operations

get-trace-summaries - Get trace overview
batch-get-traces - Retrieve full traces
get-service-graph - Visualize service map
create-group - Create trace filter group
put-trace-segments - Send trace data
get-insight-summaries - Get anomaly insights

get-trace-summaries

Retrieves trace summaries for requests that match specified filters.

Syntax
aws xray get-trace-summaries \
    --start-time <value> \
    --end-time <value> \
    [--filter-expression <value>] \
    [--sampling]
Parameters
  • --start-time (timestamp): Start time for traces
  • --end-time (timestamp): End time for traces
  • --filter-expression (string): Filter criteria for traces
  • --sampling (boolean): Enable trace sampling
Example 1: Get slow request traces

Find traces for requests taking more than 2 seconds.

#!/bin/bash
aws xray get-trace-summaries \
    --start-time $(date -d '1 hour ago' +%s) \
    --end-time $(date +%s) \
    --filter-expression 'responsetime > 2'
Output: { "TraceSummaries": [ { "Id": "1-5f4b3c2a-abc123def456789012345678", "Duration": 2.5, "ResponseTime": 2.5, "Http": { "HttpURL": "https://api.anihpj.com/jobposts/", "HttpStatus": 200, "HttpMethod": "GET" }, "Annotations": { "model": "JobPost" } } ], "TracesProcessedCount": 1500 }
Django Context: # Slow trace: GET /jobposts/ taking 2.5s # Likely cause: JobPost.objects.select_related() # Check: N+1 queries on Author and Location # Optimize: Add prefetch_related for Skills
Example 2: Filter by error status

Find failed requests returning 5xx errors.

#!/bin/bash
aws xray get-trace-summaries \
    --start-time $(date -d '24 hours ago' +%s) \
    --end-time $(date +%s) \
    --filter-expression 'http.status >= 500'
Output: { "TraceSummaries": [ { "Id": "1-5f4b3c2b-def123abc456789012345678", "Duration": 0.15, "Http": { "HttpURL": "https://api.anihpj.com/skills/999/", "HttpStatus": 500 }, "HasError": true, "HasFault": true } ] }
Django Context: # Error trace: GET /skills/999/ → 500 # Skills.objects.get(pk=999) failed # Exception: Skills.DoesNotExist # Fix: Add proper 404 handling
Example 3: Filter by annotation

Find traces with specific Django model annotations.

#!/bin/bash
aws xray get-trace-summaries \
    --start-time $(date -d '6 hours ago' +%s) \
    --end-time $(date +%s) \
    --filter-expression 'annotation.model = "JobPost"'
Output: { "TraceSummaries": [ { "Id": "1-5f4b3c2c-ghi123jkl456789012345678", "Duration": 0.45, "Annotations": { "model": "JobPost", "operation": "create" } } ] }
Django Context: # Traces for JobPost.objects.create() # 0.45s for job post creation # Includes Skills validation and Author lookup

batch-get-traces

Retrieves full trace details including all segments.

Syntax
aws xray batch-get-traces \
    --trace-ids <value>
Example 1: Get full trace details

Retrieve complete trace with all segments.

#!/bin/bash
aws xray batch-get-traces \
    --trace-ids 1-5f4b3c2a-abc123def456789012345678
Output: { "Traces": [ { "Id": "1-5f4b3c2a-abc123def456789012345678", "Duration": 2.5, "Segments": [ { "Id": "seg-django-001", "Document": { "name": "Django", "start_time": 1704974400.0, "end_time": 1704974402.5, "subsegments": [ {"name": "PostgreSQL", "duration": 1.8}, {"name": "S3", "duration": 0.3} ] } } ] } ] }
Django Context: # Full trace breakdown: # Django: 2.5s total # └── PostgreSQL: 1.8s (JobPost queries) # └── S3: 0.3s (static files) # Bottleneck: Database queries
Example 2: Get multiple traces

Batch retrieve several traces.

#!/bin/bash
aws xray batch-get-traces \
    --trace-ids \
        "1-5f4b3c2a-abc123def456789012345678" \
        "1-5f4b3c2b-def123abc456789012345678"
Output: { "Traces": [ {"Id": "1-5f4b3c2a-...", "Duration": 2.5}, {"Id": "1-5f4b3c2b-...", "Duration": 0.15} ] }
Django Context: # Compare JobPost query performance # Trace 1: 2.5s (slow - needs optimization) # Trace 2: 0.15s (fast - cached result)

get-service-graph

Retrieves the service graph showing how services interact.

Syntax
aws xray get-service-graph \
    --start-time <value> \
    --end-time <value>
Example 1: Get service map

Visualize Django app service dependencies.

#!/bin/bash
aws xray get-service-graph \
    --start-time $(date -d '1 hour ago' +%s) \
    --end-time $(date +%s)
Output: { "Services": [ { "Name": "anihpj-django", "Type": "AWS::EC2::Instance", "ResponseTimeHistogram": [ {"Value": 0.5, "Count": 1200} ], "Edges": [ {"ReferenceId": 1, "SummaryStatistics": {"TotalCount": 500}}, {"ReferenceId": 2, "SummaryStatistics": {"TotalCount": 300}} ] }, { "ReferenceId": 1, "Name": "PostgreSQL", "Type": "AWS::RDS::DBInstance" }, { "ReferenceId": 2, "Name": "S3", "Type": "AWS::S3::Bucket" } ] }
Django Context: # Service map showing: # Django App → PostgreSQL (500 calls) # - JobPost.objects queries # - Skills.objects lookups # Django App → S3 (300 calls) # - Static file serving # - Media file uploads

create-group

Creates a group to filter traces by specific criteria.

Syntax
aws xray create-group \
    --group-name <value> \
    --filter-expression <value>
Example 1: Create group for JobPost errors

Filter group for jobpost-related errors.

#!/bin/bash
aws xray create-group \
    --group-name "anihpj-jobpost-errors" \
    --filter-expression 'annotation.model = "JobPost" AND error = true'
Output: { "Group": { "GroupName": "anihpj-jobpost-errors", "GroupARN": "arn:aws:xray:us-east-1:123456789012:group/anihpj-jobpost-errors", "FilterExpression": "annotation.model = \"JobPost\" AND error = true" } }
Django Context: # Group filters for: # - JobPost.objects.get() failures # - JobPost.objects.create() validation errors # - JobPost.DoesNotExist exceptions
Example 2: Create group for slow Skills queries

Track slow Skills model operations.

#!/bin/bash
aws xray create-group \
    --group-name "anihpj-slow-skills" \
    --filter-expression 'annotation.model = "Skills" AND responsetime > 1'
Output: { "Group": { "GroupName": "anihpj-slow-skills", "FilterExpression": "annotation.model = \"Skills\" AND responsetime > 1" } }
Django Context: # Tracks slow Skills.objects queries: # - Skills.objects.filter() > 1s # - Skills prefetch operations # - Many-to-many relationship loads

Amazon Athena

Query data in S3 using standard SQL for analytics

📋 Athena Overview

Amazon Athena is an interactive query service that makes it easy to analyze data in Amazon S3 using standard SQL. Use it to analyze Django jobpost application logs, query exported JobPost data, and generate reports on Skills, Authors, and Locations stored in S3.

Core Athena Operations

start-query-execution - Run SQL query
get-query-execution - Get query status
get-query-results - Retrieve results
create-database - Create database
create-named-query - Save query
list-query-executions - List queries

start-query-execution

Runs a SQL query against data stored in Amazon S3.

Syntax
aws athena start-query-execution \
    --query-string <value> \
    --result-configuration OutputLocation=<s3-path> \
    [--query-execution-context Database=<value>]
Parameters
  • --query-string (string): SQL query to execute
  • --result-configuration (structure): S3 location for results
  • --query-execution-context (structure): Database context
Example 1: Query JobPost analytics

Analyze job post data exported to S3.

#!/bin/bash
aws athena start-query-execution \
    --query-string "SELECT 
        location_city,
        COUNT(*) as job_count,
        AVG(salary_min) as avg_min_salary
    FROM jobpost_archive
    WHERE status = 'published'
    GROUP BY location_city
    ORDER BY job_count DESC
    LIMIT 10" \
    --result-configuration OutputLocation=s3://anihpj-athena-results/ \
    --query-execution-context Database=anihpj_analytics
Output: { "QueryExecutionId": "query-exec-12345-abcde" }
Django Context: # Equivalent Django ORM: # JobPost.objects.filter(status='published') # .values('location__city') # .annotate(job_count=Count('id'), avg_min_salary=Avg('salary_min')) # .order_by('-job_count')[:10]
Example 2: Analyze Skills distribution

Query skills usage across job posts.

#!/bin/bash
aws athena start-query-execution \
    --query-string "SELECT 
        skill_name,
        COUNT(DISTINCT jobpost_id) as jobs_requiring,
        COUNT(DISTINCT author_id) as authors_with_skill
    FROM skills_analytics
    GROUP BY skill_name
    ORDER BY jobs_requiring DESC" \
    --result-configuration OutputLocation=s3://anihpj-athena-results/ \
    --query-execution-context Database=anihpj_analytics
Output: { "QueryExecutionId": "query-exec-67890-fghij" }
Django Context: # Equivalent Django ORM: # Skills.objects.annotate( # jobs_requiring=Count('jobpost', distinct=True), # authors_with_skill=Count('author', distinct=True) # ).order_by('-jobs_requiring')
Example 3: Author activity report

Generate author performance analytics.

#!/bin/bash
aws athena start-query-execution \
    --query-string "SELECT 
        author_id,
        author_name,
        COUNT(*) as total_posts,
        SUM(CASE WHEN status='published' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) as published,
        MIN(created_at) as first_post,
        MAX(created_at) as latest_post
    FROM jobpost_archive
    GROUP BY author_id, author_name
    HAVING COUNT(*) > 5
    ORDER BY total_posts DESC" \
    --result-configuration OutputLocation=s3://anihpj-athena-results/
Output: { "QueryExecutionId": "query-exec-author-123" }
Django Context: # Equivalent Django ORM: # Author.objects.annotate( # total_posts=Count('jobpost'), # published=Count('jobpost', filter=Q(jobpost__status='published')), # first_post=Min('jobpost__created_at'), # latest_post=Max('jobpost__created_at') # ).filter(total_posts__gt=5).order_by('-total_posts')

get-query-execution

Gets information about a query execution.

Syntax
aws athena get-query-execution \
    --query-execution-id <value>
Example 1: Check query status

Monitor query execution progress.

#!/bin/bash
aws athena get-query-execution \
    --query-execution-id query-exec-12345-abcde
Output: { "QueryExecution": { "QueryExecutionId": "query-exec-12345-abcde", "Query": "SELECT location_city, COUNT(*) as job_count...", "Status": { "State": "SUCCEEDED", "SubmissionDateTime": "2025-01-11T18:00:00Z", "CompletionDateTime": "2025-01-11T18:00:15Z" }, "Statistics": { "DataScannedInBytes": 1048576, "EngineExecutionTimeInMillis": 5000 }, "ResultConfiguration": { "OutputLocation": "s3://anihpj-athena-results/query-exec-12345-abcde.csv" } } }
Django Context: # Query completed in 5 seconds # Scanned 1MB of JobPost data # Results: Location-based job distribution # Download from S3 for reporting

get-query-results

Retrieves the results of a completed query.

Syntax
aws athena get-query-results \
    --query-execution-id <value> \
    [--max-results <value>]
Example 1: Get JobPost query results

Retrieve completed query results.

#!/bin/bash
aws athena get-query-results \
    --query-execution-id query-exec-12345-abcde \
    --max-results 10
Output: { "ResultSet": { "Rows": [ {"Data": [{"VarCharValue": "location_city"}, {"VarCharValue": "job_count"}, {"VarCharValue": "avg_min_salary"}]}, {"Data": [{"VarCharValue": "San Francisco"}, {"VarCharValue": "523"}, {"VarCharValue": "125000"}]}, {"Data": [{"VarCharValue": "New York"}, {"VarCharValue": "412"}, {"VarCharValue": "115000"}]}, {"Data": [{"VarCharValue": "Seattle"}, {"VarCharValue": "298"}, {"VarCharValue": "120000"}]}, {"Data": [{"VarCharValue": "Austin"}, {"VarCharValue": "187"}, {"VarCharValue": "95000"}]} ] } }
Django Context: # Results equivalent to: # Location.objects with JobPost aggregation: # - San Francisco: 523 jobs, avg $125k # - New York: 412 jobs, avg $115k # - Seattle: 298 jobs, avg $120k # - Austin: 187 jobs, avg $95k
Example 2: Get Skills analytics results

Retrieve skills distribution data.

#!/bin/bash
aws athena get-query-results \
    --query-execution-id query-exec-67890-fghij
Output: { "ResultSet": { "Rows": [ {"Data": [{"VarCharValue": "skill_name"}, {"VarCharValue": "jobs_requiring"}, {"VarCharValue": "authors_with_skill"}]}, {"Data": [{"VarCharValue": "Python"}, {"VarCharValue": "892"}, {"VarCharValue": "456"}]}, {"Data": [{"VarCharValue": "Django"}, {"VarCharValue": "654"}, {"VarCharValue": "321"}]}, {"Data": [{"VarCharValue": "AWS"}, {"VarCharValue": "523"}, {"VarCharValue": "267"}]}, {"Data": [{"VarCharValue": "JavaScript"}, {"VarCharValue": "445"}, {"VarCharValue": "234"}]} ] } }
Django Context: # Skills.objects distribution: # - Python: 892 jobs need it, 456 authors have it # - Django: 654 jobs need it, 321 authors have it # - AWS: 523 jobs need it, 267 authors have it # - JavaScript: 445 jobs need it, 234 authors have it

create-database

Creates a new database in Athena for organizing tables.

Syntax
aws athena start-query-execution \
    --query-string "CREATE DATABASE <database-name>" \
    --result-configuration OutputLocation=<s3-path>
Example 1: Create analytics database

Create database for jobpost analytics.

#!/bin/bash
aws athena start-query-execution \
    --query-string "CREATE DATABASE IF NOT EXISTS anihpj_analytics 
        COMMENT 'Analytics database for anihpj Django jobpost app'" \
    --result-configuration OutputLocation=s3://anihpj-athena-results/
Output: { "QueryExecutionId": "create-db-xyz123" }
Django Context: # Database for storing: # - JobPost historical data # - Skills analytics # - Author activity reports # - Location metrics
Example 2: Create table for JobPost data

Define table schema for S3 data.

#!/bin/bash
aws athena start-query-execution \
    --query-string "CREATE EXTERNAL TABLE IF NOT EXISTS anihpj_analytics.jobpost_archive (
        id INT,
        title STRING,
        author_id INT,
        author_name STRING,
        location_city STRING,
        location_country STRING,
        status STRING,
        salary_min INT,
        salary_max INT,
        created_at TIMESTAMP
    )
    ROW FORMAT SERDE 'org.apache.hadoop.hive.serde2.lazy.LazySimpleSerDe'
    WITH SERDEPROPERTIES ('field.delim' = ',')
    LOCATION 's3://anihpj-data-lake/jobposts/'" \
    --result-configuration OutputLocation=s3://anihpj-athena-results/
Output: { "QueryExecutionId": "create-table-abc456" }
Django Context: # Table maps to JobPost model: # - id → JobPost.pk # - title → JobPost.title # - author_id/name → Author relation # - location_city/country → Location relation # - status → JobPost.status # - salary_min/max → JobPost salary fields

💾 Backup & Disaster Recovery

AWS Backup services protect your data against loss. This section covers AWS Backup for centralized backup management and S3 Glacier for long-term archival storage.

AWS Backup

Centralized backup management for AWS resources

📋 AWS Backup Overview

AWS Backup is a fully managed backup service that makes it easy to centralize and automate the backup of data across AWS services. Use it to protect your Django jobpost application data in RDS databases, EBS volumes, and S3 buckets containing Skills, Authors, Locations, and JobPosts.

Core AWS Backup Operations

create-backup-plan - Define backup schedule
create-backup-vault - Create storage vault
start-backup-job - Initiate backup
describe-backup-job - Get backup status
start-restore-job - Restore from backup
list-recovery-points - List backups

create-backup-vault

Creates a backup vault for storing recovery points.

Syntax
aws backup create-backup-vault \
    --backup-vault-name <value> \
    [--encryption-key-arn <value>] \
    [--backup-vault-tags key=value,...]
Parameters
  • --backup-vault-name (string): Name for the vault
  • --encryption-key-arn (string): KMS key for encryption
  • --backup-vault-tags (map): Tags for the vault
Example 1: Create production backup vault

Create encrypted vault for jobpost data.

#!/bin/bash
aws backup create-backup-vault \
    --backup-vault-name "anihpj-jobpost-vault" \
    --encryption-key-arn arn:aws:kms:us-east-1:123456789012:key/mrk-abc123 \
    --backup-vault-tags Environment=production,App=jobpost
Output: { "BackupVaultName": "anihpj-jobpost-vault", "BackupVaultArn": "arn:aws:backup:us-east-1:123456789012:backup-vault:anihpj-jobpost-vault", "CreationDate": "2025-01-11T19:00:00Z" }
Django Context: # Vault stores backups of: # - RDS: JobPost, Skills, Author, Location tables # - EBS: Django application volumes # - S3: Media files and static assets
Example 2: Create DR vault in another region

Set up cross-region disaster recovery.

#!/bin/bash
aws backup create-backup-vault \
    --backup-vault-name "anihpj-dr-vault" \
    --backup-vault-tags Purpose=disaster-recovery,Region=secondary \
    --region us-west-2
Output: { "BackupVaultName": "anihpj-dr-vault", "BackupVaultArn": "arn:aws:backup:us-west-2:123456789012:backup-vault:anihpj-dr-vault" }
Django Context: # DR vault in us-west-2 for: # - Cross-region JobPost data protection # - Skills and Author data redundancy # - Business continuity

create-backup-plan

Creates a backup plan defining schedule and retention.

Syntax
aws backup create-backup-plan \
    --backup-plan <value>
Example 1: Create daily backup plan

Schedule daily backups with 30-day retention.

#!/bin/bash
aws backup create-backup-plan \
    --backup-plan '{
        "BackupPlanName": "anihpj-daily-backup",
        "Rules": [
            {
                "RuleName": "DailyBackup",
                "TargetBackupVaultName": "anihpj-jobpost-vault",
                "ScheduleExpression": "cron(0 5 ? * * *)",
                "StartWindowMinutes": 60,
                "CompletionWindowMinutes": 180,
                "Lifecycle": {
                    "DeleteAfterDays": 30
                }
            }
        ]
    }'
Output: { "BackupPlanId": "plan-abc123", "BackupPlanArn": "arn:aws:backup:us-east-1:123456789012:backup-plan:plan-abc123", "CreationDate": "2025-01-11T19:15:00Z", "VersionId": "v1" }
Django Context: # Daily backup at 5 AM UTC: # - All JobPost.objects data # - Skills.objects library # - Author.objects profiles # - Location.objects directory # Retention: 30 days
Example 2: Create weekly backup with cross-region copy

Weekly backups copied to DR region.

#!/bin/bash
aws backup create-backup-plan \
    --backup-plan '{
        "BackupPlanName": "anihpj-weekly-dr",
        "Rules": [
            {
                "RuleName": "WeeklyBackupWithCopy",
                "TargetBackupVaultName": "anihpj-jobpost-vault",
                "ScheduleExpression": "cron(0 3 ? * SUN *)",
                "Lifecycle": {
                    "MoveToColdStorageAfterDays": 30,
                    "DeleteAfterDays": 365
                },
                "CopyActions": [
                    {
                        "DestinationBackupVaultArn": "arn:aws:backup:us-west-2:123456789012:backup-vault:anihpj-dr-vault",
                        "Lifecycle": {
                            "DeleteAfterDays": 90
                        }
                    }
                ]
            }
        ]
    }'
Output: { "BackupPlanId": "plan-xyz789", "BackupPlanArn": "arn:aws:backup:us-east-1:123456789012:backup-plan:plan-xyz789" }
Django Context: # Weekly Sunday backup: # - Full Django database snapshot # - Copied to us-west-2 for DR # - Cold storage after 30 days # - 1-year retention in primary

start-backup-job

Starts an on-demand backup job immediately.

Syntax
aws backup start-backup-job \
    --backup-vault-name <value> \
    --resource-arn <value> \
    --iam-role-arn <value>
Example 1: Backup RDS database

On-demand backup of Django database.

#!/bin/bash
aws backup start-backup-job \
    --backup-vault-name "anihpj-jobpost-vault" \
    --resource-arn "arn:aws:rds:us-east-1:123456789012:db:anihpj-postgres" \
    --iam-role-arn "arn:aws:iam::123456789012:role/AWSBackupDefaultServiceRole"
Output: { "BackupJobId": "job-rds-12345", "CreationDate": "2025-01-11T19:30:00Z", "RecoveryPointArn": "arn:aws:backup:us-east-1:123456789012:recovery-point:rp-rds-12345" }
Django Context: # Backing up PostgreSQL with: # - jobpost_jobpost table (JobPost.objects) # - jobpost_skills table (Skills.objects) # - jobpost_author table (Author.objects) # - jobpost_location table (Location.objects)
Example 2: Backup EBS volume

Backup EC2 instance storage.

#!/bin/bash
aws backup start-backup-job \
    --backup-vault-name "anihpj-jobpost-vault" \
    --resource-arn "arn:aws:ec2:us-east-1:123456789012:volume/vol-0123456789abcdef0" \
    --iam-role-arn "arn:aws:iam::123456789012:role/AWSBackupDefaultServiceRole" \
    --lifecycle DeleteAfterDays=7
Output: { "BackupJobId": "job-ebs-67890", "CreationDate": "2025-01-11T19:35:00Z" }
Django Context: # Backing up EBS with: # - Django application code # - Static files # - Log files # - Configuration files

start-restore-job

Restores data from a recovery point.

Syntax
aws backup start-restore-job \
    --recovery-point-arn <value> \
    --metadata <value> \
    --iam-role-arn <value>
Example 1: Restore RDS database

Restore Django database from backup.

#!/bin/bash
aws backup start-restore-job \
    --recovery-point-arn "arn:aws:backup:us-east-1:123456789012:recovery-point:rp-rds-12345" \
    --metadata '{
        "DBInstanceIdentifier": "anihpj-postgres-restored",
        "DBInstanceClass": "db.t3.medium"
    }' \
    --iam-role-arn "arn:aws:iam::123456789012:role/AWSBackupDefaultServiceRole"
Output: { "RestoreJobId": "restore-job-abc123", "CreationDate": "2025-01-11T19:45:00Z" }
Django Context: # Restoring database with: # - All JobPost.objects records # - Skills.objects library intact # - Author.objects with relationships # - Location.objects preserved
Example 2: Restore EBS volume

Recover application storage.

#!/bin/bash
aws backup start-restore-job \
    --recovery-point-arn "arn:aws:backup:us-east-1:123456789012:recovery-point:rp-ebs-67890" \
    --metadata '{
        "availabilityZone": "us-east-1a",
        "encrypted": "true"
    }' \
    --iam-role-arn "arn:aws:iam::123456789012:role/AWSBackupDefaultServiceRole"
Output: { "RestoreJobId": "restore-job-xyz789" }
Django Context: # Restoring EBS volume with: # - Django application files # - Static assets # - Media uploads

list-recovery-points

Lists available recovery points in a vault.

Syntax
aws backup list-recovery-points-by-backup-vault \
    --backup-vault-name <value>
Example 1: List all recovery points

View available backups for restoration.

#!/bin/bash
aws backup list-recovery-points-by-backup-vault \
    --backup-vault-name "anihpj-jobpost-vault"
Output: { "RecoveryPoints": [ { "RecoveryPointArn": "arn:aws:backup:...:recovery-point:rp-rds-12345", "ResourceType": "RDS", "CreationDate": "2025-01-11T05:00:00Z", "Status": "COMPLETED", "BackupSizeInBytes": 1073741824 }, { "RecoveryPointArn": "arn:aws:backup:...:recovery-point:rp-ebs-67890", "ResourceType": "EBS", "CreationDate": "2025-01-10T05:00:00Z", "Status": "COMPLETED" } ] }
Django Context: # Available recovery points: # - RDS backup (1GB): Django database # - EBS backup: Application volume # Latest JobPost.objects data: Jan 11

Amazon S3 Glacier

Long-term archival storage for compliance and backup

📋 S3 Glacier Overview

Amazon S3 Glacier is a secure, durable, and extremely low-cost cloud storage service for data archiving and long-term backup. Use it to archive old JobPost data, compliance records, and historical Django application logs at minimal cost.

Core S3 Glacier Operations

create-vault - Create archive vault
list-vaults - List all vaults
upload-archive - Upload data
initiate-job - Start retrieval
get-job-output - Download data
delete-archive - Remove archive

create-vault

Creates a new vault for storing archives.

Syntax
aws glacier create-vault \
    --account-id - \
    --vault-name <value>
Parameters
  • --account-id (string): AWS account ID (use - for current)
  • --vault-name (string): Name for the vault
Example 1: Create archive vault

Create vault for jobpost historical data.

#!/bin/bash
aws glacier create-vault \
    --account-id - \
    --vault-name "anihpj-jobpost-archive"
Output: { "location": "/123456789012/vaults/anihpj-jobpost-archive" }
Django Context: # Glacier vault for archiving: # - Old JobPost.objects (published > 1 year ago) # - Historical Skills.objects data # - Compliance records for Author.objects
Example 2: Create compliance vault

Vault for regulatory compliance data.

#!/bin/bash
aws glacier create-vault \
    --account-id - \
    --vault-name "anihpj-compliance-archive"
Output: { "location": "/123456789012/vaults/anihpj-compliance-archive" }
Django Context: # Compliance vault storing: # - User.objects audit logs # - JobPost change history # - Author data retention records

upload-archive

Uploads an archive to a vault.

Syntax
aws glacier upload-archive \
    --account-id - \
    --vault-name <value> \
    --body <file-path> \
    [--archive-description <value>]
Example 1: Archive old job posts

Upload exported JobPost data to Glacier.

#!/bin/bash
# Export old job posts to JSON
python manage.py dumpdata jobpost.JobPost --indent 2 > jobposts-archive-2024.json

# Compress the export
gzip jobposts-archive-2024.json

# Upload to Glacier
aws glacier upload-archive \
    --account-id - \
    --vault-name "anihpj-jobpost-archive" \
    --body jobposts-archive-2024.json.gz \
    --archive-description "JobPost data export - 2024 annual archive"
Output: { "location": "/123456789012/vaults/anihpj-jobpost-archive/archives/archive-id-abc123", "checksum": "sha256-hash-value", "archiveId": "archive-id-abc123" }
Django Context: # Archived: JobPost.objects.filter(created_at__year=2024) # Includes: title, author, location, skills, status # Size: Compressed JSON export # Cost: ~$0.004/GB/month
Example 2: Archive database backup

Upload PostgreSQL dump to Glacier.

#!/bin/bash
# Create database dump
pg_dump -h localhost -U postgres jobpost_db > db-backup-20250111.sql

# Compress
gzip db-backup-20250111.sql

# Upload to Glacier
aws glacier upload-archive \
    --account-id - \
    --vault-name "anihpj-jobpost-archive" \
    --body db-backup-20250111.sql.gz \
    --archive-description "Full PostgreSQL backup - jobpost_db - 2025-01-11"
Output: { "archiveId": "archive-db-xyz789", "checksum": "sha256-checksum-value" }
Django Context: # Full database backup containing: # - jobpost_jobpost (JobPost model) # - jobpost_skills (Skills model) # - jobpost_author (Author model) # - jobpost_location (Location model) # - auth_user (User model)
Example 3: Archive audit logs

Store compliance audit logs.

#!/bin/bash
# Archive monthly audit logs
aws glacier upload-archive \
    --account-id - \
    --vault-name "anihpj-compliance-archive" \
    --body /var/log/django/audit-2024-12.log.gz \
    --archive-description "Django audit logs - December 2024"
Output: { "archiveId": "archive-audit-dec2024" }
Django Context: # Audit logs containing: # - User.objects login/logout events # - JobPost.objects create/update/delete # - Author.objects modifications # - Admin actions on all models

initiate-job

Initiates a retrieval job to download archived data.

Syntax
aws glacier initiate-job \
    --account-id - \
    --vault-name <value> \
    --job-parameters <value>
Example 1: Standard retrieval (3-5 hours)

Initiate standard retrieval of archived data.

#!/bin/bash
aws glacier initiate-job \
    --account-id - \
    --vault-name "anihpj-jobpost-archive" \
    --job-parameters '{
        "Type": "archive-retrieval",
        "ArchiveId": "archive-id-abc123",
        "Tier": "Standard",
        "Description": "Retrieve 2024 JobPost archive"
    }'
Output: { "location": "/123456789012/vaults/anihpj-jobpost-archive/jobs/job-id-12345", "jobId": "job-id-12345" }
Django Context: # Retrieving: JobPost.objects 2024 archive # Wait time: 3-5 hours (Standard tier) # Cost: $0.01/GB retrieval # Use for: Historical analysis
Example 2: Expedited retrieval (1-5 minutes)

Urgent retrieval of critical data.

#!/bin/bash
aws glacier initiate-job \
    --account-id - \
    --vault-name "anihpj-jobpost-archive" \
    --job-parameters '{
        "Type": "archive-retrieval",
        "ArchiveId": "archive-db-xyz789",
        "Tier": "Expedited",
        "Description": "URGENT: Retrieve database backup for recovery"
    }'
Output: { "jobId": "expedited-job-urgent-001" }
Django Context: # URGENT retrieval: Full database backup # Wait time: 1-5 minutes (Expedited tier) # Cost: $0.03/GB retrieval # Use for: Disaster recovery
Example 3: Bulk retrieval (5-12 hours)

Cost-effective retrieval of large archives.

#!/bin/bash
aws glacier initiate-job \
    --account-id - \
    --vault-name "anihpj-compliance-archive" \
    --job-parameters '{
        "Type": "archive-retrieval",
        "ArchiveId": "archive-audit-dec2024",
        "Tier": "Bulk",
        "Description": "Audit log retrieval for compliance review"
    }'
Output: { "jobId": "bulk-job-audit-001" }
Django Context: # Bulk retrieval: Audit logs # Wait time: 5-12 hours (Bulk tier) # Cost: $0.0025/GB (cheapest) # Use for: Non-urgent compliance review

get-job-output

Downloads the output of a completed retrieval job.

Syntax
aws glacier get-job-output \
    --account-id - \
    --vault-name <value> \
    --job-id <value> \
    <output-file>
Example 1: Download retrieved archive

Download completed job output.

#!/bin/bash
aws glacier get-job-output \
    --account-id - \
    --vault-name "anihpj-jobpost-archive" \
    --job-id "job-id-12345" \
    retrieved-jobposts-2024.json.gz

# Decompress
gunzip retrieved-jobposts-2024.json.gz

# Import back to Django
python manage.py loaddata retrieved-jobposts-2024.json
Output: { "status": 200, "contentType": "application/octet-stream", "archiveDescription": "JobPost data export - 2024 annual archive" }
Django Context: # Downloaded and restored: # - Historical JobPost.objects from 2024 # - Associated Skills and Locations # - Ready for analysis or migration

list-vaults

Lists all vaults in the account.

Syntax
aws glacier list-vaults \
    --account-id -
Example 1: List all archive vaults

View all Glacier vaults.

#!/bin/bash
aws glacier list-vaults --account-id -
Output: { "VaultList": [ { "VaultName": "anihpj-jobpost-archive", "CreationDate": "2025-01-01T00:00:00Z", "NumberOfArchives": 12, "SizeInBytes": 5368709120 }, { "VaultName": "anihpj-compliance-archive", "CreationDate": "2025-01-01T00:00:00Z", "NumberOfArchives": 24, "SizeInBytes": 1073741824 } ] }
Django Context: # Glacier vaults summary: # - jobpost-archive: 12 archives, 5GB # Contains: JobPost, Skills, Author data # - compliance-archive: 24 archives, 1GB # Contains: Audit logs, compliance records

✅ Testing with pytest

pytest integration for testing Django applications and AWS CLI commands. This section covers unit tests, integration tests, and fixtures for testing the jobpost application models with AWS services.

pytest for Django and AWS

Testing Django applications with AWS service integrations

📋 pytest Testing Overview

pytest is a powerful testing framework for Python that makes it easy to write and run tests for your Django jobpost application. Combined with moto for AWS mocking and pytest-django, you can test AWS CLI integrations, model operations, and API endpoints.

Testing Patterns

pytest-django - Django integration
moto - AWS service mocking
fixtures - Test data setup
markers - Test categorization
conftest.py - Shared fixtures
coverage - Code coverage

conftest.py Setup

Configure shared fixtures for jobpost model testing.

Syntax
# conftest.py
import pytest
from django.contrib.auth.models import User
from jobpost.models import Skills, Author, Location, JobPost

@pytest.fixture
def user(db):
    return User.objects.create_user(username='testuser', password='testpass')

@pytest.fixture
def skills(db):
    return [
        Skills.objects.create(name='Python', level='Advanced'),
        Skills.objects.create(name='Django', level='Advanced'),
        Skills.objects.create(name='AWS', level='Intermediate')
    ]

@pytest.fixture
def location(db):
    return Location.objects.create(
        city='San Francisco', state='CA', country='USA', zip_code='94105'
    )

@pytest.fixture
def author(db, user, location):
    return Author.objects.create(user=user, bio='Test author', location=location)

@pytest.fixture
def jobpost(db, author, location, skills):
    job = JobPost.objects.create(
        title='Senior Python Developer',
        author=author,
        location=location,
        status='published'
    )
    job.required_skills.set(skills[:2])
    return job
Example 1: Test JobPost creation

Test creating job posts with all relationships.

# tests/test_models.py
import pytest
from jobpost.models import JobPost, Skills, Author, Location

@pytest.mark.django_db
class TestJobPostModel:
    def test_create_jobpost(self, author, location, skills):
        """Test JobPost.objects.create() with relationships"""
        job = JobPost.objects.create(
            title='DevOps Engineer',
            author=author,
            location=location,
            description='Looking for experienced DevOps engineer',
            status='draft'
        )
        job.required_skills.add(skills[2])  # AWS skill
        
        assert job.pk is not None
        assert job.title == 'DevOps Engineer'
        assert job.author == author
        assert job.location == location
        assert job.required_skills.count() == 1
        assert skills[2] in job.required_skills.all()
    
    def test_jobpost_str_representation(self, jobpost):
        """Test JobPost.__str__() method"""
        assert str(jobpost) == 'Senior Python Developer'
    
    def test_jobpost_published_filter(self, db, author, location):
        """Test filtering published job posts"""
        # Create multiple jobs with different statuses
        JobPost.objects.create(title='Published Job', author=author, 
                              location=location, status='published')
        JobPost.objects.create(title='Draft Job', author=author, 
                              location=location, status='draft')
        
        published = JobPost.objects.filter(status='published')
        assert published.count() >= 1
        assert all(j.status == 'published' for j in published)
Output: $ pytest tests/test_models.py -v ======================== test session starts ======================== tests/test_models.py::TestJobPostModel::test_create_jobpost PASSED tests/test_models.py::TestJobPostModel::test_jobpost_str_representation PASSED tests/test_models.py::TestJobPostModel::test_jobpost_published_filter PASSED ========================= 3 passed in 0.45s =========================
Django Context: # Tests verify: # - JobPost.objects.create() works correctly # - Author and Location relationships intact # - Skills many-to-many relationship works # - Status filtering works as expected
Example 2: Test Skills model

Test Skills model operations.

# tests/test_skills.py
import pytest
from jobpost.models import Skills

@pytest.mark.django_db
class TestSkillsModel:
    def test_create_skill(self, db):
        """Test Skills.objects.create()"""
        skill = Skills.objects.create(name='Kubernetes', level='Intermediate')
        
        assert skill.pk is not None
        assert skill.name == 'Kubernetes'
        assert skill.level == 'Intermediate'
    
    def test_unique_skill_name(self, db):
        """Test skill name uniqueness (if applicable)"""
        Skills.objects.create(name='Docker', level='Advanced')
        # Depending on model constraints, this might raise IntegrityError
        skill2 = Skills.objects.create(name='Docker', level='Beginner')
        assert Skills.objects.filter(name='Docker').count() >= 1
    
    def test_skills_ordering(self, skills):
        """Test default ordering of skills"""
        all_skills = Skills.objects.all()
        assert all_skills.count() == 3
        # Verify skills exist
        skill_names = list(all_skills.values_list('name', flat=True))
        assert 'Python' in skill_names
        assert 'Django' in skill_names
        assert 'AWS' in skill_names
Output: $ pytest tests/test_skills.py -v ======================== test session starts ======================== tests/test_skills.py::TestSkillsModel::test_create_skill PASSED tests/test_skills.py::TestSkillsModel::test_unique_skill_name PASSED tests/test_skills.py::TestSkillsModel::test_skills_ordering PASSED ========================= 3 passed in 0.32s =========================
Django Context: # Tests verify: # - Skills.objects.create() creates properly # - Model constraints work correctly # - QuerySet operations return expected results
Example 3: Mock AWS S3 with moto

Test S3 integration for static file uploads.

# tests/test_aws_integration.py
import pytest
import boto3
from moto import mock_aws

@pytest.fixture
def aws_credentials():
    """Mock AWS Credentials for moto"""
    import os
    os.environ['AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID'] = 'testing'
    os.environ['AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY'] = 'testing'
    os.environ['AWS_SECURITY_TOKEN'] = 'testing'
    os.environ['AWS_SESSION_TOKEN'] = 'testing'
    os.environ['AWS_DEFAULT_REGION'] = 'us-east-1'

@pytest.fixture
def s3_client(aws_credentials):
    with mock_aws():
        yield boto3.client('s3', region_name='us-east-1')

@mock_aws
class TestS3Integration:
    def test_create_bucket_for_static_files(self, aws_credentials):
        """Test creating S3 bucket for Django static files"""
        s3 = boto3.client('s3', region_name='us-east-1')
        
        # Create bucket for static files
        s3.create_bucket(Bucket='anihpj-static-files')
        
        # Verify bucket exists
        response = s3.list_buckets()
        bucket_names = [b['Name'] for b in response['Buckets']]
        assert 'anihpj-static-files' in bucket_names
    
    def test_upload_jobpost_media(self, aws_credentials):
        """Test uploading media files for JobPost"""
        s3 = boto3.client('s3', region_name='us-east-1')
        s3.create_bucket(Bucket='anihpj-media')
        
        # Upload test file
        s3.put_object(
            Bucket='anihpj-media',
            Key='jobposts/12345/image.jpg',
            Body=b'fake image content'
        )
        
        # Verify upload
        response = s3.get_object(
            Bucket='anihpj-media',
            Key='jobposts/12345/image.jpg'
        )
        assert response['Body'].read() == b'fake image content'
Output: $ pytest tests/test_aws_integration.py -v ======================== test session starts ======================== tests/test_aws_integration.py::TestS3Integration::test_create_bucket_for_static_files PASSED tests/test_aws_integration.py::TestS3Integration::test_upload_jobpost_media PASSED ========================= 2 passed in 0.89s =========================
Django Context: # Tests verify: # - S3 bucket creation for Django static files # - Media file upload for JobPost images # - AWS integration works correctly (mocked)
Example 4: Mock SQS for async processing

Test SQS integration for job notifications.

# tests/test_sqs_integration.py
import pytest
import boto3
import json
from moto import mock_aws

@mock_aws
class TestSQSIntegration:
    def test_send_jobpost_notification(self):
        """Test sending JobPost creation notification to SQS"""
        sqs = boto3.client('sqs', region_name='us-east-1')
        
        # Create queue
        response = sqs.create_queue(QueueName='anihpj-jobpost-notifications')
        queue_url = response['QueueUrl']
        
        # Send notification
        message = {
            'event': 'jobpost_created',
            'jobpost_id': 12345,
            'author_id': 67,
            'title': 'Senior Python Developer'
        }
        sqs.send_message(
            QueueUrl=queue_url,
            MessageBody=json.dumps(message)
        )
        
        # Receive and verify
        response = sqs.receive_message(QueueUrl=queue_url)
        received = json.loads(response['Messages'][0]['Body'])
        
        assert received['event'] == 'jobpost_created'
        assert received['jobpost_id'] == 12345
    
    def test_batch_send_skill_updates(self):
        """Test batch sending skill update notifications"""
        sqs = boto3.client('sqs', region_name='us-east-1')
        response = sqs.create_queue(QueueName='anihpj-skill-updates')
        queue_url = response['QueueUrl']
        
        # Batch send skill updates
        entries = [
            {'Id': '1', 'MessageBody': json.dumps({'skill': 'Python', 'action': 'add'})},
            {'Id': '2', 'MessageBody': json.dumps({'skill': 'Django', 'action': 'add'})},
            {'Id': '3', 'MessageBody': json.dumps({'skill': 'AWS', 'action': 'update'})}
        ]
        sqs.send_message_batch(QueueUrl=queue_url, Entries=entries)
        
        # Verify messages
        messages = []
        for _ in range(3):
            resp = sqs.receive_message(QueueUrl=queue_url, MaxNumberOfMessages=3)
            if 'Messages' in resp:
                messages.extend(resp['Messages'])
        
        assert len(messages) >= 3
Output: $ pytest tests/test_sqs_integration.py -v ======================== test session starts ======================== tests/test_sqs_integration.py::TestSQSIntegration::test_send_jobpost_notification PASSED tests/test_sqs_integration.py::TestSQSIntegration::test_batch_send_skill_updates PASSED ========================= 2 passed in 0.67s =========================
Django Context: # Tests verify: # - SQS queue creation works # - JobPost notifications sent correctly # - Skills.objects updates trigger messages # - Batch processing works
Example 5: Integration test with DynamoDB

Test DynamoDB for session storage.

# tests/test_dynamodb_integration.py
import pytest
import boto3
from moto import mock_aws

@mock_aws
class TestDynamoDBIntegration:
    def test_create_session_table(self):
        """Test creating DynamoDB table for Django sessions"""
        dynamodb = boto3.client('dynamodb', region_name='us-east-1')
        
        # Create table
        dynamodb.create_table(
            TableName='anihpj-sessions',
            KeySchema=[
                {'AttributeName': 'session_key', 'KeyType': 'HASH'}
            ],
            AttributeDefinitions=[
                {'AttributeName': 'session_key', 'AttributeType': 'S'}
            ],
            BillingMode='PAY_PER_REQUEST'
        )
        
        # Verify table exists
        response = dynamodb.list_tables()
        assert 'anihpj-sessions' in response['TableNames']
    
    def test_store_user_session(self):
        """Test storing user session data"""
        dynamodb = boto3.resource('dynamodb', region_name='us-east-1')
        client = boto3.client('dynamodb', region_name='us-east-1')
        
        # Create table
        client.create_table(
            TableName='anihpj-sessions',
            KeySchema=[
                {'AttributeName': 'session_key', 'KeyType': 'HASH'}
            ],
            AttributeDefinitions=[
                {'AttributeName': 'session_key', 'AttributeType': 'S'}
            ],
            BillingMode='PAY_PER_REQUEST'
        )
        
        table = dynamodb.Table('anihpj-sessions')
        
        # Store session
        table.put_item(Item={
            'session_key': 'abc123xyz',
            'user_id': 42,
            'author_id': 67,
            'last_viewed_jobs': [12345, 12346, 12347]
        })
        
        # Retrieve session
        response = table.get_item(Key={'session_key': 'abc123xyz'})
        item = response['Item']
        
        assert item['user_id'] == 42
        assert item['author_id'] == 67
        assert len(item['last_viewed_jobs']) == 3
Output: $ pytest tests/test_dynamodb_integration.py -v ======================== test session starts ======================== tests/test_dynamodb_integration.py::TestDynamoDBIntegration::test_create_session_table PASSED tests/test_dynamodb_integration.py::TestDynamoDBIntegration::test_store_user_session PASSED ========================= 2 passed in 0.54s =========================
Django Context: # Tests verify: # - DynamoDB table creation for sessions # - User session storage works # - Author data linked in sessions # - JobPost view history tracked

Running Tests

Commands to run pytest with different options.

Common pytest Commands
# Run all tests
pytest

# Run with verbose output
pytest -v

# Run specific test file
pytest tests/test_models.py

# Run specific test class
pytest tests/test_models.py::TestJobPostModel

# Run specific test method
pytest tests/test_models.py::TestJobPostModel::test_create_jobpost

# Run with coverage report
pytest --cov=jobpost --cov-report=html

# Run tests matching a pattern
pytest -k "jobpost or skills"

# Run only Django database tests
pytest -m django_db

# Run tests in parallel
pytest -n auto

# Run with AWS mocking
pytest tests/test_aws_integration.py -v
Example: Full test suite execution

Run complete test suite with coverage.

#!/bin/bash
# Run full test suite for anihpj jobpost app
pytest tests/ \
    --cov=jobpost \
    --cov-report=html \
    --cov-report=term-missing \
    -v \
    --tb=short
Output: $ pytest tests/ --cov=jobpost --cov-report=term-missing -v ======================== test session starts ======================== tests/test_models.py::TestJobPostModel::test_create_jobpost PASSED tests/test_models.py::TestJobPostModel::test_jobpost_str_representation PASSED tests/test_models.py::TestJobPostModel::test_jobpost_published_filter PASSED tests/test_skills.py::TestSkillsModel::test_create_skill PASSED tests/test_skills.py::TestSkillsModel::test_unique_skill_name PASSED tests/test_skills.py::TestSkillsModel::test_skills_ordering PASSED tests/test_aws_integration.py::TestS3Integration::test_create_bucket PASSED tests/test_aws_integration.py::TestS3Integration::test_upload_jobpost_media PASSED tests/test_sqs_integration.py::TestSQSIntegration::test_send_jobpost_notification PASSED tests/test_sqs_integration.py::TestSQSIntegration::test_batch_send_skill_updates PASSED tests/test_dynamodb_integration.py::TestDynamoDBIntegration::test_create_session_table PASSED tests/test_dynamodb_integration.py::TestDynamoDBIntegration::test_store_user_session PASSED ---------- coverage: jobpost ---------- Name Stmts Miss Cover Missing ----------------------------------------------------- jobpost/__init__.py 0 0 100% jobpost/models.py 45 3 93% 78-80 jobpost/views.py 62 8 87% 45-52 jobpost/admin.py 12 0 100% ----------------------------------------------------- TOTAL 119 11 91% ======================== 12 passed in 2.34s =========================
Django Context: # Test coverage summary: # - models.py: 93% coverage (JobPost, Skills, Author, Location) # - views.py: 87% coverage (API endpoints) # - admin.py: 100% coverage (admin configuration) # - Overall: 91% coverage # All AWS integrations tested with moto mocking
